equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/number_of_erotions_and_d.description: Parameter 'Number Of Erotions And Dilations' of type with ID 'double'. null

block-radius.description: Parameter 'Block radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Determines the local vicinity that is used to calculate the similarity of two pixels.

max-distance.description: Parameter 'Max distance' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Maximum search distance for a most similar pixel. The maximum value is 127 due to performance reasons.

relative-distances.description: Parameter 'Relative distances' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output radius or x/y are relative to the max distance.

output-polar-coordinates.description: Parameter 'Output polar coordinates' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output contains polar coordinates (channel 0 being the radius and channel 1 being phi). If disabled, the output contains cartesian coordinates.

add-last-identity-field.description: Parameter 'Add identity field at end' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output will contain as many planes as the input. The last slide will map to identity (zero image)

clij-parameters/column_index.description: Parameter 'Column Index' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/value.description: Parameter 'Value' of type with ID 'double'. null

process-environment.description: Parameter 'Process' of type with ID 'process'. The process that should be executed. Click 'Edit' to setup the process.

override-arguments.description: Parameter 'Override arguments' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, override arguments of the environment with the one provided by the expression. Please note that this expression has access to annotations.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

output-output-folder.description: Parameter 'Output output folder' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output folder designated to the current iteration step is also output into a slot 'Output folder'. Please note that any existing slot with this name is replaced by a directory output slot. Data will be not be extracted from the process output.

image-filters.description:
Parameter 'Filter images' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression to filter the image(s). Each image is tested individually and added imported based on the test results. The expression should return a boolean value. Example: 
(title CONTAINS "data") AND (depth > 3). Defaults to 'TRUE'

only-active-image.description: Parameter 'Only extract active image' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the currently active image is extracted. There is always only one image active. If no active image is present, the node will generate no outputs.

image-filters-mode.description: Parameter 'Filter images operation' of type with ID 'logical-operation'. Determines how the 'Filter images' operations are connected.

greyscale-correction.description: Parameter 'Apply greyscale correction' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, convert greyscale images to RGBA to prevent washed-out colors

min-particle-sphericity.description: Parameter 'Min particle sphericity' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The minimum sphericity. The value range is from 0-1. If an object is not within the sphericity range, it is removed from the results.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

min-particle-size.description: Parameter 'Min particle size' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The minimum particle size in the specified unit of the input image. If no unit is available, the unit is 'pixels'. If an object is not within the size range, it is removed from the results.

max-particle-sphericity.description: Parameter 'Max particle sphericity' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The maximum sphericity. The value range is from 0-1. If an object is not within the sphericity range, it is removed from the results.

exclude-edges.description: Parameter 'Exclude edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, objects that are connected to the image edges are removed.

max-particle-size.description: Parameter 'Max particle size' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The maximum particle size in the specified unit of the input image. If no unit is available, the unit is 'pixels'. If an object is not within the size range, it is removed from the results.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

rolling-ball-radius.description: Parameter 'Rolling ball radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'.

dilation-erode-steps.description: Parameter 'Dilation erode steps' of type with ID 'primitive.int'.

gaussian-sigma.description: Parameter 'Gaussian sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.double'.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:algorithm:enabl.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If disabled, this algorithm will be skipped in a run. Please note that this will also disable all algorithms dependent on this algorithm.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:ui-locked.description: Parameter 'Lock location/size' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, lock the location and size of this node. Does not affect automated alignment operations. Will lock the size if the node supports a resize handle. Will prevent deletion of the node by the user. Slots can still be edited/connected and parameters can still be changed.

auto-thresholding/dark-background.description: Parameter 'Dark background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If the background color is dark. Disable this if your image has a bright background.

auto-thresholding/slice-threshold-mode.description:
Parameter 'Multi-slice thresholding' of type with ID 'slice-threshold-mode'. Determines how thresholds are calculated if an image has multiple slices.
  • Apply threshold per slice calculates and applies the threshold for each slice.
  • Combine slice statistics merges the slice histograms into one, which is then used for threshold calculation.
  • Combine thresholds per slice calculates the threshold for each slice. One threshold for all slices is calculated via the math expression.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:bookmarked.description: Parameter 'Bookmark this node' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node is highlighted in the graph editor UI and added into the bookmark list.

auto-thresholding/threshold-annotation-s.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:algorithm:pass-.description:
Parameter 'Pass through' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will pass the input data directly to the output data without any processing. This is different from enabling/disabling the algorithm as this will not disable dependent algorithms.
Please note that setting this parameter via adaptive parameters (if available) does not always yield the expected result. The reason behind this is that pass-through is not trivial for certain nodes. We recommend to use a split node if node execution should be made adaptive.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Custom algorithm name.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:algorithm:runti.description: Parameter 'Partition' of type with ID 'runtime-partition-reference'. Allows to move the node into a different runtime partition, which determine how the workload is executed.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:descriptio.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. A custom description

auto-thresholding/threshold-combine-expr.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

auto-thresholding/source-area.description: Parameter 'Calculate threshold based on ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines from which image areas the pixel values used for calculating the thresholds are extracted from.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:local-paralleli.description: Parameter 'Local parallelization' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the partition- and run-wide parallelization settings and apply parallelization only on this node.

auto-thresholding/threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:data-batch-gene.description: Parameter 'Merge same annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if different values for the same annotation columns are encountered.

Parameter 'Grouping method' of type with ID 'column-matching'. Algorithms with multiple inputs require to match the incoming data to data sets. This allows you to determine how interesting data annotation columns are extracted from the incoming data. Union matches using the union of annotation columns. Intersection intersects the sets of available columns. You can also customize which columns should be included or excluded.

Parameter 'Merge same data annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:data-annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if different values for the same data annotation columns are encountered.

Parameter 'Custom grouping columns' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Only used if 'Grouping method' is set to 'Custom'. Determines which annotation columns are referred to group data sets. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"
Parameter 'Skip incomplete data sets' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, incomplete data sets are silently skipped. Otherwise an error is displayed if such a configuration is detected.
Parameter 'Custom annotation matching method' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression used to compare two annotation sets.
Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. Limits which iteration steps are generated. The first index is zero.
Parameter 'Force flow graph solver' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, disable the faster dictionary-based solver. Use this if you experience unexpected behavior.

Parameter 'Annotation matching method' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-matching-method'. Allows to customize when two annotation sets are considered as equal. By default, non-empty annotation values should match exactly. You can also use a custom expression, instead.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:parameter-slot-.description: Parameter 'Multiple parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be an additional slot that consumes parameter data sets. The algorithm then will be applied for each of this parameter sets.
Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:adaptive-parame.description: Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Enable adaptive parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, you can use custom expressions to generate parameters. Please note that this will disable parallelization.

Parameter 'Overridden parameters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression:string:pair-list'. Here you can override the value of parameters based on annotations. The 'Value' settings should return a value for the parameter type or a JSON string that can be converted into a valid parameter value. The 'Parameter key' setting determines to which parameter this value is written to.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

classifier-settings/classifier.description: Parameter 'Classifier' of type with ID 'weka-classifier'. The selected classifier

classifier-settings/balance-classes.description: Parameter 'Balance classes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. The classifier uses by the default all the user traces to train. By selecting this option, we filter first the classes in order to provide a balanced distribution of the samples. This implies that the less numerous classes will duplicate some of their samples and the more populated classes will lose some of their samples for the sake of even distribution. This option is strongly recommended if we want to give the same importance to all classes. An alternative is to use the Weka CostSensitiveClassifier and set a corresponding cost matrix.

feature-settings/use-neighbors.description: Parameter 'Use neighbors' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Set the use of the neighbor features

feature-settings/membrane-patch-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane patch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. this represents the size 𝑛×𝑛 of the field of view for the membrane projection filters. Only available for 2D features.

feature-settings/membrane-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane thickness' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Expected value of the membrane thickness, 1 pixel by default. The more accurate, the more precise the filter will be.

feature-settings/min-sigma.description: Parameter 'Minimum sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Minimum radius of the isotropic filters used to create the features. By default 1 pixel.

feature-settings/training-features.description:
Parameter 'Training features' of type with ID 'weka-feature-set-2d'. Here we can select and deselect the training features, which are the key of the learning procedure. The plugin creates a stack of images —one image for each feature. For instance, if only Gaussian blur is selected as a feature, the classifier will be trained on the original image and some blurred versions to it with different 𝜎 parameters for the Gaussian. If the input image is grayscale, the features will be calculated using double precision (32-bit images). In the case of RGB input images, the features will be RGB as well.
  • Gaussian blur: performs 𝑛 individual convolutions with Gaussian kernels with the normal 𝑛 variations of 𝜎. The larger the radius the more blurred the image becomes until the pixels are homogeneous.
  • Sobel filter: calculates an approximation of the gradient of the image intensity at each pixel. Gaussian blurs with 𝜎 varying as usual are performed prior to the filter.
  • Hessian: Calculates a Hessian matrix 𝐻 at each pixel. Prior to the application of any filters, a Gaussian blur with varying 𝜎 is performed.
  • Difference of gaussians: calculates two Gaussian blur images from the original image and subtracts one from the other. 𝜎 values are varied as usual, so 𝑛(𝑛−1)2 feature images are added to the stack.
  • Membrane projections: enhances membrane-like structures of the image through directional filtering. The initial kernel for this operation is hardcoded as a 19×19 zero matrix with the middle column entries set to 1. Multiple kernels are created by rotating the original kernel by 6 degrees up to a total rotation of 180 degrees, giving 30 kernels. Each kernel is convolved with the image and then the set of 30 images are Z-projected into a single image via 6 methods (sum of the pixels in each image, mean of the pixels in each image, standard deviation of the pixels in each image, median of the pixels in each image, maximum of the pixels in each image, minimum of the pixels in each image). Each of the 6 resulting images is a feature. Hence pixels in lines of similarly valued pixels in the image that are different from the average image intensity will stand out in the Z-projections.
  • Mean, Variance, Median, Minimum, Maximum: the pixels within a radius of 𝜎 pixels from the target pixel are subjected to the pertinent operation (mean/min etc.) and the target pixel is set to that value.
  • Anisotropic diffusion: the anisotropic diffusion filtering from Fiji with 20 iterations, 𝜎 smoothing per iterations, 𝑎1=0.10,0.35, 𝑎2=0.9, and an edge threshold set to the membrane size.
  • Bilateral filter: is very similar to the Mean filter but better preserves edges while averaging/blurring other parts of the image. The filter accomplishes this task by only averaging the values around the current pixel that are close in color value to the current pixel. The ‘closeness’ of other neighborhood pixels to the current pixels is determined by the specified threshold. I.e. for a value of 10 each pixel that contributes to the current mean have to be within 10 values of the current pixel. In our case, we combine spatial radius of 5 and 10, with a range radius of 50 and 100.
  • Lipschitz filter: from Mikulas Stencel plugin. This plugin implements Lipschitz cover of an image that is equivalent to a grayscale opening by a cone. The Lipschitz cover can be applied for the elimination of a slowly varying image background by subtraction of the lower Lipschitz cover (a top-hat procedure). A sequential double scan algorithm is used. We use down and top hats combination, with slope 𝑠=5,10,15,20,25.
  • Kuwahara filter: another noise-reduction filter that preserves edges. This is a version of the Kuwahara filter that uses linear kernels rather than square ones. We use the membrane patch size as kernel size, 30 angles and the three different criteria (Variance, Variance / Mean and Variance / Mean^2).
  • abor filter: at the moment this option may take some time and memory because it generates a very diverse range of Gabor filters (22). ’ This may undergo changes in the future’. The implementation details are included in this script. The Gabor filter is an edge detection and texture filter, which convolves several kernels at different angles with an image. Gabor filters are band-pass filters and therefore implement a frequency transformation.
  • Derivatives filter: calculates high order (4, 6, 8, 10) derivatives of the input image.
  • Laplacian filter: computes the Laplacian of the input image using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎.
  • Structure filter: calculates for all elements in the input image, the eigenvalues (smallest and largest) of the so-called structure tensor using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎 and integration scales 1 and 3.
  • Entropy: draws a circle of radius 𝑟 around each pixel; gets the histogram of that circle split in numBins chunks; then calculates the entropy as ∑𝑝 in histogram−𝑝∗log2(𝑝), where 𝑝 is the probability of each chunk in the histogram. numBins is equal to 32,64,128,256. 𝑟 is equal to 𝜎.
  • Neighbors: shifts the image in 8 directions by an certain number of pixel, 𝜎. Therefore creates 8𝑛 feature images.
More information: https://imagej.net/plugins/tws/

feature-settings/max-sigma.description: Parameter 'Maximum sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Maximum radius of the isotropic filters used to create the features. By default 16 pixels.

filter.description: Parameter 'Keep screen if' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to filter the returned projects

override-credentials.description: Parameter 'Override OMERO credentials' of type with ID 'optional-omero-credentials'. Allows to override the OMERO credentials provided in the JIPipe application settings

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/n.description: Parameter 'N' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/modelfilename.description: Parameter 'Modelfilename' of type with ID 'string'. null

clij-parameters/radius_of_maximum.description: Parameter 'Radius Of Maximum' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/radius_of_minimum.description: Parameter 'Radius Of Minimum' of type with ID 'double'. null

overlap-x.description: Parameter 'Overlap (X)' of type with ID 'expression'. Sets the overlap of the tiles. Please note that the size of the tiles will increase.

overlap-y.description: Parameter 'Overlap (Y)' of type with ID 'expression'. Sets the overlap of the tiles. Please note that the size of the tiles will increase.

tile-x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with tile X' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate each tile with its X location (in tile coordinates)

tile-original-width-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original width' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate the tile with the width of the original image

tile-real-y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original Y location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate the tile with its location within the original image. Only works if the scaling anchor is top left

num-tile-x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of X tiles' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate each tile with the number of tiles in the image

tile-original-height-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original height' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate the tile with the height of the original image

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge existing annotations' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how existing annotations are merged

tile-y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with tile Y' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate each tile with its Y location (in tile coordinates)

tile-inset-y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with tile inset (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, each tile is annotated with its inset, meaning the overlap in the Y direction. This value can be utilized to remove the overlap at a later point.

tile-inset-x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with tile inset (X)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, each tile is annotated with its inset, meaning the overlap in the X direction. This value can be utilized to remove the overlap at a later point.

tile-x.description: Parameter 'Tile width' of type with ID 'expression'. The width of a tile

tile-y.description: Parameter 'Tile height' of type with ID 'expression'. The height of a tile

num-tile-y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of Y tiles' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate each tile with the number of tiles in the image

border-mode.description:
Parameter 'Border mode' of type with ID 'ij1-border-mode'. Determines how the image is expanded with borders. Only applicable if an overlap is set.
  • Constant: Set the border to a constant value (zero/black unless configurable)
  • Repeat: Repeat the nearest non-border pixel
  • Mirror: Mirror the pixel values across the border
  • Tile: Tile the image

tile-real-x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original X location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate the tile with its location within the original image. Only works if the scaling anchor is top left

add-name.description: Parameter 'Add ROI name as column' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a column 'Name' containing the ROI name is created. You can modify this name via the 'Change ROI properties' node.

apply-per-frame.description: Parameter 'Apply per frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each frame separately. If false, all frames are put together.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

index-annotation.description: Parameter 'Generated annotation' of type with ID 'optional-string'. Optional. The annotation will contain the image slice position that was used to generate the statistics.

apply-per-channel.description: Parameter 'Apply per channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each channel-slice separately. If false, all channel-slices are put together. Please note that 'Channel' does not refer to a pixel channel like Red in RGB.

apply-per-slice.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each Z-slice separately. If false, all Z-slices are put together.

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Extracted measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. Please select which measurements should be extracted. Each measurement will be assigned to one or multiple output table columns.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

line-width.description: Parameter 'Line width' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The line diameter in pixels. It estimates the 'Sigma' parameter.

lower-threshold.description: Parameter 'Lower threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Line points with a response smaller as this threshold are rejected

estimate-high-threshold.description: Parameter 'Estimate high threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the lower and higher threshold will be estimated by the contrast parameters. The formula is FLOOR(ABS(-2 * CONTRAST_HIGH * (LINE_WIDTH / 2.0) / (SQRT(2 * PI) * POW(SIGMA, 3)) * EXP(-((LINE_WIDTH / 2.0) * (LINE_WIDTH / 2.0)) / (2 * SIGMA * SIGMA))))

estimate-sigma.description: Parameter 'Estimate sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, 'Sigma' will be estimated by the 'Line width' parameter. The formula is SIGMA = LINE_WIDTH / (2 * SQRT(3)) + 0.5

upper-threshold.description: Parameter 'Upper threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Line points with a response larger as this threshold are accepted

min-length.description: Parameter 'Min line length' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Minimum length of a line

overlap-resolution.description: Parameter 'Resolve overlaps' of type with ID 'ij1-segment-ridge-detector-2d:overlap-resolver'. Method for resolving overlaps

high-contrast.description: Parameter 'High contrast' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Highest grayscale value of the line. It estimates the mandatory parameter 'Upper threshold'

low-contrast.description: Parameter 'Low contrast' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Lowest grayscale value of the line. It estimates the mandatory parameter 'Lower threshold'

sigma.description: Parameter 'Sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Determines the sigma for the derivatives. It depends on the line width.

estimate-width.description: Parameter 'Estimate width' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If this option is selected the width of the line is estimated.

max-length.description: Parameter 'Max line length' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Maximum length of a line

extend-line.description: Parameter 'Extend lines' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Extends the detect lines to find more junction points

estimate-low-threshold.description: Parameter 'Estimate low threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the lower threshold will be estimated by the contrast parameters. The formula is FLOOR(ABS(-2 * CONTRAST_LOW * (LINE_WIDTH / 2.0) / (SQRT(2 * PI) * POW(SIGMA, 3)) * EXP(-((LINE_WIDTH / 2.0) * (LINE_WIDTH / 2.0)) / (2 * SIGMA * SIGMA))))

dark-line.description: Parameter 'Dark lines' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. This parameter determines whether dark or bright lines are extracted

correct-position.description: Parameter 'Correct position' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Correct the line position if it has different contrast on each side of it.

prune-cycles-method.description:
Parameter 'Prune cycles method' of type with ID 'ij1-analyze-skeleton-2d3d:remove-cycles-method'. Allows the selection of a method to prune possible loops in the skeleton
  • None: no cycle detection nor pruning is performed.
  • Shortest branch: the shortest branch among the loop branches will be cut in its middle point.
  • Lowest intensity voxel: the darkest voxel among the loop voxels will be cut (set to 0) in the input image.
  • Lowest intensity branch: the darkest (in average) branch among the loop branches will be cut in its darkest voxel.

prune-ends-method.description:
Parameter 'Prune ends methods' of type with ID 'ij1-analyze-skeleton-2d3d:remove-ends-method'. Allows the selection of a method to prune any branch that ends in an end-point
  • None: branches that end in an end point are not removed.
  • All:branches that end in an end point are removed.
  • Exclude ROI: branches that end in an end point are removed; regions within the ROI are excluded

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clijx.clij.name: Cli Jx Equalize Mean Intensities Of Slices

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

selected-indices.description:
Parameter 'Selected indices' of type with ID 'integer-range'. The output will only contain the ROI indices specified in by the value. For example, the value '0' returns the first ROI. A value of '0-4' returns the top 5 items.

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

auto-clamp.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing items' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be not error if you select too many items (e.g. if the list only contains 10 items, but you select the top 20)

override-axes.description: Parameter 'Override axes' of type with ID 'optional-string'. Allows to override the default axes for writing the values. By default, JIPipe attempts to guess the axes based on the default axis configuration XYCZT. Allowed values are X, Y, Z, C, and T (case-insensitive)

hdf5-path.description: Parameter 'HDF5 internal path' of type with ID 'expression'. Path to the HDF5 data set to export

file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

spot-segmentation-settings/segmentation-.description:
Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'ij3d-segmentation-spot3d:spot-segmentation-method'. When the threshold value has been determined, the plugin offers several
methods of voxel clustering that segment the object. The segmentation
proceeds by successive examination of voxels, starting from the seed.

* Classical : All neighbouring voxels with intensity value higher than the
threshold value (Local Background) are clustered to form the object.
* Maximum : This mode operates similarly as the previous one, but with one
additional constraint : the voxels are clustered in the object if their
intensity value is higher than the threshold and lower than the intensity
value of the previously clustered voxels. It can prevent to cluster pixels
from another close spot.
* Block : The same Maximum procedure is applied, but instead of
considering single voxels, block of voxel (all neighbors with value greater
than the local threshold) are examined. All voxels must satisfy the
constraint to be added to the cluster, else no voxel are added.

seed-segmentation-settings/radius.description: Parameter 'Seed radius' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. The radius for the automatically detected seeds

project-overview-color.description: Parameter 'Project overview color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to mark outputs of this compartment with a color in the project overview.

show-in-project-overview.description: Parameter 'Show in project overview' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, show this output in the project overview. Please note that this setting has no effect if 'Show in project overview' on the compartment itself.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/scalars.description: Parameter 'Scalars' of type with ID 'double'. null

radians.description: Parameter 'Output angles in radians' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, angles are output in radians

outputs/output-energy.description: Parameter 'Output energy' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

outputs/output-gradient-x.description: Parameter 'Output Gradient X' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

outputs/output-orientation.description: Parameter 'Output orientation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

outputs/output-coherency.description: Parameter 'Output coherency' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

outputs/output-gradient-y.description: Parameter 'Output Gradient Y' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

structure-tensor/local-window-sigma.description: Parameter 'Local window σ' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The structure tensor computes the orientation and isotropy properties in a local window. Here, the local window is characterized by a 2D Gaussian function of standard deviation σ. The parameter σ (expressed in pixel unit) is a critical parameter that determines the scale of the analysis. It should have a value roughly close to the structure of interest (e.g. thickness of the filament)

structure-tensor/gradient.description:
Parameter 'Gradient' of type with ID 'ij1-orientationj-gradient'. OrientationJ has different methods to compute the gradient.
  • The cubic spline is always the best choice, fast, accurate, quasi-isotropic and less boundary artefact
  • The finite difference gradient is very fast but it has poor isotropy properties
  • The Fourier gradient is exact but it has periodic boundary conditions.

transformation-type.description:
Parameter 'Transformation' of type with ID 'ij1-turbo-reg:transformation-type'. The type of transformation to be used.
  • Translation. Upon translation, a straight line is mapped to a straight line of identical orientation, with conservation of the distance between any pair of points. A single landmark in each image gives a complete description of a translation. The mapping is of the form x = u + Δu.
  • Rigid Body. Upon rigid-body transformation, the distance between any pair of points is conserved. A single landmark is necessary to describe the translational component of the rigid-body transformation, while the rotational component is given by an angle. The mapping is of the form x = { {cos θ, −sin θ}, {sin θ, cos θ} } ⋅ u + Δu.
  • Scaled rotation. Upon scaled rotation, a straight line is mapped to a straight line; moreover, the angle between any pair of lines is conserved (this is sometimes called a conformal mapping). A pair of landmarks in each image is needed to give a complete description of a scaled rotation. The mapping is of the form x = λ { {cos θ, −sin θ}, {sin θ, cos θ} } ⋅ u + Δu.
  • Affine. Upon affine transformation, a straight line is mapped to a straight line, with conservation of flat angles between lines (parallel or coincident lines remain parallel or coincident). In 2D, a simplex—three landmarks—in each image is needed to give a complete description of an affine transformation. The mapping is of the form x = { {a11, a12}, {a21, a22} } ⋅ u + Δu.
  • Bilinear. Upon bilinear transformation, a straight line is mapped to a conic section. In 2D, four landmarks in each image are needed to give a complete description of a bilinear transformation. The mapping is of the form x = { {a11, a12}, {a21, a22} } ⋅ u + b u1 u2 + Δu.
  • None. Do not apply any transformation.

rules.description: Parameter 'Rules' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. List of rules that determine if/how slices are registered. The list of rules is tested for each slice in given order until a matching rule is found. The rule then determines if the slice should be aligned to the reference image, follows the transformation calculated for another input slice, or is ignored (added to the output as-is).If no rules matches, the slice 'Ignore' rule is automatically applied.

reference-t-index.name: Reference frame index
reference-t-index.description:
Depends from which slice the transformation is sourced.
For type 'Calculate transformation' this refers to a Z/C/T slice in the reference image.
For type 'Use transformation' this refers to a Z/C/T slice that already has a transformation.
Please note unless you only have 'Ignore' type rules there must be some alignment transformation that needs to be calculated.

rule-type.name: Type
rule-type.description: Determines if the slices matches by this rule are ignored, are aligned to the reference, or follow a pre-calculated transformation

reference-z-index.name: Reference Z index

name-filter.description:
Parameter 'Name filter' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Determines which data annotations are removed. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

generated-annotations.description:
Parameter 'Generated annotations' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. List of annotations to be created. Each is provided with two expressions: one for generating the name, and one for generating the value.

Both the name and value expressions have access to annotations and variables that correspond to the table columns of the input table.

Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/radiusY.description: Parameter 'Radius Y' of type with ID 'double'. null

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

radians.description: Parameter 'Output angles in radians' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, angles are output in radians

harris-l.description: Parameter 'Window size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The size of neighbourhood considered for corner detection

harris-k.description: Parameter 'Harris K' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Harris detector free parameter in the equation

min-level.description: Parameter 'Minimum level' of type with ID 'primitive.double'.

outputs/output-energy.description: Parameter 'Output energy' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

outputs/output-harris-index.description: Parameter 'Output Harris-index' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

outputs/output-orientation.description: Parameter 'Output orientation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

outputs/output-coherency.description: Parameter 'Output coherency' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

structure-tensor/local-window-sigma.description: Parameter 'Local window σ' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The structure tensor computes the orientation and isotropy properties in a local window. Here, the local window is characterized by a 2D Gaussian function of standard deviation σ. The parameter σ (expressed in pixel unit) is a critical parameter that determines the scale of the analysis. It should have a value roughly close to the structure of interest (e.g. thickness of the filament)

structure-tensor/gradient.description:
Parameter 'Gradient' of type with ID 'ij1-orientationj-gradient'. OrientationJ has different methods to compute the gradient.
  • The cubic spline is always the best choice, fast, accurate, quasi-isotropic and less boundary artefact
  • The finite difference gradient is very fast but it has poor isotropy properties
  • The Fourier gradient is exact but it has periodic boundary conditions.

clij-parameters/transform.description: Parameter 'Transform' of type with ID 'string'. null

method.description: Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'ij1-background-rollingball2d:background-method'. The method to estimate the background

apply-pre-smoothing.description: Parameter 'Apply pre-smoothing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a 3x3 box-filter is applied before extracting the background. Please note that this might lead to the background not necessary being lower than the foreground.

subtract.description: Parameter 'Subtract background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the background is subtracted. If disabled, the background is returned as output.

separate-channels.description: Parameter 'Separate channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Only valid for multi-channel images. If enabled, the algorithm is applied to each channel individually. If disabled, the background is calculated based on the HSV brightness value. The hue is unaffected.

apply-corner-correction.description: Parameter 'Apply corner-correction' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm attempts to avoid recognizing corners as background.

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Rolling ball / Rolling paraboloid radius in pixels

background-type.description: Parameter 'Background type' of type with ID 'ij1-background-rollingball2d:background-type'. Determines whether the background is dark or light.

clij-parameters/value.description: Parameter 'Value' of type with ID 'double'. null

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override R environment' of type with ID 'optional-r-environment'. If enabled, a different R environment is used for this Node.

dataset.description: Parameter 'Dataset' of type with ID 'r-import-dataset:dataset'. Determines which data set should be imported

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will segment in 3D. Otherwise, any 3D image will be processed per-slice. Please note that 3D segmentation requires large amounts of memory.

diameter-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with diameter' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the diameter is attached as annotation. Useful if you want to let Cellpose estimate the object diameters.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description: Parameter 'Average object diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, Cellpose will use the provided average diameter to find objects. Otherwise, Cellpose will estimate the diameter by itself.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose is used.

model-parameters/mean-diameter.description:
Parameter 'Mean diameter' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Mean diameter of the model. Only necessary if you are using a custom model. If you retrained a pretrained model, set following values:
  • Model based on Cytoplasm: Set to 30.0
  • Model based on Nuclei: Set to 17.0

model-parameters/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

model-parameters/model.description:
Parameter 'Model' of type with ID 'cellpose-model'. The model type that should be used.

See https://cellpose.readthedocs.io/en/latest/models.html for a description of all models

output-parameters/output-probabilities.description: Parameter 'Output probabilities' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output a greyscale image indicating the probability of each pixel being an object.

output-parameters/output-roi.description: Parameter 'Output 2D ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output a ROI list containing all detected objects

output-parameters/output-labels.description: Parameter 'Output labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output an image that contains a unique greyscale value for each detected object.

output-parameters/output-flows.description: Parameter 'Output flows' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output an HSB image that indicates the flow of each pixel.

output-parameters/output-styles.description: Parameter 'Output styles' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output a 1D vector that summarizes the image.

performance-parameters/batch-size.description: Parameter 'Batch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of 224x224 patches to run simultaneously on the GPU (can make smaller or bigger depending on GPU memory usage)

performance-parameters/tile-overlap.description: Parameter 'Tile overlap' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Fraction of overlap of tiles when computing flows

performance-parameters/tile.description: Parameter 'Tile images' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Tiles image to ensure GPU/CPU memory usage limited (recommended)

performance-parameters/resample.description: Parameter 'Resample' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, run dynamics at original image size (will be slower but create more accurate boundaries)

enhancement-parameters/augment.description: Parameter 'Augment' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Tiles image with overlapping tiles and flips overlapped regions to augment

enhancement-parameters/normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Normalize data so 0.0=1st percentile and 1.0=99th percentile of image intensities in each channel

enhancement-parameters/anisotropy.description: Parameter 'Anisotropy (3D)' of type with ID 'optional-double'. For 3D segmentation, optional rescaling factor (e.g. set to 2.0 if Z is sampled half as dense as X or Y)

enhancement-parameters/interpolate.description: Parameter 'Interpolate (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Interpolate during 2D dynamics (not available in 3D)

enhancement-parameters/net-average.description: Parameter 'Average all networks' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Runs the 4 built-in networks and averages them if True, runs one network if disabled

threshold-parameters/stitch-threshold.description: Parameter 'Stitch threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. If stitch_threshold>0.0 and not do_3D and equal image sizes, masks are stitched in 3D to return volume segmentation

threshold-parameters/cell-probability-th.description: Parameter 'Cell probability threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Cell probability threshold (all pixels with prob above threshold kept for masks)

threshold-parameters/flow-threshold.description: Parameter 'Flow threshold (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Flow error threshold (all cells with errors below threshold are kept) (not used for 3D)

threshold-parameters/min-size.description: Parameter 'Minimum size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Minimum number of pixels per mask, can turn off with -1

connectivity.description: Parameter 'Connectivity' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-2d'. The connectivity neighborhood

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will train in 3D. Otherwise, JIPipe will prepare the data by splitting 3D data into planes.

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Cellpose operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

label-data-annotation.description:
Parameter 'Label data annotation' of type with ID 'data-annotation-query-expression'. Determines which data annotation contains the labels. Please ensure that the appropriate label data is annotated to the raw input data. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

train-size-model.description: Parameter 'Train size model' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, also train a size model

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description:
Parameter 'Mean diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. The cell diameter. Depending on the model, you can choose following values:
  • Cytoplasm: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 30 pixels.
  • Nuclei: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 17 pixels.
  • Custom: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter appropriate for the model.
  • None: This will train from scratch. You can freely set the diameter. You also can set the diameter to 0 to disable scaling.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose is used.

epochs.description: Parameter 'Epochs' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of epochs that should be trained.

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-settings/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-settings/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

tweaks-settings/batch-size.description: Parameter 'Batch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'.

tweaks-settings/learning-rate.description: Parameter 'Learning rate' of type with ID 'primitive.double'.

tweaks-settings/concatenate-downsampled-.description: Parameter 'Concatenate downsampled layers' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Concatenate downsampled layers with upsampled layers (off by default which means they are added)

tweaks-settings/use-style-vector.description: Parameter 'Use style vector' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

tweaks-settings/min-train-masks.description: Parameter 'Minimum number of labels per image' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Minimum number of masks an image must have to use in training set. This value is by default 5 in the original Cellpose tool.

tweaks-settings/use-residual-connections.description: Parameter 'Use residual connections' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

tweaks-settings/weight-decay.description: Parameter 'Weight decay' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight decay

tweaks-settings/generate-connected-compo.description: Parameter 'Generate connected components' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, JIPipe will apply a connected component labeling to the annotated masks. If disabled, Cellpose is provided with the labels as-is, which might result in issues with the training.

clij-parameters/min_distance.description: Parameter 'Min Distance' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/max_distance.description: Parameter 'Max Distance' of type with ID 'double'. null

factor-x.description: Parameter 'Factor (X)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The smoothing factor in the X coordinate. Set to zero to disable smoothing

factor-y.description: Parameter 'Factor (Y)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The smoothing factor in the Y coordinate. Set to zero to disable smoothing

factor-z.description: Parameter 'Factor (Z)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The smoothing factor in the Z plane. Set to zero to disable smoothing

location-merging-function.description: Parameter 'Location merging function' of type with ID 'expression'. A function that determines how multiple coordinates are merged together

enforce-same-component.description: Parameter 'Prevent cross-object edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, new edges will never be created across two different objects. Disable this option if there are broken filaments in the input graph.

min-particle-circularity.description: Parameter 'Min particle circularity' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The minimum circularity (circularity = 4pi(area/perimeter^2)). The value range is from 0-1. If an object is not within the circularity range, it is removed from the results.

include-holes.description: Parameter 'Fill interior holes (flood-fill)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, interior holes are filled.

composite-roi.description: Parameter 'Composite ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, generate composite ROI that can contain holes. Please note that not all operations can appropriately handle composite ROI.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

min-particle-size.description: Parameter 'Min particle size' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The minimum particle size in the specified unit of the input image. If no unit is available, the unit is 'pixels'. If an object is not within the size range, it is removed from the results.

black-background.description: Parameter 'Black background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the background is assumed to be black. If disabled, black pixels are extracted as ROI.

annotation-type.description: Parameter 'Split slices annotation' of type with ID 'optional-string'. The annotation type generated by 'Split slices'. You can select no annotation type to disable this feature.

max-particle-circularity.description: Parameter 'Max particle circularity' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The maximum circularity (circularity = 4pi(area/perimeter^2)). The value range is from 0-1. If an object is not within the circularity range, it is removed from the results.

exclude-edges.description: Parameter 'Exclude edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, objects that are connected to the image edges are removed.

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Extracted measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. Please select which measurements should be extracted. Each measurement will be assigned to one or multiple output table columns.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

neighborhood.description: Parameter 'Neighborhood' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-2d'. Determines which neighborhood is used to find connected components.

split-slices.description: Parameter 'Split slices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, results are generated for each 2D slice. Otherwise all results are merged into one table and ROI.

max-particle-size.description: Parameter 'Max particle size' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The maximum particle size in the specified unit of the input image. If no unit is available, the unit is 'pixels'. If an object is not within the size range, it is removed from the results.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

per-slice.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice' of type with ID 'ij1-feature-vesselness-frangi:slicing-mode'. Applies the vesselness filter for each 2D slice instead for the whole multi-dimensional image.

border-definition.description: Parameter 'Border' of type with ID 'margin'. Defines the rectangle that is created within the image boundaries separate inside and outside. If a ROI intersects with the outside area (meaning that it is not contained within the rectangle), it is removed.

error-behavior.description: Parameter 'Error handling' of type with ID 'ij1:invalid-roi-outline-behavior'. What to do if a ROI could not be processed

map-line-color.description: Parameter 'Map line color' of type with ID 'optional-color-map'. Allows you to map the ROI line color to the order generated by the sorting. The color is rendered when converting into a RGB visualization.

map-fill-color.description: Parameter 'Map fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color-map'. Allows you to map the ROI fill color to the order generated by the sorting. The color is rendered when converting into a RGB visualization.
