The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/tileWidth.description: Parameter 'Tile Width' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/tileHeight.description: Parameter 'Tile Height' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/tileDepth.description: Parameter 'Tile Depth' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/marginWidth.description: Parameter 'Margin Width' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/marginHeight.description: Parameter 'Margin Height' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/marginDepth.description: Parameter 'Margin Depth' of type with ID 'double'. null

expression-z.description: Parameter 'Generate Z slices' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that is executed for each Z/C/T slice and generates an array of slice indices (or a single slice index) that determine which Z indices are exported. All indices begin with zero. Indices outside the available range are automatically wrapped. Return an empty array to skip a slice.

iterate-per-t.description: Parameter 'Iterate incoming frame slices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, all expressions are executed per T slice.

iterate-per-z.description: Parameter 'Iterate incoming Z slices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, all expressions are executed per Z slice.

annotate-c.description: Parameter 'Annotate with C indices' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the output is annotated with the source channel slices (zero-based)

expression-c.description: Parameter 'Generate channel slices' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that is executed for each Z/C/T slice and generates an array of slice indices (or a single slice index) that determine which C indices are exported. All indices begin with zero. Indices outside the available range are automatically wrapped. Return an empty array to skip a slice.

remove-duplicates.description: Parameter 'Remove duplicates' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, duplicate stack index definitions are removed.

annotate-z.description: Parameter 'Annotate with Z indices' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the output is annotated with the source Z slices (zero-based)

iterate-per-c.description: Parameter 'Iterate incoming channel slices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, all expressions are executed per C slice.

annotate-t.description: Parameter 'Annotate with T indices' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the output is annotated with the source frame slices (zero-based)

expression-t.description: Parameter 'Generate frame slices' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that is executed for each Z/C/T slice and generates an array of slice indices (or a single slice index) that determine which T indices are exported. All indices begin with zero. Indices outside the available range are automatically wrapped. Return an empty array to skip a slice.

continue-on-failure.description: Parameter 'Continue on failure' of type with ID 'jipipe:graph-run-partition-inherited-boolean'. If enabled, the pipeline will continue if a node within the group fails. For pass-through iteration, JIPipe will continue with the pipeline (no data is output from the group). If you enabled iteration, all successful results will be stored and passed as output.

show-limited-parameters.description: Parameter 'Show limited parameter set' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, only the exported parameters, name, and description are shown as parameters. The iteration step generation will also be hidden. This can be useful for educational pipelines.

exported-parameters.description: Parameter 'Exported parameters' of type with ID 'graph-node-parameters'. Allows you to export parameters from the group into the group node

continue-on-failure-backup-mode.description: Parameter 'Save inputs of failed sub-pipeline' of type with ID 'jipipe:graph-run-partition-inherited-boolean'. If enabled, the inputs of saved sub-pipelines will be saved to allow diagnostics. Increases memory consumption.

contents.description: Parameter 'Wrapped graph' of type with ID 'node-group:content'. The graph that is wrapped inside this node

iteration-mode.description:
Parameter 'Graph iteration mode' of type with ID 'graph-wrapper:iteration-mode'. Determines how the wrapped graph is iterated:
  • The data can be passed through. This means that the wrapped graph receives all data as-is and will be executed once.
  • The wrapped graph can be executed per iteration step. Here you can choose between an iterative iteration step (one item per slot) or a merging iteration step (multiple items per slot).

tag-to-annotation-importer/tags-to-list-.description: Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, write all tag names into a single annotation (as list). The name of the annotation is determined by the value of this parameter

Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation (filter)' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter out tags that will be included into 'Convert tags into single annotation' This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

spot-detector-parameters/target-channel.description:
Parameter 'Target channel' of type with ID 'int'. This detector is based on computing the determinant of the
Hessian matrix of the image to detector bright blobs.

It can be configured with a different spots size in XY and Z.
It can also return a normalized quality value, scaled from 0
to 1 for the spots of each time-point.

As discussed in Mikolajczyk et al.(2005), this detector has
a better edge response elimination than the LoG detector and is
suitable for detect spots in images with many strong edges.

suitable for detect spots in images with many strong edges.

code.description:
Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'. here must be an array 'tables' that contains all input rows as dictionary with following entries: 'data', 'nrow', and 'annotations'.
  • 'data' is a dictionary with the column name as key and values being an array of strings or doubles.
  • 'nrow' is an integer that contains the number of rows.
  • 'annotations' is a dictionary from string to string containing all annotations

code.description: Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'. Each table is passed as dictionary 'table' with the column name as key and values being an array of strings or doubles. The number of input rows can be accessed via the 'nrow' variable.

channel-name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with channel name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, create an annotation that contains the channel name as defined by the output slot

ignore-missing-channels.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm silently skips invalid assignments like extracting the 4th channel of a 2-channel image. If disabled, an error will be thrown if such a condition is detected.

channel-index-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with channel index' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, create an annotation that contains the channel index (starting with zero)

module-parameters/rangemin.description: Parameter 'Range Min' of type with ID 'double'.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/translateZ.description: Parameter 'Translate Z' of type with ID 'double'. null

spot-tracker-parameters/linking-max-dist.description:
Parameter 'Linking max distance' of type with ID 'double'. This tracker is the most simple one, and is based on nearest neighbor
search. The spots in the target frame are searched for the nearest neighbor
of each spot in the source frame. If the spots found are closer than the
maximal allowed distance, a link between the two is created.

The nearest neighbor search relies upon the KD-tree technique implemented
in imglib by Johannes Schindelin and friends. This ensure a very efficient tracking and makes this tracker suitable for situation where a huge number
of particles are to be tracked over a very large number of frames. However,
because of the naiveness of its principles, it can result in pathological
tracks. It can only do frame-to-frame linking; there cannot be any track
merging or splitting, and gaps will not be closed. Also, the end results are non-deterministic.

spot-tracker-parameters/iou-calculation.description:
Parameter 'Iou calculation' of type with ID 'string'. This tracker is a simple extension of the Intersection - over - Union (IoU) tracker.

It generates links between spots whose shapes overlap between consecutive frames. When several spots are eligible as a source for a target, the one with the largest IoU is chosen.

The minimal IoU parameter sets a threshold below which links won't be created. The scale factor allows for enlarging (>1) or shrinking (<1) the spot shapes before computing their IoU. Two methods can be used to compute IoU: The Fast one approximates the spot shapes by their rectangular bounding-box. The Precise one uses the actual spot polygon.

This tracker works in 2D and 3D. However in 3D, the IoU is computed from the bounding-boxes regardless of the choice of the IoU computation method. The Precise method is not implemented.

spot-tracker-parameters/scale-factor.description:
Parameter 'Scale factor' of type with ID 'double'. This tracker is a simple extension of the Intersection - over - Union (IoU) tracker.

It generates links between spots whose shapes overlap between consecutive frames. When several spots are eligible as a source for a target, the one with the largest IoU is chosen.

The minimal IoU parameter sets a threshold below which links won't be created. The scale factor allows for enlarging (>1) or shrinking (<1) the spot shapes before computing their IoU. Two methods can be used to compute IoU: The Fast one approximates the spot shapes by their rectangular bounding-box. The Precise one uses the actual spot polygon.

This tracker works in 2D and 3D. However in 3D, the IoU is computed from the bounding-boxes regardless of the choice of the IoU computation method. The Precise method is not implemented.

spot-tracker-parameters/min-iou.description:
Parameter 'Min iou' of type with ID 'double'. This tracker is a simple extension of the Intersection - over - Union (IoU) tracker.

It generates links between spots whose shapes overlap between consecutive frames. When several spots are eligible as a source for a target, the one with the largest IoU is chosen.

The minimal IoU parameter sets a threshold below which links won't be created. The scale factor allows for enlarging (>1) or shrinking (<1) the spot shapes before computing their IoU. Two methods can be used to compute IoU: The Fast one approximates the spot shapes by their rectangular bounding-box. The Precise one uses the actual spot polygon.

This tracker works in 2D and 3D. However in 3D, the IoU is computed from the bounding-boxes regardless of the choice of the IoU computation method. The Precise method is not implemented.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). file-format.description:
Parameter 'File format' of type with ID 'ij1-export-image-to-web:file-format'. The file format that should be used.
  • PNG, JPEG, BMP: 2D images only
  • AVI: 2D or 3D images only
  • TIFF: All images supported

movie-animated-dimension.description: Parameter 'Animated dimension' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. Only used if the format is AVI. Determines which dimension is animated

movie-fps.description: Parameter 'Animation FPS' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Only used if the format is AVI. The number of frames shown per second.

avi-compression.description: Parameter 'AVI compression' of type with ID 'avi-compression'. Only used if the format is AVI. Determines how the frames are compressed.

jpeg-quality.description: Parameter 'AVI JPEG quality' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Only used if the format is AVI and the compression is JPEG. Value from 0-100 that determines the JPEG quality (100 is best).

exporter/make-filesystem-compatible.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

exporter/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

exporter/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

tables.description: Parameter 'Tables' of type with ID 'results-table-list'. The tables are stored into the output slot.

module-parameters/concatenationaxis.description: Parameter 'Concatenation Axis' of type with ID 'int'.

num-threads.description: Parameter 'Number of threads' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of threads for parallel processing

kernel-y.description: Parameter 'Radius (Y)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the Y direction

kernel-x.description: Parameter 'Radius (X)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the X direction

kernel-z.description: Parameter 'Radius (Z)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the Z direction

prefer-isotropic-filter.description: Parameter 'Prefer isotropic filter (faster)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If possible, utilize a faster implementation using a rolling ball algorithm. Requires that the radii are the same. Not supported on all filters. If isotropic filtering is not possible or disabled, the node will fall back to a parallelized algorithm.

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge strategy' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how the newly generated annotations are merged with existing annotations.

column-names-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with column names' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the column names as JSON string

column-count-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with column count' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the column count

row-count-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with row count' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the row count

code.description: Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'.  The Python script contains a variable 'roi_list' that contains dictionaries. Each one has an item 'data' containing the ImageJ ROI, and a dictionary 'stats' containing the statistics.

max-volume-pixels.description: Parameter 'Maximum volume (pixels)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The maximum volume of objects (pixel units)

output-32-bit.description: Parameter 'Output 32-bit labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, generate 32-bit labels (support more than 32767 objects)

enable-watershed.description: Parameter 'Enable watershed' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, close spots will be separated

seeds-threshold.description: Parameter 'Seeds threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Determines which seeds will be used

min-volume-pixels.description: Parameter 'Minimum volume (pixels)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The minimum volume of objects (pixel units)

difference.description: Parameter 'Difference' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. A constant difference is used to compute local threshold. The local threshold value is then computed as ValueOfSeed – diff.

spot-segmentation-settings/segmentation-.description:
Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'ij3d-segmentation-spot3d:spot-segmentation-method'. When the threshold value has been determined, the plugin offers several
methods of voxel clustering that segment the object. The segmentation
proceeds by successive examination of voxels, starting from the seed.

* Classical : All neighbouring voxels with intensity value higher than the
threshold value (Local Background) are clustered to form the object.
* Maximum : This mode operates similarly as the previous one, but with one
additional constraint : the voxels are clustered in the object if their
intensity value is higher than the threshold and lower than the intensity
value of the previously clustered voxels. It can prevent to cluster pixels
from another close spot.
* Block : The same Maximum procedure is applied, but instead of
considering single voxels, block of voxel (all neighbors with value greater
than the local threshold) are examined. All voxels must satisfy the
constraint to be added to the cluster, else no voxel are added.

seed-segmentation-settings/radius.description: Parameter 'Seed radius' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. The radius for the automatically detected seeds

clij-parameters/min_grey_value.description: Parameter 'Min Grey Value' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/max_grey_value.description: Parameter 'Max Grey Value' of type with ID 'double'. null

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will train in 3D. Otherwise, JIPipe will prepare the data by splitting 3D data into planes.

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Cellpose operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

label-data-annotation.description:
Parameter 'Label data annotation' of type with ID 'data-annotation-query-expression'. Determines which data annotation contains the labels. Please ensure that the appropriate label data is annotated to the raw input data. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

train-size-model.description: Parameter 'Train size model' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, also train a size model

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description:
Parameter 'Mean diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. The cell diameter. Depending on the model, you can choose following values:
  • Cytoplasm: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 30 pixels.
  • Nuclei: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 17 pixels.
  • Custom: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter appropriate for the model.
  • None: This will train from scratch. You can freely set the diameter. You also can set the diameter to 0 to disable scaling.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose is used.

epochs.description: Parameter 'Epochs' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of epochs that should be trained.

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-settings/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-settings/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

tweaks-settings/num-test-images-per-epoc.description: Parameter 'Test images per epoch' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Allows to override the number of test images per epoch. Defaults to all images.

tweaks-settings/batch-size.description: Parameter 'Batch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'.

tweaks-settings/use-sgd.description: Parameter 'Use SGD' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Enabling this parameter may be useful for re-training. It is recommended to train with more epoch (e.g. 300 instead of 100) with this parameter enabled.

tweaks-settings/learning-rate.description: Parameter 'Learning rate' of type with ID 'primitive.double'.

tweaks-settings/num-training-images-per-.description: Parameter 'Training images per epoch' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Allows to override the number of training images per epoch. Defaults to all images.

tweaks-settings/min-train-masks.description: Parameter 'Minimum number of labels per image' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Minimum number of masks an image must have to use in training set. This value is by default 5 in the original Cellpose tool.

tweaks-settings/weight-decay.description: Parameter 'Weight decay' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight decay

tweaks-settings/generate-connected-compo.description: Parameter 'Generate connected components' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, JIPipe will apply a connected component labeling to the annotated masks. If disabled, Cellpose is provided with the labels as-is, which might result in issues with the training.

spot-detector-parameters/target-channel.description:
Parameter 'Target channel' of type with ID 'int'. This detector creates spots by thresholding a grayscale image.

Pixels in the designated channel that have a value larger than the threshold are considered as part of the foreground, and used to build connected regions. In 2D, spots are created with the (possibly simplified) contour of the region. In 3D, a spherical spot is created for each region in its center, with a volume equal to the region volume.

The spot quality stores the object area or volume in pixels.

spot-detector-parameters/intensity-thres.description:
Parameter 'Intensity threshold' of type with ID 'double'. This detector creates spots by thresholding a grayscale image.

Pixels in the designated channel that have a value larger than the threshold are considered as part of the foreground, and used to build connected regions. In 2D, spots are created with the (possibly simplified) contour of the region. In 3D, a spherical spot is created for each region in its center, with a volume equal to the region volume.

The spot quality stores the object area or volume in pixels.

spot-detector-parameters/simplify-contou.description:
Parameter 'Simplify contours' of type with ID 'boolean'. This detector creates spots by thresholding a grayscale image.

Pixels in the designated channel that have a value larger than the threshold are considered as part of the foreground, and used to build connected regions. In 2D, spots are created with the (possibly simplified) contour of the region. In 3D, a spherical spot is created for each region in its center, with a volume equal to the region volume.

The spot quality stores the object area or volume in pixels.

columns.description:
Parameter 'Selected columns' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that selects the columns. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

add-as-annotations.description: Parameter 'Add as annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, columns are added as annotation columns

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clij2.plug.name: CLIJ2 Euclidean Distance From Label Centroid Map

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

classifier-settings/classifier.description: Parameter 'Classifier' of type with ID 'weka-classifier'. The selected classifier

classifier-settings/balance-classes.description: Parameter 'Balance classes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. The classifier uses by the default all the user traces to train. By selecting this option, we filter first the classes in order to provide a balanced distribution of the samples. This implies that the less numerous classes will duplicate some of their samples and the more populated classes will lose some of their samples for the sake of even distribution. This option is strongly recommended if we want to give the same importance to all classes. An alternative is to use the Weka CostSensitiveClassifier and set a corresponding cost matrix.

feature-settings/use-neighbors.description: Parameter 'Use neighbors' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Set the use of the neighbor features

feature-settings/membrane-patch-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane patch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. this represents the size 𝑛×𝑛 of the field of view for the membrane projection filters. Only available for 2D features.

feature-settings/membrane-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane thickness' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Expected value of the membrane thickness, 1 pixel by default. The more accurate, the more precise the filter will be.

feature-settings/min-sigma.description: Parameter 'Minimum sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Minimum radius of the isotropic filters used to create the features. By default 1 pixel.

feature-settings/training-features.description:
Parameter 'Training features' of type with ID 'weka-feature-set-2d'. Here we can select and deselect the training features, which are the key of the learning procedure. The plugin creates a stack of images —one image for each feature. For instance, if only Gaussian blur is selected as a feature, the classifier will be trained on the original image and some blurred versions to it with different 𝜎 parameters for the Gaussian. If the input image is grayscale, the features will be calculated using double precision (32-bit images). In the case of RGB input images, the features will be RGB as well.
  • Gaussian blur: performs 𝑛 individual convolutions with Gaussian kernels with the normal 𝑛 variations of 𝜎. The larger the radius the more blurred the image becomes until the pixels are homogeneous.
  • Sobel filter: calculates an approximation of the gradient of the image intensity at each pixel. Gaussian blurs with 𝜎 varying as usual are performed prior to the filter.
  • Hessian: Calculates a Hessian matrix 𝐻 at each pixel. Prior to the application of any filters, a Gaussian blur with varying 𝜎 is performed.
  • Difference of gaussians: calculates two Gaussian blur images from the original image and subtracts one from the other. 𝜎 values are varied as usual, so 𝑛(𝑛−1)2 feature images are added to the stack.
  • Membrane projections: enhances membrane-like structures of the image through directional filtering. The initial kernel for this operation is hardcoded as a 19×19 zero matrix with the middle column entries set to 1. Multiple kernels are created by rotating the original kernel by 6 degrees up to a total rotation of 180 degrees, giving 30 kernels. Each kernel is convolved with the image and then the set of 30 images are Z-projected into a single image via 6 methods (sum of the pixels in each image, mean of the pixels in each image, standard deviation of the pixels in each image, median of the pixels in each image, maximum of the pixels in each image, minimum of the pixels in each image). Each of the 6 resulting images is a feature. Hence pixels in lines of similarly valued pixels in the image that are different from the average image intensity will stand out in the Z-projections.
  • Mean, Variance, Median, Minimum, Maximum: the pixels within a radius of 𝜎 pixels from the target pixel are subjected to the pertinent operation (mean/min etc.) and the target pixel is set to that value.
  • Anisotropic diffusion: the anisotropic diffusion filtering from Fiji with 20 iterations, 𝜎 smoothing per iterations, 𝑎1=0.10,0.35, 𝑎2=0.9, and an edge threshold set to the membrane size.
  • Bilateral filter: is very similar to the Mean filter but better preserves edges while averaging/blurring other parts of the image. The filter accomplishes this task by only averaging the values around the current pixel that are close in color value to the current pixel. The ‘closeness’ of other neighborhood pixels to the current pixels is determined by the specified threshold. I.e. for a value of 10 each pixel that contributes to the current mean have to be within 10 values of the current pixel. In our case, we combine spatial radius of 5 and 10, with a range radius of 50 and 100.
  • Lipschitz filter: from Mikulas Stencel plugin. This plugin implements Lipschitz cover of an image that is equivalent to a grayscale opening by a cone. The Lipschitz cover can be applied for the elimination of a slowly varying image background by subtraction of the lower Lipschitz cover (a top-hat procedure). A sequential double scan algorithm is used. We use down and top hats combination, with slope 𝑠=5,10,15,20,25.
  • Kuwahara filter: another noise-reduction filter that preserves edges. This is a version of the Kuwahara filter that uses linear kernels rather than square ones. We use the membrane patch size as kernel size, 30 angles and the three different criteria (Variance, Variance / Mean and Variance / Mean^2).
  • abor filter: at the moment this option may take some time and memory because it generates a very diverse range of Gabor filters (22). ’ This may undergo changes in the future’. The implementation details are included in this script. The Gabor filter is an edge detection and texture filter, which convolves several kernels at different angles with an image. Gabor filters are band-pass filters and therefore implement a frequency transformation.
  • Derivatives filter: calculates high order (4, 6, 8, 10) derivatives of the input image.
  • Laplacian filter: computes the Laplacian of the input image using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎.
  • Structure filter: calculates for all elements in the input image, the eigenvalues (smallest and largest) of the so-called structure tensor using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎 and integration scales 1 and 3.
  • Entropy: draws a circle of radius 𝑟 around each pixel; gets the histogram of that circle split in numBins chunks; then calculates the entropy as ∑𝑝 in histogram−𝑝∗log2(𝑝), where 𝑝 is the probability of each chunk in the histogram. numBins is equal to 32,64,128,256. 𝑟 is equal to 𝜎.
  • Neighbors: shifts the image in 8 directions by an certain number of pixel, 𝜎. Therefore creates 8𝑛 feature images.
More information: https://imagej.net/plugins/tws/

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

column-radius.description:
Parameter 'Column 'Radius'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the radius. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-x1.description:

transformation-type.description:
Parameter 'Transformation' of type with ID 'ij1-turbo-reg:transformation-type'. The type of transformation to be used.
  • Translation. Upon translation, a straight line is mapped to a straight line of identical orientation, with conservation of the distance between any pair of points. A single landmark in each image gives a complete description of a translation. The mapping is of the form x = u + Δu.
  • Rigid Body. Upon rigid-body transformation, the distance between any pair of points is conserved. A single landmark is necessary to describe the translational component of the rigid-body transformation, while the rotational component is given by an angle. The mapping is of the form x = { {cos θ, −sin θ}, {sin θ, cos θ} } ⋅ u + Δu.
  • Scaled rotation. Upon scaled rotation, a straight line is mapped to a straight line; moreover, the angle between any pair of lines is conserved (this is sometimes called a conformal mapping). A pair of landmarks in each image is needed to give a complete description of a scaled rotation. The mapping is of the form x = λ { {cos θ, −sin θ}, {sin θ, cos θ} } ⋅ u + Δu.
  • Affine. Upon affine transformation, a straight line is mapped to a straight line, with conservation of flat angles between lines (parallel or coincident lines remain parallel or coincident). In 2D, a simplex—three landmarks—in each image is needed to give a complete description of an affine transformation. The mapping is of the form x = { {a11, a12}, {a21, a22} } ⋅ u + Δu.
  • Bilinear. Upon bilinear transformation, a straight line is mapped to a conic section. In 2D, four landmarks in each image are needed to give a complete description of a bilinear transformation. The mapping is of the form x = { {a11, a12}, {a21, a22} } ⋅ u + b u1 u2 + Δu.
  • None. Do not apply any transformation.

rules.description: Parameter 'Rules' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. List of rules that determine if/how slices are registered. The list of rules is tested for each slice in given order until a matching rule is found. The rule then determines if the slice should be aligned to the reference image, follows the transformation calculated for another input slice, or is ignored (added to the output as-is).If no rules matches, the slice 'Ignore' rule is automatically applied.

reference-t-index.name: Reference frame index
reference-t-index.description:
Depends from which slice the transformation is sourced.
For type 'Calculate transformation' this refers to a Z/C/T slice in the reference image.
For type 'Use transformation' this refers to a Z/C/T slice that already has a transformation.
Please note unless you only have 'Ignore' type rules there must be some alignment transformation that needs to be calculated.

rule-type.name: Type
rule-type.description: Determines if the slices matches by this rule are ignored, are aligned to the reference, or follow a pre-calculated transformation

reference-z-index.name: Reference Z index

Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/constant.description: Parameter 'Constant' of type with ID 'double'. null

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

method.description:
Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'org.hkijena.jipipe.plugins.imagejalgorithms.nodes.contrast.HistogramContrastEnhancerAlgorithm.Method'. The method that should be used to enhance the contrast.
  • Stretch histogram sets the min/max display range to the min/max of the histogram.
  • Stretch histogram + normalize additionally will recalculate the pixel values of the image so the range is equal to the maximum range for the data type, or 0 - 1.0 for float images. The maximum range is 0-255 for 8-bit images and 0-65535 for 16-bit images. Normalization is silently skipped for RGB images.
  • Equalize histogram uses an histogram equalization algorithm. 32bit images are not supported and will be silently skipped.
  • Equalize histogram (Classic) also uses an histogram equalization algorithm. Histogram values are used directly instead of their SQRT. 32bit images are not supported and will be silently skipped.

saturated-pixels.description:
Parameter 'Saturated pixels (%)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Only used if histogram stretching is used.
Determines the number of pixels in the image that are allowed to become saturated. Increasing this value increases contrast. This value should be greater than zero to prevent a few outlying pixel from causing the histogram stretch to not work as intended.

use-stack-histogram.description: Parameter 'Use stack histogram' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the histogram of all slices is used instead of the per-slice histograms.

max-radius.description: Parameter 'Max radius' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. The maximum radius (0 for no maximum radius)

source-wrap-mode.description: Parameter 'Border pixel mode' of type with ID 'ij1-source-wrap-mode'. Determines how border pixels are handled. If set to 'Skip' border pixels are still processed by the expression, but the local area variable will be missing pixels.

expression.description: Parameter 'Expression' of type with ID 'expression'. The mathematical expression that is applied to each pixel position in the input images. Additionally to the positional variables, there are variables available that are named according to the input slots and contain the current pixel value of this slot. Arrays prefixed with 'Local.' are also available and contain the local image values (Example: Local.L1). The array contains the pixel values in Row-Major form (x0y0, x1y0, x2y0, x1y1, ...)

local-window-height.description: Parameter 'Local window height' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Height of the local window

local-window-width.description: Parameter 'Local window width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Width of the local window

connectivity.description: Parameter 'Connectivity' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-2d'. Determines the neighborhood around each pixel that is checked for connectivity

dark-background.description: Parameter 'Dark background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If the background color is dark. Disable this if your image has a bright background.

contrast-threshold.description: Parameter 'Contrast threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The contrast threshold. Bernsen recommends a value of 15.

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The radius of the circular local window.

classifier-settings/classifier.description: Parameter 'Classifier' of type with ID 'weka-classifier'. The selected classifier

classifier-settings/balance-classes.description: Parameter 'Balance classes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. The classifier uses by the default all the user traces to train. By selecting this option, we filter first the classes in order to provide a balanced distribution of the samples. This implies that the less numerous classes will duplicate some of their samples and the more populated classes will lose some of their samples for the sake of even distribution. This option is strongly recommended if we want to give the same importance to all classes. An alternative is to use the Weka CostSensitiveClassifier and set a corresponding cost matrix.

feature-settings/use-neighbors.description: Parameter 'Use neighbors' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Set the use of the neighbor features

feature-settings/membrane-patch-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane patch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. this represents the size 𝑛×𝑛 of the field of view for the membrane projection filters. Only available for 2D features.

feature-settings/membrane-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane thickness' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Expected value of the membrane thickness, 1 pixel by default. The more accurate, the more precise the filter will be.

feature-settings/min-sigma.description: Parameter 'Minimum sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Minimum radius of the isotropic filters used to create the features. By default 1 pixel.

feature-settings/training-features.description:
Parameter 'Training features' of type with ID 'weka-feature-set-2d'. Here we can select and deselect the training features, which are the key of the learning procedure. The plugin creates a stack of images —one image for each feature. For instance, if only Gaussian blur is selected as a feature, the classifier will be trained on the original image and some blurred versions to it with different 𝜎 parameters for the Gaussian. If the input image is grayscale, the features will be calculated using double precision (32-bit images). In the case of RGB input images, the features will be RGB as well.
  • Gaussian blur: performs 𝑛 individual convolutions with Gaussian kernels with the normal 𝑛 variations of 𝜎. The larger the radius the more blurred the image becomes until the pixels are homogeneous.
  • Sobel filter: calculates an approximation of the gradient of the image intensity at each pixel. Gaussian blurs with 𝜎 varying as usual are performed prior to the filter.
  • Hessian: Calculates a Hessian matrix 𝐻 at each pixel. Prior to the application of any filters, a Gaussian blur with varying 𝜎 is performed.
  • Difference of gaussians: calculates two Gaussian blur images from the original image and subtracts one from the other. 𝜎 values are varied as usual, so 𝑛(𝑛−1)2 feature images are added to the stack.
  • Membrane projections: enhances membrane-like structures of the image through directional filtering. The initial kernel for this operation is hardcoded as a 19×19 zero matrix with the middle column entries set to 1. Multiple kernels are created by rotating the original kernel by 6 degrees up to a total rotation of 180 degrees, giving 30 kernels. Each kernel is convolved with the image and then the set of 30 images are Z-projected into a single image via 6 methods (sum of the pixels in each image, mean of the pixels in each image, standard deviation of the pixels in each image, median of the pixels in each image, maximum of the pixels in each image, minimum of the pixels in each image). Each of the 6 resulting images is a feature. Hence pixels in lines of similarly valued pixels in the image that are different from the average image intensity will stand out in the Z-projections.
  • Mean, Variance, Median, Minimum, Maximum: the pixels within a radius of 𝜎 pixels from the target pixel are subjected to the pertinent operation (mean/min etc.) and the target pixel is set to that value.
  • Anisotropic diffusion: the anisotropic diffusion filtering from Fiji with 20 iterations, 𝜎 smoothing per iterations, 𝑎1=0.10,0.35, 𝑎2=0.9, and an edge threshold set to the membrane size.
  • Bilateral filter: is very similar to the Mean filter but better preserves edges while averaging/blurring other parts of the image. The filter accomplishes this task by only averaging the values around the current pixel that are close in color value to the current pixel. The ‘closeness’ of other neighborhood pixels to the current pixels is determined by the specified threshold. I.e. for a value of 10 each pixel that contributes to the current mean have to be within 10 values of the current pixel. In our case, we combine spatial radius of 5 and 10, with a range radius of 50 and 100.
  • Lipschitz filter: from Mikulas Stencel plugin. This plugin implements Lipschitz cover of an image that is equivalent to a grayscale opening by a cone. The Lipschitz cover can be applied for the elimination of a slowly varying image background by subtraction of the lower Lipschitz cover (a top-hat procedure). A sequential double scan algorithm is used. We use down and top hats combination, with slope 𝑠=5,10,15,20,25.
  • Kuwahara filter: another noise-reduction filter that preserves edges. This is a version of the Kuwahara filter that uses linear kernels rather than square ones. We use the membrane patch size as kernel size, 30 angles and the three different criteria (Variance, Variance / Mean and Variance / Mean^2).
  • abor filter: at the moment this option may take some time and memory because it generates a very diverse range of Gabor filters (22). ’ This may undergo changes in the future’. The implementation details are included in this script. The Gabor filter is an edge detection and texture filter, which convolves several kernels at different angles with an image. Gabor filters are band-pass filters and therefore implement a frequency transformation.
  • Derivatives filter: calculates high order (4, 6, 8, 10) derivatives of the input image.
  • Laplacian filter: computes the Laplacian of the input image using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎.
  • Structure filter: calculates for all elements in the input image, the eigenvalues (smallest and largest) of the so-called structure tensor using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎 and integration scales 1 and 3.
  • Entropy: draws a circle of radius 𝑟 around each pixel; gets the histogram of that circle split in numBins chunks; then calculates the entropy as ∑𝑝 in histogram−𝑝∗log2(𝑝), where 𝑝 is the probability of each chunk in the histogram. numBins is equal to 32,64,128,256. 𝑟 is equal to 𝜎.
  • Neighbors: shifts the image in 8 directions by an certain number of pixel, 𝜎. Therefore creates 8𝑛 feature images.
More information: https://imagej.net/plugins/tws/

feature-settings/max-sigma.description: Parameter 'Maximum sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Maximum radius of the isotropic filters used to create the features. By default 16 pixels.

expression.description: Parameter 'Expression' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that processes the path. Should return a new path. If 'null' is returned, the path will be filtered out.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

slice-threshold-mode.description:
Parameter 'Multi-slice thresholding' of type with ID 'slice-threshold-mode'. Determines how thresholds are calculated if an image has multiple slices.
  • Apply threshold per slice calculates and applies the threshold for each slice.
  • Combine slice statistics calculates statistics for all pixels in the image.
  • Combine thresholds per slice calculates the threshold for each slice. One threshold for all slices is calculated via another math expression.

access-pixels.description: Parameter 'Pixels are available as variables' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the list of all pixels that are considered for the statistics are available as variable 'pixels'. Please note that this will slow down the performance due to necessary conversions.

threshold-annotation-strategy.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

source-area.description: Parameter 'Calculate threshold based on ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines from which image areas the pixel values used for calculating the thresholds are extracted from.

min-threshold/threshold-combine-expressi.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

min-threshold/threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

min-threshold/thresholding-function.description: Parameter 'Thresholding function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed for each set of slices. The expression should return a number that will be used as threshold. A pixel is set to 255 if its value is larger than this threshold.

max-threshold/threshold-combine-expressi.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

max-threshold/threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

max-threshold/thresholding-function.description: Parameter 'Thresholding function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed for each set of slices. The expression should return a number that will be used as threshold. A pixel is set to 255 if its value is larger than this threshold.

counting-column.description:
Parameter 'Counting column' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. The column that contains the counter (e.g., the time frame) This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

ignore-empty-tables.description: Parameter 'Ignore empty tables' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ignore empty tables

max-counter.description: Parameter 'Custom maximum counter' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, override the automatically determined maximum counter. Otherwise, the maximum is the maximum in the whole table

expected-step.description: Parameter 'Expected step' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The step between two consecutive series points. For example, the step between 5 and 6 is 1. If the calculated step is larger than the provided value, a new row is generated. Must be a positive number.

min-counter.description: Parameter 'Custom minimum counter' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, override the automatically determined minimum counter. Otherwise, the minimum is the minimum in the whole table

default-values.description: Parameter 'Default values' of type with ID 'table-column-expression-generator-column-processor-list'. Determines the default values if a row is missing

prune-cycles-method.description:
Parameter 'Prune cycles method' of type with ID 'ij1-analyze-skeleton-2d3d:remove-cycles-method'. Allows the selection of a method to prune possible loops in the skeleton
  • None: no cycle detection nor pruning is performed.
  • Shortest branch: the shortest branch among the loop branches will be cut in its middle point.
  • Lowest intensity voxel: the darkest voxel among the loop voxels will be cut (set to 0) in the input image.
  • Lowest intensity branch: the darkest (in average) branch among the loop branches will be cut in its darkest voxel.

prune-ends-method.description:
Parameter 'Prune ends methods' of type with ID 'ij1-analyze-skeleton-2d3d:remove-ends-method'. Allows the selection of a method to prune any branch that ends in an end-point
  • None: branches that end in an end point are not removed.
  • All:branches that end in an end point are removed.
  • Exclude ROI: branches that end in an end point are removed; regions within the ROI are excluded

output-parameters/output-skeleton-table.description: Parameter 'Output skeleton table' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, output a table with all skeletons

output-parameters/output-largest-shortes.description: Parameter 'Output largest shortest paths' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, output an image that highlights the largest shortest paths

output-parameters/output-tagged-skeleton.description: Parameter 'Output tagged skeletons' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, output an image where end-point voxels are displayed in blue, slab voxels in orange and junction voxels in purple

output-parameters/output-branch-table.description: Parameter 'Output branch table' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, output a table with all detected branches

output-parameters/output-skeleton-labels.description: Parameter 'Output skeleton labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, output a label image of all skeletons

value-column.description:
Parameter 'Value column' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The column that contains the values This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

cumulative.description: Parameter 'Cumulative' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the histogram will be cumulative

key-column.description:

  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/iterations.description: Parameter 'Iterations' of type with ID 'double'. null

annotation-prefix.description: Parameter 'Annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Prefix added to columns generated from data annotations.

annotation-name-filter.description:
Parameter 'Annotation name filter' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Filters the name of the added annotations. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

apply-per-frame.description: Parameter 'Apply per frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each frame separately. If false, all frames are put together.

split-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Split after operation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are split into connected components after the operation is applied. This is useful as some operations create only one ROI output with multiple unconnected components.

apply-per-channel.description: Parameter 'Apply per channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each channel-slice separately. If false, all channel-slices are put together. Please note that 'Channel' does not refer to a pixel channel like Red in RGB.

apply-per-slice.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each Z-slice separately. If false, all Z-slices are put together.

operation.description: Parameter 'Operation' of type with ID 'logical-operation'. The operation to apply on the list of ROI

series-name.description:
Parameter 'Series name' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to generate the series name This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

input-columns/Category.description: Parameter 'Category' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Categories displayed in the X axis. Must correspond to each value. (String column)

input-columns/Group.description: Parameter 'Group' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Groups to color the bars. Shown in the legend. Must correspond to each value. (String column)

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge strategy' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how annotations that are added in the Python script are merged into existing annotations.

code.description:
Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'. The Python script to be executed. The script comes with various API functions and variables that allow to communicate with JIPipe:
  • jipipe_inputs is a dict of input slots.
  • jipipe_outputs is a dict of output slots.
  • jipipe_annotations is a dict of annotation variables of the current iteration step.
  • jipipe_variables is a dict of variables passed from the script parameters.
The script is designed to be used with the JIPipe Python API (supplied automatically by default). You can find the full API documentation here: https://jipipe.hki-jena.de/apidocs/python-current/index.html

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Python operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node.

output-probability-maps.description: Parameter 'Output probability maps' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, output probability maps instead of class labels.

num-threads.description: Parameter 'Override number of threads' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, set the number of threads to be utilized. Set to zero for automated assignment of threads.

tiling-parameters/tile-size-y.description: Parameter 'Tile height' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Height of each tile

tiling-parameters/overlap-x.description: Parameter 'Overlap (X)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. If greater than zero, adds an overlap border around each tile to potentially remove issues when classification is applied close to the image border. Has no effect if 'Use Weka's integrated tiling' is active.

tiling-parameters/tile-size-x.description: Parameter 'Tile width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Width of each tile

tiling-parameters/overlap-y.description: Parameter 'Overlap (Y)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. If greater than zero, adds an overlap border around each tile to potentially remove issues when classification is applied close to the image border. Has no effect if 'Use Weka's integrated tiling' is active.

tiling-parameters/border-mode.description:
Parameter 'Border mode' of type with ID 'ij1-border-mode'. Determines how the border is generated if an overlap is applied. Has no effect if 'Use Weka's integrated tiling' is active.
  • Constant: Set the border to a constant value (zero/black unless configurable)
  • Repeat: Repeat the nearest non-border pixel
  • Mirror: Mirror the pixel values across the border
  • Tile: Tile the image

tiling-parameters/apply-tiling.description: Parameter 'Apply tiling' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the input image is first split into tiles that are processed individually by the Weka segmentation. This can greatly reduce the memory cost. If borders are an issue, consider enabling the generation of a border.

tiling-parameters/use-weka-native-tiling.description: Parameter 'Use Weka's integrated tiling' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the native Weka tiling algorithm is utilized. 'Border mode', 'Overlap (X)', and 'Overlap ('Y') do not have any effect in this case.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

apply-per-frame.description: Parameter 'Apply per frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each frame separately. If false, all frames are put together.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

index-annotation.description: Parameter 'Generated annotation' of type with ID 'optional-string'. Optional. The annotation will contain the image slice position that was used to generate the statistics.

apply-per-channel.description: Parameter 'Apply per channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each channel-slice separately. If false, all channel-slices are put together. Please note that 'Channel' does not refer to a pixel channel like Red in RGB.

apply-per-slice.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each Z-slice separately. If false, all Z-slices are put together.

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Extracted measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. Please select which measurements should be extracted. Each measurement will be assigned to one or multiple output table columns.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized.
