prefer-associated-image.description: Parameter 'Prefer ROI-associated images' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. ROI can carry a reference to an image (e.g. the thresholding input). With this option enabled, this image is preferred to generating a mask based on the pure ROIs.

Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

max-volume-pixels.description: Parameter 'Max volume (pixels)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The maximum volume of the detected objects

criteria-method.description:
Parameter 'Criteria method' of type with ID 'ij3d-segmentation-iterative-thresholding:criteria-method'.

criteria to pick the best threshold :

  • Elongation : the threshold leading to the most round object is chosen (minimal elongation).
  • Volume : the threshold leading to the largest object.
  • MSER : the threshold where the volume of the object is most stable (minimal variation).
  • Edges : the threshold where the objects has maximal edges.

segment-results-method.description: Parameter 'Segment results' of type with ID 'ij3d-segmentation-iterative-thresholding:segment-results-method'.

min-threshold.description: Parameter 'Minimum threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The minimum threshold

min-volume-pixels.description: Parameter 'Min volume (pixels)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The minimum volume of the detected objects

enable-filtering.description: Parameter 'Filter before thresholding' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Filter the image before thresholding with a 3D median filter with radii proportional to the minimal volume.

threshold-method.description:
Parameter 'Threshold method' of type with ID 'ij3d-segmentation-iterative-thresholding:threshold-method'.

The thresholds tested can be tuned with 3 options with the value parameter :

  • Step : threshold are tested each step value.
  • Kmeans : histogram is analysed and clustered into value classes using a KMeans algorithm.
  • Volume : a constant number of pixels between two thresholds using value thresholds.

For 8-bits images it is recommended to use the method Step with value between 1 and 5. For 16-bits images try Step with values between 5 and 100 depending on the dynamic of your data. Note than the more threshold tested the more memory used.

min-contrast-exp.description: Parameter 'Minimum contrast (Exp)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to exclude regions from the calculation that have a low contrast

value-method.description: Parameter 'Threshold method: value' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Value associated to the thresholding method. Please read the documentation of the 'Threshold method' parameter.

start-at-mean.description: Parameter 'Start at mean' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. In order not to test low thresholds you can specify to start with the mean value of the image as the lowest threshold or specify manually the lowest threshold to start with.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. file-format.description: Parameter 'File format' of type with ID 'ij1-export-table:file-format'. The format of the exported file

relative-to-project-dir.description: Parameter 'Output relative to project directory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, outputs will be preferably generated relative to the project directory. Otherwise, JIPipe will store the results in an automatically generated directory. Has no effect if an absolute path is provided.

output-directory.description: Parameter 'Output directory' of type with ID 'path'. Can be a relative or absolute directory. All collected files will be put into this directory. If relative, it is relative to the output slot's output directory that is generated based on the current run's output path.

exporter/make-filesystem-compatible.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

exporter/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

exporter/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

parameter-key-filter.description:
Parameter 'Parameter key filter' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter only specific parameter keys. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

node-name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with node name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the output parameters are annotated with the node name

node-alias-id-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with node alias ID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the output parameters are annotated with the unique node ID (human-readable)

node-uuid-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with node UUID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the output parameters are annotated with the unique node ID

bio-formats-settings/split-time-points.description: Parameter 'Split time points' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Timelapse data will be opened as a separate stack for each timepoint.

bio-formats-settings/stitch-tiles.description: Parameter 'Stitch tiles' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Stitch tiles - Performs very simple stitching of tiles.  The overlap is assumed to be 0%, and the stage coordinates are used to determine the proper placement of the tiles. This is useful for seeing a quick preview of what the stitched image might look like, but is not a substitute for proper stitching plugins such as the 2D/3D Stitching plugin.

bio-formats-settings/concatenate.description: Parameter 'Concatenate compatible series' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Allows multiple image series to be joined end to end.  Example: You want to join two sequential timelapse series.

bio-formats-settings/stack-order.description: Parameter 'Stack order' of type with ID 'import-imagej-bioformats:order'.

bio-formats-settings/split-channels.description: Parameter 'Split channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Each channel is opened as a separate stack.  This option is especially useful if you want to merge the channels into a specific order, rather than automatically assign channels to the order of RGB. The bit depth is preserved.

bio-formats-settings/color-mode.description:
Parameter 'Color mode' of type with ID 'import-imagej-bioformats:color-mode'. Color mode - Visualizes channels according to the specified scheme.  Possible choices are:
Default - Display channels as closely as possible to how they are stored in the file.
Composite - Open as a merged composite image. Channels are colorized according to metadata present in the dataset (if any), or in the following default order: 1=red, 2=green, 3=blue, 4=gray, 5=cyan, 6=magenta, 7=yellow.
Colorized - Open with each channel in a separate plane, colorized according to metadata present in the dataset (if any), or in the default order (see Composite above).
Grayscale - Open with each channel in a separate plane, displayed in plain grayscale.
Custom - Same as Colorized, except that you can explicitly choose how to colorize each channel.
Note that ImageJ can only composite together 7 or fewer channels. With more than 7 channels, some of the modes above may not work.

bio-formats-settings/split-focal-planes.description: Parameter 'Split focal planes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Each focal plane is opened as a separate stack.

bio-formats-settings/swap-dimensions.description: Parameter 'Swap dimensions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Allows reassignment of dimensional axes (e.g., channel, Z and time).  Bio-Formats is supposed to be smart about handling multidimensional image data, but in some cases gets things wrong. For example, when stitching together a dataset from multiple files using the Group files with similar names option, Bio-Formats may not know which dimensional axis the file numbering is supposed to represent. It will take a guess, but in case it guesses wrong, you can use Swap dimensions to reassign which dimensions are which.

bio-formats-settings/crop.description: Parameter 'Crop images' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Image planes may be cropped during import to conserve memory. Use the 'Crop regions' parameter to define which regions should be cropped.

bio-formats-settings/auto-scale.description: Parameter 'Auto scale' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Stretches the channel histograms to each channel's global minimum and maximum value throughout the stack. Does not alter underlying values in the image.  If unselected, all channel histograms are scaled to the image's digitization bit depth. Note that this range may be narrower than the bit depth of the file. For example, a 16-bit file may contain intensities digitized to 16-bits (0-65,535), to 12-bits (0-4,095), to 10-bits (0-1,023), etc.  Note that you can use the Brightness & Contrast or Window/Level controls to adjust the contrast range regardless of whether this option is used. The histogram will provide min/max values in the stack.

Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

form-data/true-string.description: Parameter 'Value for 'TRUE'' of type with ID 'optional-string'. The annotation value that is written if the form value is TRUE

form-data/form:description.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. Description of the element displayed to the user.

form-data/form:show-name.description: Parameter 'Show name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the name of the form element is shown next to it.

form-data/form:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Name of the form element. Hidden if 'Show name' is disabled.

form-data/false-string.description: Parameter 'Value for 'FALSE'' of type with ID 'optional-string'. The annotation value that is written if the form value is FALSE

form-data/initial-value.description: Parameter 'Initial value' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. The initial value

form-data/form:tabs/form:tab-annotation.description: Parameter 'Tab annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The annotation that contains the tab name.

form-data/form:tabs/form:tab.description: Parameter 'Tab' of type with ID 'string'. The tab where this form data will appear in.

form-data/form:io/output-annotation.description: Parameter 'Output annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Determines into which annotation the user input is written.

form-data/form:io/merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge output annotation' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how the output annotation is merged with existing values.

form-data/form:io/input-annotation.description: Parameter 'Input annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The annotation used to override the initial value. If the annotation does not exist, the standard initial value is used.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

iterations.description: Parameter 'Iterations' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of removal iterations

ij1-color-convert-to-lab.name: Convert color image to LAB colors

reinterpret-channels.description: Parameter 'Reinterpret channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the image channels are reinterpreted instead of converted.

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will segment in 3D. Otherwise, any 3D image will be processed per-slice. Please note that 3D segmentation requires large amounts of memory.

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Cellpose operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

diameter-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with diameter' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the diameter is attached as annotation. Useful if you want to let Cellpose estimate the object diameters.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description: Parameter 'Average object diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, Cellpose will use the provided average diameter to find objects. Otherwise, Cellpose will estimate the diameter by itself.

enable-multichannel.description: Parameter 'Enable multichannel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, multiple image channels are passed to Cellpose. Otherwise, each channel will be processed individually.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose 3.x is used.

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-parameters/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-parameters/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

output-parameters/output-probabilities.description: Parameter 'Output probabilities' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel

output-parameters/output-roi.description: Parameter 'Output 2D ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output a ROI list containing all detected objects

output-parameters/output-labels.description: Parameter 'Output labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output an image that contains a unique greyscale value for each detected object.

output-parameters/output-flows-xy.description: Parameter 'Output flows XY' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel

output-parameters/output-flows-z.description: Parameter 'Output flows Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)

output-parameters/output-flows-d.description: Parameter 'Output flows d' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)

enhancement-parameters/normalize-percent.description: Parameter 'Normalize percentile' of type with ID 'optional-vector2i'. The lower and upper percentile values for normalization

enhancement-parameters/normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Normalize data so 0.0=1st percentile and 1.0=99th percentile of image intensities in each channel

enhancement-parameters/anisotropy.description: Parameter 'Anisotropy (3D)' of type with ID 'optional-double'. For 3D segmentation, optional rescaling factor (e.g. set to 2.0 if Z is sampled half as dense as X or Y)

enhancement-parameters/interpolate.description: Parameter 'Interpolate (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Interpolate during 2D dynamics (not available in 3D)

enhancement-parameters/niter.description: Parameter 'Niter dynamics (elongated objects)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of iterations for dynamics for mask creation, default of 0 means it is proportional to diameter, set to a larger number like 2000 for very long ROIs

enhancement-parameters/flow-3d-smoothing.description: Parameter 'Flow smoothing (3D)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Stddev of gaussian for smoothing of dP for dynamics in 3D, default of 0 means no smoothing

enhancement-parameters/resample.description: Parameter 'Resample' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Enable dynamics on the full image, making the algorithm slower for images with large object diameters

threshold-parameters/stitch-threshold.description: Parameter 'Stitch threshold' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Compute masks in 2D then stitch together masks with IoU>0.9 across planes

threshold-parameters/cell-probability-th.description: Parameter 'Cell probability threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Cellprob threshold, default is 0, decrease to find more and larger masks

threshold-parameters/flow-threshold.description: Parameter 'Flow threshold (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Flow error threshold, 0 turns off this optional QC step. Default: 0.4

threshold-parameters/exclude-on-edges.description: Parameter 'Exclude masks on edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Discard masks which touch edges of image

limit.description:
Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'integer-range'. Determines which indices are passed to the output. The first index is zero.

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

spot-detector-parameters/target-channel.description:
Parameter 'Target channel' of type with ID 'int'. This detector is based on an approximation of the LoG operator
by differences of Gaussian (DoG). Computations are made in direct space.
It is the quickest for small spot sizes (< ~5 pixels).

Spots found too close are suppressed. This detector can do sub-pixel
localization of spots using a quadratic fitting scheme. It is based on
the scale-space framework made by Stephan Preibisch for ImgLib.

clij-parameters/scalar.description: Parameter 'Scalar' of type with ID 'double'. null

sigma-color.description: Parameter 'Sigma (Color)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Filter sigma in the color space. A larger value of the parameter means that farther colors within the pixel neighborhood (see sigmaSpace) will be mixed together, resulting in larger areas of semi-equal color.

sigma-space.description: Parameter 'Sigma (Space)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Filter sigma in the coordinate space. A larger value of the parameter means that farther pixels will influence each other as long as their colors are close enough (see sigmaColor ). When d>0, it specifies the neighborhood size regardless of sigmaSpace. Otherwise, d is proportional to sigmaSpace.

diameter.description: Parameter 'Diameter' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Diameter of each pixel neighborhood that is used during filtering. If it is non-positive, it is computed from sigmaSpace.

border-type.description: Parameter 'Border type' of type with ID 'opencv-border-type'. Border mode used to extrapolate pixels outside of the image

Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

annotation-type.description:
Parameter 'Removed annotations' of type with ID 'annotation-query-expression'. Annotations that match any of the filters are removed. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

code.description: Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'. Access to the iteration step is done via a variable 'data_batch' that provides access to all input and output data, as well as annotations.Input slots can be accessed from variables 'input_slots' (array), 'input_slots_map' (map from name to slot). Output slots can be accessed from variables 'output_slots' (array), 'output_slots_map' (map from name to slot). A variable 'progress_info' provides the current progress logger instance.

index-meshes.description: Parameter 'Index/simplify meshes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, meshes are automatically indexed (simplified), which reduces the size of the output file, but needs additional processing time

relative-to-project-dir.description: Parameter 'Output relative to project directory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, outputs will be preferably generated relative to the project directory. Otherwise, JIPipe will store the results in an automatically generated directory. Has no effect if an absolute path is provided.

output-directory.description: Parameter 'Output directory' of type with ID 'path'. Can be a relative or absolute directory. All collected files will be put into this directory. If relative, it is relative to the output slot's output directory that is generated based on the current run's output path.

exporter/make-filesystem-compatible.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

exporter/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

exporter/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

draw-vertical.description: Parameter 'Draw vertical scale bar' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a vertical scale bar is drawn

bar-thickness.description: Parameter 'Bar thickness (px)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Thickness of the scale bar in pixels.

background-color.description: Parameter 'Background color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, the background of the bar

bar-color.description: Parameter 'Bar color' of type with ID 'color'. Color of the bar

draw-horizontal.description: Parameter 'Draw horizontal scale bar' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a horizontal scale bar is drawn

bar-size-horizontal.description: Parameter 'Bar size (horizontal)' of type with ID 'optional-quantity'. Size of the scale bar. If disabled, a calculated default value is used.

bar-size-vertical.description: Parameter 'Bar size (vertical)' of type with ID 'optional-quantity'. Size of the scale bar. If disabled, a calculated default value is used.

location.description: Parameter 'Location' of type with ID 'ij1-calibration-draw-scale-bar:location'. Location of the scale bar

text-settings/label-h.description: Parameter 'Label (horizontal)' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that determines the text to be rendered.

text-settings/label-v.description: Parameter 'Label (vertical)' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that determines the text to be rendered.

text-settings/hide-labels.description: Parameter 'Hide labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, all labels will be hidden

text-settings/text-font-size.description: Parameter 'Font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Size of the text

text-settings/font-style.description: Parameter 'Font style' of type with ID 'font-style'. The style of the font

text-settings/text-color.description: Parameter 'Text color' of type with ID 'color'. The color of the text

text-settings/text-font.description: Parameter 'Font' of type with ID 'font-family-enum'. Font of the text

chamfer-weights.description: Parameter 'Chamfer weights' of type with ID 'ij-math-chamfer-distance-map-2d:weights'. Determines the Chamfer weights for this distance transform

normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize weights' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Indicates whether the resulting distance map should be normalized (divide distances by the first chamfer weight)

output-type.description: Parameter 'Output type' of type with ID 'data-type'. Determines the output type. You can choose between an  16-bit or 32-bit.

If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

spot-tracker-parameters/max-frame-gap.description:
Parameter 'Max frame gap' of type with ID 'int'. This tracker is based on the Linear Assignment Problem mathematical framework.
Its implementation is adapted from the following paper:
Robust single-particle tracking in live-cell time-lapse sequences -
Jaqaman et al., 2008, Nature Methods.

Tracking happens in 2 steps: First spots are linked from frame to frame to
build track segments. These track segments are investigated in a second step
for gap-closing (missing detection), splitting and merging events.

Linking costs are proportional to the square distance between source and
target spots, which makes this tracker suitable for Brownian motion.
Penalties can be set to favor linking between spots that have similar
features.

Solving the LAP relies on the Jonker-Volgenant solver, and a sparse cost matrix formulation, allowing it to handle very large problems.

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clij2.plug.name: CLIJ2 Generate N Nearest Neighbors Matrix

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/n.description: Parameter 'N' of type with ID 'double'. null

series-name.description:
Parameter 'Series name' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to generate the series name This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

input-columns/Category.description: Parameter 'Category' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Categories displayed in the X axis. Must correspond to each value. (String column)

input-columns/Group.description: Parameter 'Group' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Groups to color the bars. Shown in the legend. Must correspond to each value. (String column)

expression.description: Parameter 'Expression' of type with ID 'expression'. The mathematical expression that is applied to each pixel position in the input images. Additionally to the positional variables, there are variables available that are named according to the input slots and contain the current pixel value of this slot.

weka-training-label-2d-v2.name: Train Weka model from labels (multiple images 2D)

label-data-annotation.description:
Parameter 'Label data annotation' of type with ID 'data-annotation-query-expression'. Determines which data annotation contains the labels. Please ensure that the appropriate label data is annotated to the raw input data. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

classifier-settings/classifier.description: Parameter 'Classifier' of type with ID 'weka-classifier'. The selected classifier

classifier-settings/balance-classes.description: Parameter 'Balance classes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. The classifier uses by the default all the user traces to train. By selecting this option, we filter first the classes in order to provide a balanced distribution of the samples. This implies that the less numerous classes will duplicate some of their samples and the more populated classes will lose some of their samples for the sake of even distribution. This option is strongly recommended if we want to give the same importance to all classes. An alternative is to use the Weka CostSensitiveClassifier and set a corresponding cost matrix.

feature-settings/use-neighbors.description: Parameter 'Use neighbors' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Set the use of the neighbor features

feature-settings/membrane-patch-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane patch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. this represents the size 𝑛×𝑛 of the field of view for the membrane projection filters. Only available for 2D features.

feature-settings/membrane-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane thickness' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Expected value of the membrane thickness, 1 pixel by default. The more accurate, the more precise the filter will be.

feature-settings/min-sigma.description: Parameter 'Minimum sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Minimum radius of the isotropic filters used to create the features. By default 1 pixel.

feature-settings/training-features.description:
Parameter 'Training features' of type with ID 'weka-feature-set-2d'. Here we can select and deselect the training features, which are the key of the learning procedure. The plugin creates a stack of images —one image for each feature. For instance, if only Gaussian blur is selected as a feature, the classifier will be trained on the original image and some blurred versions to it with different 𝜎 parameters for the Gaussian. If the input image is grayscale, the features will be calculated using double precision (32-bit images). In the case of RGB input images, the features will be RGB as well.
  • Gaussian blur: performs 𝑛 individual convolutions with Gaussian kernels with the normal 𝑛 variations of 𝜎. The larger the radius the more blurred the image becomes until the pixels are homogeneous.
  • Sobel filter: calculates an approximation of the gradient of the image intensity at each pixel. Gaussian blurs with 𝜎 varying as usual are performed prior to the filter.
  • Hessian: Calculates a Hessian matrix 𝐻 at each pixel. Prior to the application of any filters, a Gaussian blur with varying 𝜎 is performed.
  • Difference of gaussians: calculates two Gaussian blur images from the original image and subtracts one from the other. 𝜎 values are varied as usual, so 𝑛(𝑛−1)2 feature images are added to the stack.
  • Membrane projections: enhances membrane-like structures of the image through directional filtering. The initial kernel for this operation is hardcoded as a 19×19 zero matrix with the middle column entries set to 1. Multiple kernels are created by rotating the original kernel by 6 degrees up to a total rotation of 180 degrees, giving 30 kernels. Each kernel is convolved with the image and then the set of 30 images are Z-projected into a single image via 6 methods (sum of the pixels in each image, mean of the pixels in each image, standard deviation of the pixels in each image, median of the pixels in each image, maximum of the pixels in each image, minimum of the pixels in each image). Each of the 6 resulting images is a feature. Hence pixels in lines of similarly valued pixels in the image that are different from the average image intensity will stand out in the Z-projections.
  • Mean, Variance, Median, Minimum, Maximum: the pixels within a radius of 𝜎 pixels from the target pixel are subjected to the pertinent operation (mean/min etc.) and the target pixel is set to that value.
  • Anisotropic diffusion: the anisotropic diffusion filtering from Fiji with 20 iterations, 𝜎 smoothing per iterations, 𝑎1=0.10,0.35, 𝑎2=0.9, and an edge threshold set to the membrane size.
  • Bilateral filter: is very similar to the Mean filter but better preserves edges while averaging/blurring other parts of the image. The filter accomplishes this task by only averaging the values around the current pixel that are close in color value to the current pixel. The ‘closeness’ of other neighborhood pixels to the current pixels is determined by the specified threshold. I.e. for a value of 10 each pixel that contributes to the current mean have to be within 10 values of the current pixel. In our case, we combine spatial radius of 5 and 10, with a range radius of 50 and 100.
  • Lipschitz filter: from Mikulas Stencel plugin. This plugin implements Lipschitz cover of an image that is equivalent to a grayscale opening by a cone. The Lipschitz cover can be applied for the elimination of a slowly varying image background by subtraction of the lower Lipschitz cover (a top-hat procedure). A sequential double scan algorithm is used. We use down and top hats combination, with slope 𝑠=5,10,15,20,25.
  • Kuwahara filter: another noise-reduction filter that preserves edges. This is a version of the Kuwahara filter that uses linear kernels rather than square ones. We use the membrane patch size as kernel size, 30 angles and the three different criteria (Variance, Variance / Mean and Variance / Mean^2).
  • abor filter: at the moment this option may take some time and memory because it generates a very diverse range of Gabor filters (22). ’ This may undergo changes in the future’. The implementation details are included in this script. The Gabor filter is an edge detection and texture filter, which convolves several kernels at different angles with an image. Gabor filters are band-pass filters and therefore implement a frequency transformation.
  • Derivatives filter: calculates high order (4, 6, 8, 10) derivatives of the input image.
  • Laplacian filter: computes the Laplacian of the input image using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎.
  • Structure filter: calculates for all elements in the input image, the eigenvalues (smallest and largest) of the so-called structure tensor using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎 and integration scales 1 and 3.
  • Entropy: draws a circle of radius 𝑟 around each pixel; gets the histogram of that circle split in numBins chunks; then calculates the entropy as ∑𝑝 in histogram−𝑝∗log2(𝑝), where 𝑝 is the probability of each chunk in the histogram. numBins is equal to 32,64,128,256. 𝑟 is equal to 𝜎.
  • Neighbors: shifts the image in 8 directions by an certain number of pixel, 𝜎. Therefore creates 8𝑛 feature images.
More information: https://imagej.net/plugins/tws/

feature-settings/max-sigma.description: Parameter 'Maximum sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Maximum radius of the isotropic filters used to create the features. By default 16 pixels.

remove-column.description: Parameter 'Remove whole columns' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, a whole annotation column is removed if one of the values is an array.

annotation-name-filter.description:
Parameter 'Annotation column filter' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter for specific annotation columns. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

split.description: Parameter 'Split output' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each rectangle is put into its own ROI list

rectangles.description: Parameter 'Rectangles' of type with ID 'rectangle-list'. List of rectangles

ignore-missing-roi-indices.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing ROI indices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, missing ROI indices are ignored (a log message is written)

roi-index-column.description:
Parameter 'ROI index' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Determines how the table column is associated to the index of the ROI (zero-based). Defaults to assuming that the row index is the ROI index. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/radius_x.description: Parameter 'Radius X' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/radius_y.description: Parameter 'Radius Y' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/radius_z.description: Parameter 'Radius Z' of type with ID 'double'. null

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will train in 3D. Otherwise, JIPipe will prepare the data by splitting 3D data into planes.

label-data-annotation.description:
Parameter 'Label data annotation' of type with ID 'data-annotation-query-expression'. Determines which data annotation contains the labels. Please ensure that the appropriate label data is annotated to the raw input data. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

train-size-model.description: Parameter 'Train size model' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, also train a size model

concatenate-downsampled-layers.description: Parameter 'Concatenate downsampled layers' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Concatenate downsampled layers with upsampled layers (off by default which means they are added)

diameter.description:
Parameter 'Mean diameter' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The cell diameter. Depending on the model, you can choose following values:
  • Cytoplasm: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 30 pixels.
  • Nuclei: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 17 pixels.
  • Custom: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter appropriate for the model.
  • None: This will train from scratch. You can freely set the diameter. You also can set the diameter to 0 to disable scaling.

use-style-vector.description: Parameter 'Use style vector' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose is used.

weight-decay.description: Parameter 'Weight decay' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight decay

min-train-masks.description: Parameter 'Minimum number of labels per image' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Minimum number of masks an image must have to use in training set. This value is by default 5 in the original Cellpose tool.

use-residual-connections.description: Parameter 'Use residual connections' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

batch-size.description: Parameter 'Batch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

learning-rate.description: Parameter 'Learning rate' of type with ID 'primitive.double'.

pretrained-model.description:
Parameter 'Model' of type with ID 'cellpose-pretrained-model'. The pretrained model that should be used. You can either choose one of the models provided by Cellpose, a custom model, or train from scratch. The pre-trained model has influence on the diameter and how the input images should be prepared:
  • Cytoplasm: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 30 pixels.
  • Nuclei: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 17 pixels.
  • Custom: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter appropriate for the model. Don't forget to set the diameter value.
  • None: This will train from scratch. You can freely set the diameter. You also can set the diameter to 0 to disable scaling.
See https://cellpose.readthedocs.io/en/latest/models.html for a description of all models

nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

generate-connected-components.description: Parameter 'Generate connected components' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, JIPipe will apply a connected component labeling to the annotated masks. If disabled, Cellpose is provided with the labels as-is, which might result in issues with the training.

epochs.description: Parameter 'Epochs' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of epochs that should be trained.

output-parameters/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

output-parameters/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

remove-in-z.description: Parameter 'Check Z coordinate' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, check if the object's Z coordinate

remove-in-y.description: Parameter 'Check Y coordinate' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, check if the object's Y coordinate

remove-in-x.description: Parameter 'Check X coordinate' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, check if the object's X coordinate

border-distance.description: Parameter 'Border distance' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The maximum distance to the border (defaults to zero)

generated-annotation.description: Parameter 'Generated annotation name' of type with ID 'optional-string'. Optional. Annotation that is added to each individual ROI list. Contains the value index=[index];name=[name].

roi-name.description: Parameter 'Annotation value' of type with ID 'expression'. If an annotation is generated, sets the value

form-data/extensions.description: Parameter 'Extensions' of type with ID 'string-list'. Only for loading/saving files. List of extensions that are loaded/saved. The extensions are provided without the leading dot, e.g. jpg or tiff.

form-data/form:description.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. Description of the element displayed to the user.

form-data/form:show-name.description: Parameter 'Show name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the name of the form element is shown next to it.

form-data/form:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Name of the form element. Hidden if 'Show name' is disabled.

form-data/initial-value.description: Parameter 'Initial value' of type with ID 'path'. The initial string value

form-data/path-type.description: Parameter 'Path type' of type with ID 'path-type'. Allows to restrict the path type.

form-data/validation-expression.description:
Parameter 'Validation expression' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to validate the user input. There is a variable 'value' available that contains the tested string. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

form-data/io-mode.description: Parameter 'Path I/O mode' of type with ID 'path-io-mode'. Decides if the user sees an open or save dialog.

form-data/form:tabs/form:tab-annotation.description: Parameter 'Tab annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The annotation that contains the tab name.

form-data/form:tabs/form:tab.description: Parameter 'Tab' of type with ID 'string'. The tab where this form data will appear in.

form-data/form:io/output-annotation.description: Parameter 'Output annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Determines into which annotation the user input is written.

form-data/form:io/merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge output annotation' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how the output annotation is merged with existing values.

form-data/form:io/input-annotation.description: Parameter 'Input annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The annotation used to override the initial value. If the annotation does not exist, the standard initial value is used.

annotation-merging.description: Parameter 'Annotation merging' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how generated annotations are merged with existing annotations

annotation-width.description: Parameter 'Annotate with ROI width' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the width of the ROI

annotation-height.description: Parameter 'Annotate with ROI height' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the height of the ROI

roi.description: Parameter 'ROI' of type with ID 'margin'. Defines the area to crop.

annotation-y.description: Parameter 'Annotate with Y location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the top-left Y coordinate of the ROI bounding box.

annotation-x.description: Parameter 'Annotate with X location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the top-left X coordinate of the ROI bounding box.

clij-parameters/row_index.description: Parameter 'Row Index' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/value.description: Parameter 'Value' of type with ID 'double'. null

entries.description: Parameter 'Sort entries' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. Each entry contains an expression that should return a value that is compared across the data.

frames-size-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of frames (T)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains the number of frames (number of T slices).

image-type-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with image type' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains one of the following values: GRAY8, GRAY16, GRAY32, COLOR_256, COLOR_RGB, or UNKNOWN

stack-size-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with stack size (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains the image stack size (number of Z slices).

height-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with image height' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains image height.

color-space-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with color space' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the color space is stored in an annotation. If a greyscale image is provided, the color space will be 'Greyscale'.

image-plane-number-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of planes' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains the number of 2D image planes (also referred as 'Stack size'). Please note that this value might be different from the number of Z slices. This number is size(Z) * size(C) * size(T).

channel-size-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with channel size (C)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains the composite image channel size (number of C slices). Please note that is is different from the pixel channels like RGB.

physical-dimension-t-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with physical dimension (Time)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the time unit for multi-frame images is annotated to the image.

physical-dimension-value-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with physical dimension (Value)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the physical size of greyscale pixel values is annotated to the image.

width-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with image width' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains image width.

physical-dimension-x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with physical dimension (X)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the physical size of one pixel (including the unit) is annotated to the image.

physical-dimension-z-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with physical dimension (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the physical size of one pixel (including the unit) is annotated to the image.

title-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with title' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains the ImageJ image name.

image-bit-depth-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with bit depth' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains one of the following values: 0, 8, 16, 24 or 32

physical-dimension-y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with physical dimension (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the physical size of one pixel (including the unit) is annotated to the image.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

hough-array-nms-neighborhood-size.description: Parameter 'Neighborhood size (H)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Neighborhood size for the non-maximum suppression in the space of the hough array (the X axis containing the theta values, Y containing the rho values). Defaults to [width / 50, height / 50] with a minimum of 3 if not enabled. Must be positive odd integers.

roi-name-expression.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'expression'. The name of the generated line ROI is calculated from this expression

thetas.description: Parameter 'Thetas' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that generates the tested theta angles.

nms-algorithm.description: Parameter 'Non-maximum suppression' of type with ID 'ij1-hough-nms-algorithm'. Determines the algorithm used for the non-maximum suppression. The classic method erases peaks within the Hough array, which is fast, but may not always yield the expected results. Alternatively, detected lines can be erased within the input mask (slower, as the Hough array needs to be regenerated)

num-peaks.description: Parameter 'Number of peaks' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The maximum number of peaks/lines that will be detected.

peak-threshold.description: Parameter 'Peak threshold' of type with ID 'expression'. Threshold for the Hough array H peak detection. Defaults to 0.5 * MAX(H).

input-nms-neighborhood-size.description: Parameter 'Neighborhood size (I)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Neighborhood size for the non-maximum suppression on the input mask. Set to zero or lower to turn off.

min-length.description: Parameter 'Minimum length' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Minimum length of the underlying line structure

high-threshold.description: Parameter 'Hysteresis high threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Sets the high threshold for hysteresis. Suitable values for this parameter must be determined experimentally for each application. It is nonsensical (though not prohibited) for this value to be less than the low threshold value.

low-threshold.description: Parameter 'Hysteresis low threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Sets the low threshold for hysteresis. Suitable values for this parameter must be determined experimentally for each application. It is nonsensical (though not prohibited) for this value to exceed the high threshold value.

normalize-contrast.description: Parameter 'Normalize contrast' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Sets whether the contrast is normalized

gaussian-kernel-radius.description: Parameter 'Gaussian kernel radius' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Sets the radius of the Gaussian convolution kernel used to smooth the source image prior to gradient calculation. Must exceed 0.1

gaussian-kernel-width.description: Parameter 'Max gaussian kernel width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of pixels across which the Gaussian kernel is applied. This implementation will reduce the radius if the contribution of pixel values is deemed negligable, so this is actually a maximum radius. Must be at least 2.

output-empty-lists.description: Parameter 'Output empty lists' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will also output empty lists.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

filter.description: Parameter 'Keep ROI if' of type with ID 'expression'. Filtering expression. This is applied per ROI. Click the 'f' button to see all available variables you can test for (note: requires from you to enable the corresponding measurement!).An example for an expression would be 'Area > 200 AND Mean > 10'.Annotations are available as variables.

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. The measurements to calculate.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

slice-range.description:
Parameter 'Slice range' of type with ID 'integer-range'. Only the items within this range are copied into the output. The first index is zero. If a iteration step does not contain the requested index, no data is copied.

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

noise.description: Parameter 'Noise' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. All connected pixels with values above the value of (LocalMaxima - NoiseValue) are marked as zone of the local maximum

radius-z.description: Parameter 'Radius (Z)' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Radius in the Z direction

spot-detector-parameters/target-channel.description:
Parameter 'Target channel' of type with ID 'int'. This detector applies a LoG (Laplacian of Gaussian) filter
to the image, with a sigma suited to the blob estimated size.
Calculations are made in the Fourier space. The maxima in the
filtered image are searched for, and maxima too close from each
other are suppressed. A quadratic fitting scheme allows to do
sub-pixel localization.

sub-pixel localization.

silently-override.description: Parameter 'Silently override duplicate indices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, duplicate plane indices are ignored and silently overriden

new-location-expression.description: Parameter 'New slice location' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that returns an array with the new Z, channel, and frame location (zero-based) of the slice. If the expression returns a boolen TRUE, the original location is preserved. If the expression returns a boolean FALSE or an empty array, the slice is discarded. Locations do not need to be consecutive and positive (compensation by the algorithm). Duplicate locations are not supported and will be overwritten by one of the affected images.

The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

lines.description: Parameter 'ROI' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. List of ROI to be created

roi-properties/position-t.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Frame)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different frame/time-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all frames.

roi-properties/line-width.description: Parameter 'Line width' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Allows to change the line width when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/position-z.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Z)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different Z-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all slices.

roi-properties/roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to change the ROI name

roi-properties/fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the fill color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/line-color.description: Parameter 'Line color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the line color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/position-c.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Channel)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different channel-position. Please note that 'Channel' refers to an image slice and not to a pixel channel. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all channels.

filter.description: Parameter 'Remove ROI if' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed per ROI property and should return true if the property should be removed

order-expression.description: Parameter 'Order function' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that should return an ordered list of workbook sheets as array of strings. If a name is missing, the sheet is placed at the end of the list. If a string is returned, the sheet with the name is set as the first sheet.

relative-to-project-dir.description: Parameter 'Output relative to project directory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, outputs will be preferably generated relative to the project directory. Otherwise, JIPipe will store the results in an automatically generated directory. Has no effect if an absolute path is provided.

output-directory.description: Parameter 'Output directory' of type with ID 'path'. Can be a relative or absolute directory. All collected files will be put into this directory. If relative, it is relative to the output slot's output directory that is generated based on the current run's output path.

sheet-name-expression.description: Parameter 'Sheet name function' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that determines the name of the sheet. Please note that there are certain restrictions on the naming of sheets that are automatically enforced by JIPipe (see https://poi.apache.org/apidocs/dev/org/apache/poi/ss/usermodel/Workbook.html#createSheet--).

exporter/make-filesystem-compatible.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

exporter/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

exporter/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

filter.description: Parameter 'Keep track if' of type with ID 'expression'. The expression is executed per track. If it returns TRUE, the track is kept.

output-roi.description: Parameter 'Output ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Also outputs ROI that separate background from the original image.

expand-canvas.description: Parameter 'Expand canvas' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the canvas will be expanded, so it fits the rotated results.

background-color.description: Parameter 'Background color' of type with ID 'color'. The background color used if the canvas needs to be expanded.

anchor.description: Parameter 'Anchor' of type with ID 'anchor'. Anchor for the rotation. Please not that with the current implementation, only Center-Center will expand the canvas properly.

angle.description: Parameter 'Angle (in degrees)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Determines by which angle the image is rotated in clock-wise direction. To do counter-clockwise rotation, put in a negative angle.
