block-radius.description: Parameter 'Block radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Determines the local vicinity that is used to calculate the similarity of two pixels.

max-distance.description: Parameter 'Max distance' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Maximum search distance for a most similar pixel. The maximum value is 127 due to performance reasons.

relative-distances.description: Parameter 'Relative distances' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output radius or x/y are relative to the max distance.

output-polar-coordinates.description: Parameter 'Output polar coordinates' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output contains polar coordinates (channel 0 being the radius and channel 1 being phi). If disabled, the output contains cartesian coordinates.

add-last-identity-field.description: Parameter 'Add identity field at end' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output will contain as many planes as the input. The last slide will map to identity (zero image)

target-c.description: Parameter 'Move C to ...' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. Determines how the C (channel) dimension is re-mapped.

target-t.description: Parameter 'Move T to ...' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. Determines how the T (time) dimension is re-mapped.

target-z.description: Parameter 'Move Z to ...' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. Determines how the Z dimension is re-mapped.

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clijx.clij.name: Cli Jx Mode Of N Nearest Neighbors Map

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

values.description:
Parameter 'Label values' of type with ID 'integer-range'. The label values to be kept/to be removed

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

keep-values.description: Parameter 'Mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines if labels are removed or kept.

discard-existing-annotations.description: Parameter 'Replace all existing annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, existing annotations will not be carried over into the output.

table-merge-settings/annotation-merge-st.description: Parameter 'Merge same annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if different values for the same annotation columns are encountered.

table-merge-settings/column-matching.description: Parameter 'Grouping method' of type with ID 'column-matching'. Algorithms with multiple inputs require to match the incoming data to data sets. This allows you to determine how interesting data annotation columns are extracted from the incoming data. Union matches using the union of annotation columns. Intersection intersects the sets of available columns. You can also customize which columns should be included or excluded.

table-merge-settings/data-annotation-mer.description: Parameter 'Merge same data annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:data-annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if different values for the same data annotation columns are encountered.

table-merge-settings/custom-matched-colu.description:
Parameter 'Custom grouping columns' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Only used if 'Grouping method' is set to 'Custom'. Determines which annotation columns are referred to group data sets. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

table-merge-settings/skip-incomplete.description: Parameter 'Skip incomplete data sets' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, incomplete data sets are silently skipped. Otherwise an error is displayed if such a configuration is detected.

table-merge-settings/custom-annotation-m.description: Parameter 'Custom annotation matching method' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression used to compare two annotation sets.

table-merge-settings/limit.description: Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. Limits which iteration steps are generated. The first index is zero.

table-merge-settings/force-flow-graph-so.description: Parameter 'Force flow graph solver' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, disable the faster dictionary-based solver. Use this if you experience unexpected behavior.

table-merge-settings/annotation-matching.description: Parameter 'Annotation matching method' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-matching-method'. Allows to customize when two annotation sets are considered as equal. By default, non-empty annotation values should match exactly. You can also use a custom expression, instead.

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clijx.clij.name: Cli Jx Divide By Gaussian Background

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

drawer-settings/opacity.description: Parameter 'Opacity' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Opacity of the added ROI and labels. If zero, they are not visible. If set to one, they are fully visible.

annotation-c.description: Parameter 'Get C location' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, the channel location is extracted from the given annotation column. The first index is one. Zero indicates that the target is present in all C slices.

do-scaling.description: Parameter 'Apply scaling for greyscale conversions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the appropriate scaling is applied when converting between 16 and 8 bit images.

annotation-t.description: Parameter 'Get T location' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, the frame location is extracted from the given annotation column. The first index is one. Zero indicates that the target is present in all T slices.

annotation-y.description: Parameter 'Y location' of type with ID 'string'. If enabled, the Y location of each target image is extracted from its annotation. If you disable this, you can enable extraction of the width and center Y as alternative.

annotation-x.description: Parameter 'X location' of type with ID 'string'. If enabled, the X location of each target image is extracted from its annotations. If you disable this, you can enable extraction of the width and center X as alternative.

xy-anchor.description: Parameter 'X/Y location anchor' of type with ID 'anchor'. Determines how the X and Y locations are interpreted.

annotation-z.description: Parameter 'Get Z location' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, the Z location is extracted from the given annotation column. The first index is one. Zero indicates that the target is present in all Z slices.

relative-to-project-dir.description: Parameter 'Output relative to project directory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, outputs will be preferably generated relative to the project directory. Otherwise, JIPipe will store the results in an automatically generated directory. Has no effect if an absolute path is provided.

output-directory.description: Parameter 'Output directory' of type with ID 'path'. Can be a relative or absolute directory. All collected files will be put into this directory. If relative, it is relative to the output slot's output directory that is generated based on the current run's output path.

exporter/make-filesystem-compatible.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

exporter/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

exporter/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

filter-small-diameter.description: Parameter 'Small diameter (px)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Smaller structures are filtered up to the specified diameter

tolerance-of-direction-perc.description: Parameter 'Tolerance of direction (%)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Tolerance of direction. Use to calculate the sharpness of the filter.

suppress-stripes.description: Parameter 'Suppress stripes' of type with ID 'ij1-fft-bandpass2d:stripe-suppression'. If enabled, suppress stripes in the specified direction

do-scaling.description: Parameter 'Auto-scale after filtering' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. After filtering, apply automated scaling of values

saturate.description: Parameter 'Auto-scale saturates values' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. During auto-scaling, saturate pixel values

filter-large-diameter.description: Parameter 'Large diameter (px)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Larger structures are filtered down to the specified diameter

module-parameters/maxvalue.description: Parameter 'Max Value' of type with ID 'long'.

Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

project-validation-mode.description: Parameter 'Validate Ilastik project' of type with ID 'ilastik-project-validation-mode'. Determines how/if the node validates the input projects. This is done to check if the project is supported by this node.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Ilastik environment' of type with ID 'optional-ilastik-environment'. If enabled, a different Ilastik environment is used for this node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Ilastik is used.

output-parameters/output-probabilities.description: Parameter 'Output probabilities' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Exports a multi-channel image where pixel values represent the probability that that pixel belongs to the class represented by that channel

output-parameters/output-labels.description: Parameter 'Output labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Outputs an image representing the users’ manually created annotations

output-parameters/output-uncertainty.description: Parameter 'Output uncertainty' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Produces an image where pixel intensity is proportional to the uncertainty found when trying to classify that pixel

output-parameters/output-features.description: Parameter 'Output features' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Outputs a multi-channel image where each channel represents one of the computed pixel features

output-parameters/output-simple-segmenta.description: Parameter 'Output simple segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Produces a single-channel image where the (integer) pixel values indicate the class to which a pixel belongs. For this image, every pixel with the same value should belong to the same class of pixels

annotation-width.description: Parameter 'Annotate with ROI width' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the width of the ROI

annotation-height.description: Parameter 'Annotate with ROI height' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the height of the ROI

crop-c.description: Parameter 'Crop channel plane' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, images are cropped in the channel plane.

annotation-t.description: Parameter 'Annotate with T location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the frame slice of the ROI. The first index is 1. A value of zero indicates that the ROI is located on all T slices.

crop-z.description: Parameter 'Crop Z plane' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, images are cropped in the Z plane.

annotation-y.description: Parameter 'Annotate with Y location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the top-left Y coordinate of the ROI bounding box.

annotation-x.description: Parameter 'Annotate with X location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the top-left X coordinate of the ROI bounding box.

annotation-z.description: Parameter 'Annotate with Z location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the Z slice of the ROI. The first index is 1. A value of zero indicates that the ROI is located on all Z slices.

crop-t.description: Parameter 'Crop frame plane' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, images are cropped in the time plane.

crop-xy.description: Parameter 'Crop XY plane' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, images are cropped according to the boundaries in the XY plane.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

filters.description: Parameter 'Filters' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows you to select how to filter the values. Each row is iterated individually and its columns are available as variables inside the expression. For example there are columns 'Area' and 'X'. Then you can filter the table via an expression 'AREA > 100 AND X > 200 AND X < 1000'.Annotations are available as variables.

export-as-roi-file.description: Parameter 'Force exporting *.roi files' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the exporter will always export *.roi files and if necessary split the ROI list.

relative-to-project-dir.description: Parameter 'Output relative to project directory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, outputs will be preferably generated relative to the project directory. Otherwise, JIPipe will store the results in an automatically generated directory. Has no effect if an absolute path is provided.

output-directory.description: Parameter 'Output directory' of type with ID 'path'. Can be a relative or absolute directory. All collected files will be put into this directory. If relative, it is relative to the output slot's output directory that is generated based on the current run's output path.

exporter/make-filesystem-compatible.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

exporter/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

exporter/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

minimum-value.description: Parameter 'Min Voxel Number' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The minimum number of voxels

connectivity.description: Parameter 'Connectivity' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-3d'. The neighborhood connectivity

attribute.description: Parameter 'Attribute' of type with ID 'ij1-morph-grayscale-attribute-filtering-3d:attribute'. The attribute that is used for filtering

operation.description: Parameter 'Operation' of type with ID 'ij1-morph-grayscale-attribute-filtering-3d:operation'. The operation that should be applied

radius-x.description: Parameter 'Radius (X)' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Filter radius (pixels) in X direction. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

radius-z.description: Parameter 'Radius (Z)' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Filter radius (pixels) in Z direction. If zero or less, radius in X direction is automatically used instead. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

radius-y.description: Parameter 'Radius (Y)' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Filter radius (pixels) in Y direction. If zero or less, radius in X direction is automatically used instead. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

line-width.description: Parameter 'Line width' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the line width when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the ROI name

fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the fill color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The color can be a common color name (e.g. 'red'), a hex color (e.g. '#ff00ff'), or an RGB color (e.g. '0,128,255').

line-color.description: Parameter 'Line color' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the line color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The color can be a common color name (e.g. 'red'), a hex color (e.g. '#ff00ff'), or an RGB color (e.g. '0,128,255').

clij-parameters/radius_z.description: Parameter 'Radius Z' of type with ID 'double'. null

spot-tracker-parameters/kalman-search-ra.description:
Parameter 'Kalman search radius' of type with ID 'double'. This tracker is best suited for objects that move with a roughly constant velocity vector.

It relies on the Kalman filter to predict the next most likely position of a spot. The predictions for all current tracks are linked to the spots actually found in the next frame, thanks to the LAP framework already present in the LAP tracker. Predictions are continuously refined and the tracker can accommodate moderate velocity direction and magnitude changes.

This tracker can bridge gaps: If a spot is not found close enough to a prediction, then the Kalman filter will make another prediction in the next frame and re-iterate the search.

The first frames of a track are critical for this tracker to work properly: Tracksare initiated by looking for close neighbors (again via the LAP tracker). Spurious spots in the beginning of each track can confuse the tracker.

This tracker needs two parameters (on top of the maximal frame gap tolerated):
- the max search radius defines how far from a predicted position it should look for candidate spots;
- the initial search radius defines how far two spots can be apart when initiating a new track.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/flip_y.description: Parameter 'Flip Y' of type with ID 'boolean'. null

frames-size-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of frames (T)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains the number of frames (number of T slices).

image-type-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with image type' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains one of the following values: GRAY8, GRAY16, GRAY32, COLOR_256, COLOR_RGB, or UNKNOWN

stack-size-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with stack size (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains the image stack size (number of Z slices).

height-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with image height' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains image height.

color-space-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with color space' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the color space is stored in an annotation. If a greyscale image is provided, the color space will be 'Greyscale'.

image-plane-number-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of planes' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains the number of 2D image planes (also referred as 'Stack size'). Please note that this value might be different from the number of Z slices. This number is size(Z) * size(C) * size(T).

channel-size-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with channel size (C)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains the composite image channel size (number of C slices). Please note that is is different from the pixel channels like RGB.

physical-dimension-t-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with physical dimension (Time)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the time unit for multi-frame images is annotated to the image.

physical-dimension-value-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with physical dimension (Value)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the physical size of greyscale pixel values is annotated to the image.

width-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with image width' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains image width.

physical-dimension-x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with physical dimension (X)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the physical size of one pixel (including the unit) is annotated to the image.

physical-dimension-z-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with physical dimension (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the physical size of one pixel (including the unit) is annotated to the image.

title-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with title' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains the ImageJ image name.

image-bit-depth-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with bit depth' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with provided name is created. The annotation contains one of the following values: 0, 8, 16, 24 or 32

physical-dimension-y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with physical dimension (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the physical size of one pixel (including the unit) is annotated to the image.

file-format.description:
Parameter 'File format' of type with ID 'ij1-export-image-to-web:file-format'. The file format that should be used.
  • PNG, JPEG, BMP: 2D images only
  • AVI: 2D or 3D images only
  • TIFF: All images supported

movie-animated-dimension.description: Parameter 'Animated dimension' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. Only used if the format is AVI. Determines which dimension is animated

movie-fps.description: Parameter 'Animation FPS' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Only used if the format is AVI. The number of frames shown per second.

avi-compression.description: Parameter 'AVI compression' of type with ID 'avi-compression'. Only used if the format is AVI. Determines how the frames are compressed.

file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

jpeg-quality.description: Parameter 'AVI JPEG quality' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Only used if the format is AVI and the compression is JPEG. Value from 0-100 that determines the JPEG quality (100 is best).

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

column-x1.description:
Parameter 'Column 'X1'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the X1 coordinate. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-y2.description:
Parameter 'Column 'Y2'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the Y2 coordinate. The usage of this column depends on the current 'Anchor' setting. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-t.description:

For zero-based positions the value is -1 or lower. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.

one-based-positions.description: Parameter 'Use one-based positions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the first slice is 1. Otherwise, the first slice is zero.

overlap-filter-measurements.description:
Parameter 'Overlap filter measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. Measurements extracted for the overlap filter.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

labels2/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, labels are stored into the output if they do not overlap.

labels2/overlap-filter.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter' of type with ID 'expression'. This filter is applied to any combination of Labels that have an overlap. You will have three sets of measurements: Labels1, Overlap, and Labels2.'Labels1'and 'Labels2' correspond to a Labels from the input slots,respectively. 'Overlap' is the overlap between these labels. Please open the expression builder to see a list of all available variables. If the filter is empty, no filtering is applied. Please note that writing a custom expression into this field requires that statistics are obtained from labels and overlapping regions, which has a significant impact on the performance.

labels2/output-overlaps.description: Parameter 'Output overlapping regions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the overlapping regions, instead of the Labels are extracted.

labels2/enforce-overlap.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter: enforce overlap' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a pair of labels is not considered for custom filtering if it does not overlap. Disable this setting if you want to implement special behavior.

labels2/enabled.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. You can use this setting to disable generating this output.

labels2/measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available. Measurements will be in the physical sizes of the respective images. The overlap is measured in the targeted labels' calibration.

labels1/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, labels are stored into the output if they do not overlap.

labels1/overlap-filter.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter' of type with ID 'expression'. This filter is applied to any combination of Labels that have an overlap. Please open the expression builder to see a list of all available variables. If the filter is empty, no filtering is applied. Please note that writing a custom expression into this field requires that statistics are obtained from labels and overlapping regions, which has a significant impact on the performance.

labels1/output-overlaps.description: Parameter 'Output overlapping regions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the overlapping regions, instead of the Labels are extracted.

labels1/enforce-overlap.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter: enforce overlap' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a pair of labels is not considered for custom filtering if it does not overlap. Disable this setting if you want to implement special behavior.

labels1/enabled.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. You can use this setting to disable generating this output.

labels1/measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available. Measurements will be in the physical sizes of the respective images. The overlap is measured in the targeted labels' calibration.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

override-radius.description: Parameter 'Override radius' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, the radius of newly created vertices is set manually. Otherwise it is the average of the two target vertices.

maximum-length.description: Parameter 'Split if longer than ...' of type with ID 'optional-quantity'. If enabled, only edges longer than the specified length are split

max-num-iterations.description: Parameter 'Maximum iterations' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. The maximum number of split iterations

add-metadata.description: Parameter 'Add metadata to created vertices' of type with ID 'string:string:pair-list'. Allows to add metadata to newly created vertices

override-value.description: Parameter 'Override value' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, the value if the newly created vertices is set manually. Otherwise it is the average of the two target vertices.

parameter-table.description: Parameter 'Parameters' of type with ID 'parameter-table'. Parameters that are generated

position-t.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Frame)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different frame/time-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all frames.

line-width.description: Parameter 'Line width' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Allows to change the line width when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

position-x.description: Parameter 'Location (X)' of type with ID 'optional-double'. The X location

position-y.description: Parameter 'Location (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-double'. The Y location

position-z.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different Z-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all slices.

position-c.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Channel)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different channel-position. Please note that 'Channel' refers to an image slice and not to a pixel channel. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all channels.

scale-x.description: Parameter 'Scale X' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Scales the ROI. Please note that the scale will not be saved inside the ROI. Must be a number.

scale-y.description: Parameter 'Scale Y' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Scales the ROI. Please note that the scale will not be saved inside the ROI. Must be a number.

roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'optional-string'. Allows to change the ROI name

center-scale.description: Parameter 'Center scale' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, each ROI is scaled relative to its center.

fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the fill color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

line-color.description: Parameter 'Line color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the line color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

clij-parameters/radiusY.description: Parameter 'Radius Y' of type with ID 'double'. null

bio-formats-settings/split-time-points.description: Parameter 'Split time points' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Timelapse data will be opened as a separate stack for each timepoint.

bio-formats-settings/stitch-tiles.description: Parameter 'Stitch tiles' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Stitch tiles - Performs very simple stitching of tiles.  The overlap is assumed to be 0%, and the stage coordinates are used to determine the proper placement of the tiles. This is useful for seeing a quick preview of what the stitched image might look like, but is not a substitute for proper stitching plugins such as the 2D/3D Stitching plugin.

bio-formats-settings/concatenate.description: Parameter 'Concatenate compatible series' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Allows multiple image series to be joined end to end.  Example: You want to join two sequential timelapse series.

bio-formats-settings/stack-order.description: Parameter 'Stack order' of type with ID 'import-imagej-bioformats:order'.

bio-formats-settings/split-channels.description: Parameter 'Split channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Each channel is opened as a separate stack.  This option is especially useful if you want to merge the channels into a specific order, rather than automatically assign channels to the order of RGB. The bit depth is preserved.

bio-formats-settings/color-mode.description:
Parameter 'Color mode' of type with ID 'import-imagej-bioformats:color-mode'. Color mode - Visualizes channels according to the specified scheme.  Possible choices are:
Default - Display channels as closely as possible to how they are stored in the file.
Composite - Open as a merged composite image. Channels are colorized according to metadata present in the dataset (if any), or in the following default order: 1=red, 2=green, 3=blue, 4=gray, 5=cyan, 6=magenta, 7=yellow.
Colorized - Open with each channel in a separate plane, colorized according to metadata present in the dataset (if any), or in the default order (see Composite above).
Grayscale - Open with each channel in a separate plane, displayed in plain grayscale.
Custom - Same as Colorized, except that you can explicitly choose how to colorize each channel.
Note that ImageJ can only composite together 7 or fewer channels. With more than 7 channels, some of the modes above may not work.

bio-formats-settings/split-focal-planes.description: Parameter 'Split focal planes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Each focal plane is opened as a separate stack.

bio-formats-settings/swap-dimensions.description: Parameter 'Swap dimensions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Allows reassignment of dimensional axes (e.g., channel, Z and time).  Bio-Formats is supposed to be smart about handling multidimensional image data, but in some cases gets things wrong. For example, when stitching together a dataset from multiple files using the Group files with similar names option, Bio-Formats may not know which dimensional axis the file numbering is supposed to represent. It will take a guess, but in case it guesses wrong, you can use Swap dimensions to reassign which dimensions are which.

bio-formats-settings/crop.description: Parameter 'Crop images' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Image planes may be cropped during import to conserve memory. Use the 'Crop regions' parameter to define which regions should be cropped.

bio-formats-settings/auto-scale.description: Parameter 'Auto scale' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Stretches the channel histograms to each channel's global minimum and maximum value throughout the stack. Does not alter underlying values in the image.  If unselected, all channel histograms are scaled to the image's digitization bit depth. Note that this range may be narrower than the bit depth of the file. For example, a 16-bit file may contain intensities digitized to 16-bits (0-65,535), to 12-bits (0-4,095), to 10-bits (0-1,023), etc.  Note that you can use the Brightness & Contrast or Window/Level controls to adjust the contrast range regardless of whether this option is used. The histogram will provide min/max values in the stack.

column-x1.description:
Parameter 'Column 'X'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the X coordinate. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-t.description:
Parameter 'Column 'T'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Table column that determines the frame location. For one-based positions, 0 indicates that the ROI is present in all frame-slices. For zero-based positions the value is -1 or lower. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

one-based-positions.description: Parameter 'Use one-based positions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the first slice is 1. Otherwise, the first slice is zero.

column-metadata.description:

Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

metadata-generators.description: Parameter 'Generated metadata' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. Each entry contains an expression that is applied for each ROI. The generated value is written into the metadata key. Please note that ImageJ ROI metadata are subject to some limitations. For example, keys cannot have space characters, equal signs, and colons.

value.name: Value
value.description: Expression that generates the value. This is applied per ROI. Click the 'f' button to see all available variables you can test for (note: requires from you to enable the corresponding measurement!).An example for an expression would be 'Area'.Annotations are available as variables.

clear-before-write.description: Parameter 'Clear properties before write' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, all existing ROI properties are deleted before writing the new properties

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. The measurements to calculate.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

clij-parameters/constant.description: Parameter 'Constant' of type with ID 'double'. null

file-name-generator.description:
Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the file name for the OME TIFF to be uploaded. Please note that the directory will be ignored and 'unnamed' will be assumed if no name is provided. This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

tag-exporter/tag-list-annotation.description: Parameter 'Tag list annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, extract all tag names from a single annotation that contains a JSON-serialized list of names. If no JSON data is found, the whole annotation is converted into a tag. Nested JSON arrays are flattened. Numbers are converted into strings. JSON objects are ignored.

filters.description:
Parameter 'Filters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Filter expression that is used to find columns to be removed. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"
