annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge same annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if an annotation already exists.

generated-annotation.description: Parameter 'Generated annotation' of type with ID 'string'. Select which annotation type is generated for each data row

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will segment in 3D. Otherwise, any 3D image will be processed per-slice. Please note that 3D segmentation requires large amounts of memory.

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Cellpose operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

diameter-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with diameter' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the diameter is attached as annotation. Useful if you want to let Cellpose estimate the object diameters.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description: Parameter 'Average object diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, Cellpose will use the provided average diameter to find objects. Otherwise, Cellpose will estimate the diameter by itself.

enable-multichannel.description: Parameter 'Enable multichannel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, multiple image channels are passed to Cellpose. Otherwise, each channel will be processed individually.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose is used.

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-parameters/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-parameters/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

output-parameters/output-probabilities.description: Parameter 'Output probabilities' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel

output-parameters/output-roi.description: Parameter 'Output 2D ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output a ROI list containing all detected objects

output-parameters/output-labels.description: Parameter 'Output labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output an image that contains a unique greyscale value for each detected object.

output-parameters/output-flows-xy.description: Parameter 'Output flows XY' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel

output-parameters/output-flows-z.description: Parameter 'Output flows Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)

output-parameters/output-flows-d.description: Parameter 'Output flows d' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)

enhancement-parameters/normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Normalize data so 0.0=1st percentile and 1.0=99th percentile of image intensities in each channel

enhancement-parameters/anisotropy.description: Parameter 'Anisotropy (3D)' of type with ID 'optional-double'. For 3D segmentation, optional rescaling factor (e.g. set to 2.0 if Z is sampled half as dense as X or Y)

enhancement-parameters/interpolate.description: Parameter 'Interpolate (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Interpolate during 2D dynamics (not available in 3D)

enhancement-parameters/no-resample.description: Parameter 'Disable resample' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Disable dynamics on full image (makes algorithm faster for images with large diameters)

enhancement-parameters/net-average.description: Parameter 'Average all networks' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Runs the 4 built-in networks and averages them if True, runs one network if disabled

threshold-parameters/stitch-threshold.description: Parameter 'Stitch threshold' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Compute masks in 2D then stitch together masks with IoU>0.9 across planes

threshold-parameters/cell-probability-th.description: Parameter 'Cell probability threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Cellprob threshold, default is 0, decrease to find more and larger masks

threshold-parameters/flow-threshold.description: Parameter 'Flow threshold (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Flow error threshold, 0 turns off this optional QC step. Default: 0.4

threshold-parameters/exclude-on-edges.description: Parameter 'Exclude masks on edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Discard masks which touch edges of image

rectangles.description: Parameter 'Rectangles' of type with ID 'margin-list'. List of rectangles

module-parameters/isfull.description: Parameter 'Is Full' of type with ID 'boolean'.

projected-axis.description: Parameter 'Projected axis' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. The axis that should be projected

method.description: Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'ij1-dimensions-zproject:method'. The function that is applied to each stack of pixels.

restrict-to-indices.description: Parameter 'Restrict to indices' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. If enabled, allows to restrict to specific projected indices

delete-masked-labels.description: Parameter 'Operation on masked labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Labels that overlap with a masked pixel (value larger than zero) can be either kept or deleted.

expression.description:
Parameter 'Expression' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. The expression is executed per ROI. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. Measurements to be included. Please open the expression builder to see the variables generated by the measurements.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

column-filter.description:
Parameter 'Column filter' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter the columns for their name. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

row-normalization.description: Parameter 'Row normalization' of type with ID 'table-column-row-normalization'. Determines how missing column values are handled if the input tables have different numbers of rows. You can set it to zero/empty (depending on numeric or string type), to the row number (starting with zero), copy the last value, or cycle.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

slice-threshold-mode.description:
Parameter 'Multi-slice thresholding' of type with ID 'slice-threshold-mode'. Determines how thresholds are calculated if an image has multiple slices.
  • Apply threshold per slice calculates and applies the threshold for each slice.
  • Combine slice statistics calculates statistics for all pixels in the image.
  • Combine thresholds per slice calculates the threshold for each slice. One threshold for all slices is calculated via another math expression.

access-pixels.description: Parameter 'Pixels are available as variables' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the list of all pixels that are considered for the statistics are available as variable 'pixels'. Please note that this will slow down the performance due to necessary conversions.

threshold-annotation-strategy.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

source-area.description: Parameter 'Calculate threshold based on ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines from which image areas the pixel values used for calculating the thresholds are extracted from.

min-threshold/threshold-combine-expressi.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

min-threshold/threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

min-threshold/thresholding-function.description: Parameter 'Thresholding function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed for each set of slices. The expression should return a number that will be used as threshold. A pixel is set to 255 if its value is larger than this threshold.

max-threshold/threshold-combine-expressi.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

max-threshold/threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

max-threshold/thresholding-function.description: Parameter 'Thresholding function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed for each set of slices. The expression should return a number that will be used as threshold. A pixel is set to 255 if its value is larger than this threshold.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius (pixel)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'.

series-name.description:
Parameter 'Series name' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to generate the series name This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

input-columns/Amount.description: Parameter 'Amount' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The values to be displayed (Numeric column)

input-columns/Category.description: Parameter 'Category' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The categories to be displayed (String column)

plot-parameters/use-custom-color-map.description: Parameter 'Use custom color map' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, you can define a custom color map

plot-parameters/background-color.description: Parameter 'Background color' of type with ID 'color'. Background color of the plot area.

plot-parameters/export-height.description: Parameter 'Export height' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Height of the output image generated via an export

plot-parameters/color-map.description: Parameter 'Color map' of type with ID 'plot-color-map'. Determines how elements are colored

plot-parameters/title-font-size.description: Parameter 'Title font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Font size of the title

plot-parameters/label-font-size.description: Parameter 'Label font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Font size of the pie chart labels

plot-parameters/export-width.description: Parameter 'Export width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Width of the output image generated via an export

plot-parameters/custom-color-map.description: Parameter 'Custom color map' of type with ID 'color-list'. Add colors into this list to define a custom color map

plot-parameters/with-legend.description: Parameter 'Show legend' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a legend is shown.

plot-parameters/title.description: Parameter 'Title' of type with ID 'string'. The title of this plot.

plot-parameters/legend-font-size.description: Parameter 'Legend font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The font size of legends

plot-parameters/grid-color.description: Parameter 'Grid color' of type with ID 'color'. Color of the grid

apply-per-frame.description: Parameter 'Apply per frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each frame separately. If false, all frames are put together.

columns.description: Parameter 'Generated columns' of type with ID 'table-column-expression-generator-column-processor-list'. Use these expressions to generate the table columns. The expressions contain statistics, as well as incoming annotations of the current image.

roi:target-area.description: Parameter 'Get statistics from ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines where the algorithm is applied to.

apply-per-channel.description: Parameter 'Apply per channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each channel-slice separately. If false, all channel-slices are put together. Please note that 'Channel' does not refer to a pixel channel like Red in RGB.

apply-per-slice.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the operation is applied for each Z-slice separately. If false, all Z-slices are put together.

sub-path.description: Parameter 'Concatenated path' of type with ID 'path'. The path is concatenated by this path.

code.description: Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'. All annotations are passed as dictionary 'annotations' that can be modified using Python functions. The values are then extracted and converted into their respective JIPipe types.

form-data/form:description.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. Description of the element displayed to the user.

form-data/form:show-name.description: Parameter 'Show name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the name of the form element is shown next to it.

form-data/form:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Name of the form element. Hidden if 'Show name' is disabled.

form-data/initial-value.description: Parameter 'Initial value' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The initial value

form-data/validation-expression.description: Parameter 'Validation expression' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that is used to validate the user input. There is a variable 'value' available that tests the current number.

form-data/form:tabs/form:tab-annotation.description: Parameter 'Tab annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The annotation that contains the tab name.

form-data/form:tabs/form:tab.description: Parameter 'Tab' of type with ID 'string'. The tab where this form data will appear in.

form-data/form:io/output-annotation.description: Parameter 'Output annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Determines into which annotation the user input is written.

form-data/form:io/merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge output annotation' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how the output annotation is merged with existing values.

form-data/form:io/input-annotation.description: Parameter 'Input annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The annotation used to override the initial value. If the annotation does not exist, the standard initial value is used.

form-data/form:description.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. Description of the element displayed to the user.

form-data/form:show-name.description: Parameter 'Show name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the name of the form element is shown next to it.

form-data/form:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Name of the form element. Hidden if 'Show name' is disabled.

form-data/initial-value.description: Parameter 'Initial value' of type with ID 'string'. The initial string value

form-data/validation-expression.description:
Parameter 'Validation expression' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to validate the user input. There is a variable 'value' available that contains the tested string. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

form-data/form:tabs/form:tab-annotation.description: Parameter 'Tab annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The annotation that contains the tab name.

form-data/form:tabs/form:tab.description: Parameter 'Tab' of type with ID 'string'. The tab where this form data will appear in.

form-data/form:io/output-annotation.description: Parameter 'Output annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Determines into which annotation the user input is written.

form-data/form:io/merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge output annotation' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how the output annotation is merged with existing values.

form-data/form:io/input-annotation.description: Parameter 'Input annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The annotation used to override the initial value. If the annotation does not exist, the standard initial value is used.

processors.description: Parameter 'Processors' of type with ID 'expression-table-column-processor-list'. Defines which columns are processed

append.description: Parameter 'Append to existing table' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the converted columns are appended to the existing table. Existing columns are overwritten.

force-2d.description: Parameter 'Force 2D' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each 2D image slice is processed individually.

z-order.description: Parameter 'z-Order' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The differentiation order (up tp 10) in the z-dimension. Order 0 in any dimension implies that only smoothing is applied in that dimension. The differentiation operation is carried out for each time frame and channel in a 5D image.

isotropic-gaussian.description: Parameter 'Isotropic Gaussian smoothing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use the voxel width, height, and depth specified in the image properties (calibration). That is, each dimension is assigned its own smoothing scale, computed by dividing the scale as specified in the plugin dialog by the specified pixel/voxel size for that dimension.

y-order.description: Parameter 'y-Order' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The differentiation order (up tp 10) in the y-dimension. Order 0 in any dimension implies that only smoothing is applied in that dimension. The differentiation operation is carried out for each time frame and channel in a 5D image.

x-order.description: Parameter 'x-Order' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The differentiation order (up tp 10) in the x-dimension. Order 0 in any dimension implies that only smoothing is applied in that dimension. The differentiation operation is carried out for each time frame and channel in a 5D image.

smoothing-scale.description: Parameter 'Smoothing scale' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel. Must be larger than zero.

Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

ij2:op:transform.permuteCoordinatesInver.name: IJ2: Permute Coordinate Inverse View Of Dimension

selected-column.description:
Parameter 'Column to convert' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The column to be converted into an image This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

code.description: Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'. All annotations are passed as dictionary 'annotations' that can be modified using Python functions. The values are then extracted and converted into their respective JIPipe types. The target slot is extracted from a variable 'output_slot' that should be present within the script. If the variable is set to null or empty, the data is discarded.

value.code:
# This script is executed for each row
# Annotations are passed as dictionary 'annotations'
# Modifications are copied into JIPipe
# The output slot is determined by a variable 'output_slot'

output_slot = "lhs" if "condition" in annotation["sample"] else "rhs"

file-format.description:
Parameter 'File format' of type with ID 'import-imagej-results-table-from-file:file-format'. Allows to set the file format. If set to 'Auto', the format is detected from the file extension.
  • CSV: Comma separated values
  • TSV: Tab separated values (variant of CSV with tabs instead of commas)
  • XLSX: Excel table (please ensure that the first row is a heading and that the table begins at A1)

sheet-name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with sheet name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the tables are annotated with the sheet names. No annotation is generated for file formats without sheets (e.g., CSV).

sheets.description: Parameter 'Restrict to sheets' of type with ID 'string-list'. If the file contains multiple tables, specifies which sheets should be imported. If empty, all sheets are imported

ignore-missing-sheets.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing sheets' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, missing sheets are ignored. Otherwise an error is raised if a sheet is missing.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/radiusX.description: Parameter 'Radius X' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/radiusY.description: Parameter 'Radius Y' of type with ID 'double'. null

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Radius of the filter kernel in pixels.

operation.description:
Parameter 'Operation' of type with ID 'ij1-morph:operation'. The morphological operation. Following operations are supported:
  • Erosion: A local minimum filter
  • Dilation: A local maximum filter
  • Opening: Erosion, followed by dilation
  • Closing: Dilation, followed by erosion
  • White Top Hat: Image - Opening
  • Black Top Hat (Black Hat): Closing - Image
  • Gradient: Dilation - Erosion
  • Laplacian: (Dilation + Erosion) / 2 - Image
  • Internal gradient: Image - Erosion
  • External gradient: Dilation - Image

element.description: Parameter 'Structure element' of type with ID 'ij1-morph:strel-3d'. The structure element.

clij-parameters/num_iterations.description: Parameter 'Num Iterations' of type with ID 'double'. null

restore-annotations.description: Parameter 'Restore original annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, original annotations that were not changed by the form processor will be restored in the output data. Otherwise, merged annotation values from the iteration step are used.

tab-annotation.description: Parameter 'Form tab annotation' of type with ID 'string'. The annotation that is used to group form elements into tabs.

output-columns.description: Parameter 'Output columns' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. Output columns to be calculated.

weight-column.description:
Parameter 'Weight column' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Column containing the weight value for each value This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

value-filter.description: Parameter 'Value filter' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to remove values before they are included into the histogram calculations

input-column.description:
Parameter 'Input column' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The column to be used for creating a histogram This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

num-bins.description: Parameter 'Number of bins' of type with ID 'expression'. The number of bins. If set to infinite (inf), there will be as many bins as unique values

grid-size.description: Parameter 'Grid size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The grid size

outputs/output-coherency.description: Parameter 'Output coherency' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

structure-tensor/local-window-sigma.description: Parameter 'Local window σ' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The structure tensor computes the orientation and isotropy properties in a local window. Here, the local window is characterized by a 2D Gaussian function of standard deviation σ. The parameter σ (expressed in pixel unit) is a critical parameter that determines the scale of the analysis. It should have a value roughly close to the structure of interest (e.g. thickness of the filament)

structure-tensor/gradient.description:
Parameter 'Gradient' of type with ID 'ij1-orientationj-gradient'. OrientationJ has different methods to compute the gradient.
  • The cubic spline is always the best choice, fast, accurate, quasi-isotropic and less boundary artefact
  • The finite difference gradient is very fast but it has poor isotropy properties
  • The Fourier gradient is exact but it has periodic boundary conditions.

sigma.description: Parameter 'Sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Determines the local vicinity that is used to calculate the similarity of two pixels.

max-distance.description: Parameter 'Max distance' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Maximum search distance for a most similar pixel. The maximum value is 127 due to performance reasons.

relative-distances.description: Parameter 'Relative distances' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output radius or x/y are relative to the max distance.

output-polar-coordinates.description: Parameter 'Output polar coordinates' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output contains polar coordinates (channel 0 being the radius and channel 1 being phi). If disabled, the output contains cartesian coordinates.

add-last-identity-field.description: Parameter 'Add identity field at end' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output will contain as many planes as the input. The last slide will map to identity (zero image)

plot-type.description: Parameter 'Plot type' of type with ID 'data-type'. The type of plot to be generated.

series-name.description:
Parameter 'Series name' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to generate the series name This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

label-index.description: Parameter 'Label index' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate the resulting images with the label index

ij1-math-generate-vector-from-expression.name: Generate vector field from math expression

component-dimension.description: Parameter 'Component dimension' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. Determines which dimension (Z, C, or T) contains the vector components.

size-z.description: Parameter 'Number of slices (Z)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of generated Z slices.

size-t.description: Parameter 'Number of frames (T)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of generated frame slices.

function.description: Parameter 'Function' of type with ID 'expression'. Generates a vector. Must return an array of numbers with the required vector components. If the vector has one component, it can also return a number.

remove-extensions.description: Parameter 'Remove file extensions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If a path is a file, remove its extension. The extension is the substring starting with the last dot. Unix dot-files (that start with a dot) are ignored. Ignores files that have no extension.

full-path.description: Parameter 'Annotate with full path' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the full path is put into the annotation. Otherwise, only the file or folder name is stored.

generated-annotation.description: Parameter 'Generated annotation' of type with ID 'string'. Select which annotation type is generated for each path

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

background-color.description: Parameter 'Background color' of type with ID 'color'. The color of the outside canvas

y-axis.description: Parameter 'Y axis' of type with ID 'int-modification'. Defines the size of the output canvas

anchor.description: Parameter 'Anchor' of type with ID 'anchor'. From which point to expand the canvas

x-axis.description: Parameter 'X axis' of type with ID 'int-modification'. Defines the size of the output canvas

clij-parameters/flip_x.description: Parameter 'Flip X' of type with ID 'boolean'. null

clij-parameters/flip_y.description: Parameter 'Flip Y' of type with ID 'boolean'. null

clij-parameters/flip_z.description: Parameter 'Flip Z' of type with ID 'boolean'. null

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will segment in 3D. Otherwise, any 3D image will be processed per-slice. Please note that 3D segmentation requires large amounts of memory.

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Python operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

diameter-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with diameter' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the diameter is attached as annotation. Useful if you want to let Cellpose estimate the object diameters.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description: Parameter 'Average object diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, Cellpose will use the provided average diameter to find objects. Otherwise, Cellpose will estimate the diameter by itself.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose is used.

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-parameters/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-parameters/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

output-parameters/output-probabilities.description: Parameter 'Output probabilities' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel

output-parameters/output-roi.description: Parameter 'Output 2D ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output a ROI list containing all detected objects

output-parameters/output-labels.description: Parameter 'Output labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output an image that contains a unique greyscale value for each detected object.

output-parameters/output-flows-xy.description: Parameter 'Output flows XY' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel

output-parameters/output-flows-z.description: Parameter 'Output flows Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)

output-parameters/output-flows-d.description: Parameter 'Output flows d' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)

enhancement-parameters/cluster.description: Parameter 'Cluster' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. DBSCAN clustering. Reduces oversegmentation of thin features

enhancement-parameters/fast-mode.description: Parameter 'Fast mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. disable dynamics on full image (makes algorithm faster for images with large diameters)

enhancement-parameters/anisotropy.description: Parameter 'Anisotropy (3D)' of type with ID 'optional-double'. For 3D segmentation, optional rescaling factor (e.g. set to 2.0 if Z is sampled half as dense as X or Y)

enhancement-parameters/interpolate.description: Parameter 'Interpolate (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Interpolate during 2D dynamics (not available in 3D)

enhancement-parameters/no-resample.description: Parameter 'Disable resample' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Disable dynamics on full image (makes algorithm faster for images with large diameters)

enhancement-parameters/net-average.description: Parameter 'Average all networks' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Runs the 4 built-in networks and averages them if True, runs one network if disabled

threshold-parameters/stitch-threshold.description: Parameter 'Stitch threshold' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Compute masks in 2D then stitch together masks with IoU>0.9 across planes

threshold-parameters/diam-threshold.description: Parameter 'Cell diameter threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Cell diameter threshold for upscaling before mask reconstruction, default 12.

threshold-parameters/mask-threshold.description: Parameter 'Mask threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Mask threshold, default is 0, decrease to find more and larger masks

threshold-parameters/flow-threshold.description: Parameter 'Flow threshold (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Flow error threshold, 0 turns off this optional QC step. Default: 0.4

threshold-parameters/exclude-on-edges.description: Parameter 'Exclude masks on edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Discard masks which touch edges of image

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

slice-indices.description:
Parameter 'Slices to import' of type with ID 'integer-range'. Determines which files should be imported based on the sorted and filtered list of files. The first index is 0. Duplicates are allowed. If left empty, all slices will be imported. 

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

generated-image-type.description: Parameter 'Generated image type' of type with ID 'data-type'. The image type that is generated.

filter-expression.description: Parameter 'File filter' of type with ID 'path-query-expression'. Allows you to filter the files.

remove-lut.description: Parameter 'Remove LUT' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, remove the LUT information if present

force-native-import.description: Parameter 'Force native ImageJ importer' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, always use the native ImageJ file importer, even if the file looks like it can only be read by Bio-Formats

output-dimension.description: Parameter 'Output dimension' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. Determines in which dimension the stack grows. You can choose between Z (default), channel, and time.

sort-numerically.description: Parameter 'Sort numerically' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the files are sorted in a natural order. For example a1, a2, a15, a100, b1. Otherwise, they are ordered lexicographically. For example a1, a100, a15, a2, b1

ignore-invalid-slice-indices.description: Parameter 'Ignore invalid slice indices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Used if you limit the imported slices via 'Slices to import'. If enabled, all invalid indices (e.g. negative ones or ones larger than the list) are ignored.

indent.description: Parameter 'Indent' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The indent of a level

add-border.description: Parameter 'Add border before applying operation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a custom border is created around the image and removed afterwards. Otherwise, MorphoLibJ will assume a default outside value determined by ImageJ.

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Radius of the filter kernel in pixels.

operation.description:
Parameter 'Operation' of type with ID 'ij1-morph:operation'. The morphological operation. Following operations are supported:
  • Erosion: A local minimum filter
  • Dilation: A local maximum filter
  • Opening: Erosion, followed by dilation
  • Closing: Dilation, followed by erosion
  • White Top Hat: Image - Opening
  • Black Top Hat (Black Hat): Closing - Image
  • Gradient: Dilation - Erosion
  • Laplacian: (Dilation + Erosion) / 2 - Image
  • Internal gradient: Image - Erosion
  • External gradient: Dilation - Image

element.description: Parameter 'Structure element' of type with ID 'ij1-morph:strel'. The structure element.

border-parameters/jipipe:algorithm:enabl.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If disabled, this algorithm will be skipped in a run. Please note that this will also disable all algorithms dependent on this algorithm.

border-parameters/jipipe:node:ui-locked.description: Parameter 'Lock location/size' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, lock the location and size of this node. Does not affect automated alignment operations. Will lock the size if the node supports a resize handle. Will prevent deletion of the node by the user. Slots can still be edited/connected and parameters can still be changed.

border-parameters/border-color.description: Parameter 'Border color' of type with ID 'expression'. Only applicable if the mode is 'Constant'. The value of the border. Return a number to return a greyscale value. Return an array to set the border to an RGB color.

border-parameters/jipipe:node:bookmarked.description: Parameter 'Bookmark this node' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node is highlighted in the graph editor UI and added into the bookmark list.

border-parameters/jipipe:algorithm:pass-.description:
Parameter 'Pass through' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will pass the input data directly to the output data without any processing. This is different from enabling/disabling the algorithm as this will not disable dependent algorithms.
Please note that setting this parameter via adaptive parameters (if available) does not always yield the expected result. The reason behind this is that pass-through is not trivial for certain nodes. We recommend to use a split node if node execution should be made adaptive.

border-parameters/jipipe:node:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Custom algorithm name.

border-parameters/margin-right.description: Parameter 'Margin right' of type with ID 'expression'. Pixels to add to the right of the image

border-parameters/jipipe:algorithm:runti.description: Parameter 'Partition' of type with ID 'runtime-partition-reference'. Allows to move the node into a different runtime partition, which determine how the workload is executed.

border-parameters/jipipe:node:descriptio.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. A custom description

border-parameters/margin-left.description: Parameter 'Margin left' of type with ID 'expression'. Pixels to add to the left of the image

border-parameters/jipipe:local-paralleli.description: Parameter 'Local parallelization' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the partition- and run-wide parallelization settings and apply parallelization only on this node.

border-parameters/border-mode.description:
Parameter 'Mode' of type with ID 'ij1-border-mode'. Determines how border values are generated
  • Constant: Set the border to a constant value (zero/black unless configurable)
  • Repeat: Repeat the nearest non-border pixel
  • Mirror: Mirror the pixel values across the border
  • Tile: Tile the image

border-parameters/margin-top.description: Parameter 'Margin top' of type with ID 'expression'. Pixels to add to the top of the image

border-parameters/margin-bottom.description: Parameter 'Margin bottom' of type with ID 'expression'. Pixels to add to the bottom of the image

border-parameters/jipipe:data-batch-gene.description: Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. Limits which iteration steps are generated. The first index is zero.

border-parameters/jipipe:parameter-slot-.description: Parameter 'Multiple parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be an additional slot that consumes parameter data sets. The algorithm then will be applied for each of this parameter sets.
Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

border-parameters/jipipe:adaptive-parame.description: Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Enable adaptive parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, you can use custom expressions to generate parameters. Please note that this will disable parallelization.

Parameter 'Overridden parameters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression:string:pair-list'. Here you can override the value of parameters based on annotations. The 'Value' settings should return a value for the parameter type or a JSON string that can be converted into a valid parameter value. The 'Parameter key' setting determines to which parameter this value is written to.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

generated-annotation.description: Parameter 'ROI index annotation' of type with ID 'optional-string'. Optional. Annotation that is added to each individual ROI list. Contains the value index=[index];name=[name].

roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to change the ROI name

enable-3d.description: Parameter 'Connect in 3D' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will look for endpoints in other Z planes.

ignore-if-has-path.description: Parameter 'Ignore if path exists' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, do not consider edges between vertices that already have a path. Impacts performance.

limit-connections.description: Parameter 'Limit created edges (source/target)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, limit the number of created edges per vertex. Edges are sorted by their length.

source-vertex-filter.description: Parameter 'Source vertex filter' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to pre-select source vertices

enforce-edges-within-mask.description: Parameter 'Prevent edges outside mask' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled and a mask is available, check if edges crosses outside the mask boundaries and exclude those from being marked as candidate edge

limit-connections-length-sort-order.description: Parameter 'Edge sort order (length)' of type with ID 'sort-order'. The sort order of edges by their length if 'Limit created edges' is active.

target-vertex-filter.description: Parameter 'Target vertex filter' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to pre-select target vertices

connect-across-t.description: Parameter 'Connect across frames' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm considers also endpoints between different frame planes.

new-edge-metadata.description: Parameter 'Set new edges metadata' of type with ID 'string:string:pair-list'. Allows to set the metadata of newly created edges

ignore-limit-connections-for-target.description: Parameter 'Bypass connection limit for targets' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the 'Limit connections' parameter does not apply to target vertices.

edge-length.description: Parameter 'Edge length' of type with ID 'vector2d'. Determines the minimum and maximum edge length

connect-across-c.description: Parameter 'Connect across channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm considers also endpoints between different channel planes.

candidate-vertex-degree-limit.description: Parameter 'Candidate vertex degree limit' of type with ID 'vector2i'. Limits candidate vertices by their degree

candidate-vertex-value-limit.description: Parameter 'Candidate vertex value limit' of type with ID 'vector2d'. Limits candidate vertices by their value

ignore-limit-connections-for-source.description: Parameter 'Bypass connection limit for sources' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the 'Limit connections' parameter does not apply to source vertices.

new-edge-color.description: Parameter 'Color new edges' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to color newly made edges

reagent-set-description-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with reagent set description' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the reagent set description

id-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with OMERO screen ID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, adds the OMERO screen ID as annotation

name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with screen name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Optional annotation type where the screen title is written.

reagent-set-id-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with reagent set ID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the reagent set ID

description-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with screen description' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Optional annotation type where the screen description is written.

protocol-id-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with protocol ID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the protocol ID

protocol-description-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with protocol description' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the protocol description

annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how newly generated annotations are merged with existing ones

override-credentials.description: Parameter 'Override OMERO credentials' of type with ID 'optional-omero-credentials'. Allows to override the OMERO credentials provided in the JIPipe application settings

tag-to-annotation-importer/tags-to-list-.description: Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, write all tag names into a single annotation (as list). The name of the annotation is determined by the value of this parameter

Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation (filter)' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter out tags that will be included into 'Convert tags into single annotation' This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/fi.description: Parameter 'Keep kv-pair if' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to filter out key-value pairs from being converted into annotations

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/an.description: Parameter 'Annotation name' of type with ID 'expression'. The name of the generated annotation
Parameter 'Annotation value' of type with ID 'expression'. The value of the generated annotation

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/en.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determined if the import of key-value pairs is enabled

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

file-format.description: Parameter 'File format' of type with ID 'ij1-export-table:file-format'. The format of the exported file

file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

slice-threshold-mode.description:
Parameter 'Multi-slice thresholding' of type with ID 'slice-threshold-mode'. Determines how thresholds are calculated if an image has multiple slices.
  • Apply threshold per slice calculates and applies the threshold for each slice.
  • Combine slice statistics calculates statistics for all pixels in the image.
  • Combine thresholds per slice calculates the threshold for each slice. One threshold for all slices is calculated via another math expression.

access-pixels.description: Parameter 'Pixels are available as variables' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the list of all pixels that are considered for the statistics are available as variable 'pixels'. Please note that this will slow down the performance due to necessary conversions.

threshold-annotation-strategy.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

threshold-combine-expression.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

source-area.description: Parameter 'Calculate threshold based on ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines from which image areas the pixel values used for calculating the thresholds are extracted from.

threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

thresholding-function.description: Parameter 'Thresholding function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed for each set of slices. The expression should return a number that will be used as threshold. A pixel is set to 255 if its value is larger than this threshold.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/radiusZ.description: Parameter 'Radius Z' of type with ID 'double'. null

binarize.description: Parameter 'Binarize outputs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output is binarized.

cycle-finder.description: Parameter 'Cycle finder algorithm' of type with ID 'filaments-cycle-finder-algorithm'. The algorithm used for finding cycles. See https://jgrapht.org/javadoc/org.jgrapht.core/org/jgrapht/alg/cycle/package-summary.html for more information.

roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that determines the ROI name

code.description: Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'. Each table is passed as dictionary 'table' with the column name as key and values being an array of strings or doubles. The number of input rows can be accessed via the 'nrow' variable.

skip-invalid-inputs.description: Parameter 'Delete invalid ROIs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If a ROI has less than 3 points, delete it from the output. Otherwise, the ROI is not processed and be stored in the output as-is.

kmeans-k.description: Parameter 'K-means k' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The k parameter of the underlying k-means algorithm. If this value is < 3, it is set to 3. Determines the number of closes neighbors are selected from the current point.

operation.description: Parameter 'Function' of type with ID 'ij1-math-imagecalculator2d:operation'. The function is applied to each pixel pair.
