clij-parameters/input.description: Parameter 'Input' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/width.description: Parameter 'Width' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/height.description: Parameter 'Height' of type with ID 'double'. null

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Cellpose operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

diameter-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with diameter' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the diameter is attached as annotation. Useful if you want to let Cellpose estimate the object diameters.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description: Parameter 'Average object diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, Cellpose will use the provided average diameter to find objects. Otherwise, Cellpose will estimate the diameter by itself.

enable-multichannel.description: Parameter 'Enable multichannel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, multiple image channels are passed to Cellpose. Otherwise, each channel will be processed individually.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose 3.x is used.

enable-3d-denoising.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D denoising' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will denoise in 3D. Otherwise, any 3D image will be processed per-slice. Please note that 3D denoising requires large amounts of memory.

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-parameters/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-parameters/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

connectivity.description: Parameter 'Connectivity' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-3d'. Determines the neighborhood around each pixel that is checked for connectivity

position-t.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Frame)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different frame/time-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all frames. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

line-width.description: Parameter 'Line width' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the line width when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

position-x.description: Parameter 'Location (X)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. The X location. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

position-y.description: Parameter 'Location (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. The Y location. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

position-z.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different Z-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all slices. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

scale-x.description: Parameter 'Scale X' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Scales the ROI. Please note that the scale will not be saved inside the ROI. Must evaluate to a number.

scale-y.description: Parameter 'Scale Y' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Scales the ROI. Please note that the scale will not be saved inside the ROI. Must evaluate to a number.

roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the ROI name

line-color.description: Parameter 'Line color' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the line color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The color can be a common color name (e.g. 'red'), a hex color (e.g. '#ff00ff'), or an RGB color (e.g. '0,128,255').

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. The measurements to calculate.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

position-c.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Channel)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different channel-position. Please note that 'Channel' refers to an image slice and not to a pixel channel. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all channels. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

center-scale.description: Parameter 'Center scale' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, each ROI is scaled relative to its center. Must evaluate to a boolean.

fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the fill color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The color can be a common color name (e.g. 'red'), a hex color (e.g. '#ff00ff'), or an RGB color (e.g. '0,128,255').

radius1.description: Parameter 'Radius 1' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The first radius. Zingman et al suggest that the first radius should be chosen according to the following constraints: maxDistDetails < r1 < minDistIsolatedFeatures, where maxDistDetails is the maximum distance between texture details and minDistIsolatedFeatures is the minimum distance to isolated features.

structure-element.description: Parameter 'Structure element' of type with ID 'ij1-morph:strel'. The shape of the structure element (default is square)

radius2.description: Parameter 'Radius 2' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The second radius. Zingman et al suggest that the second radius should be chosen according to the following constraints: maxFeatureSize < r1 < minTextureRegionsSize, where maxFeatureSize is the maximum size of features and minTextureRegionsSize is the minimum size of texture regions.

validate-json.description: Parameter 'Validate JSON' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, validate if the imported file contains valid JSON

with-edges.description: Parameter 'With edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, edges are converted to ROI

forced-vertex-radius.description: Parameter 'Override vertex radius' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the radius of vertices. Must be at least one.

forced-line-thickness.description: Parameter 'Override edge thickness' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, set the thickness of edges. Must be at least zero.

with-vertices.description: Parameter 'With vertices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, vertices are converted to ROI

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

dark-background.description: Parameter 'Dark background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If the background color is dark. Disable this if your image has a bright background.

k.description: Parameter 'K' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Value of the parameter 'k' in the threshold formula (see Phansalskar et al., 2011). A recommended value is 0.25.

p.description: Parameter 'P' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Value of the parameter 'p' in the threshold formula (see Phansalskar et al., 2011). A recommended value is 2.

q.description: Parameter 'Q' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Value of the parameter 'q' in the threshold formula (see Phansalskar et al., 2011). A recommended value is 10.

r.description: Parameter 'R' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Value of the parameter 'r' in the threshold formula (see Phansalskar et al., 2011). A recommended value is 0.5.

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The radius of the circular local window.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

map-fill-color.description: Parameter 'Map fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color-map'. Allows you to map the 3D ROI fill color to the order generated by the sorting. The color is rendered when converting into a RGB visualization.

output-value-column-name.description: Parameter 'Output value column name' of type with ID 'string'. Name of the output value column. If the column already exists as category column, a unique name is generated based on this one.

output-category-column-name.description: Parameter 'Output category column name' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, the source column name is added into another column. If the column already exists, a unique name is generated.

value-columns.description:
Parameter 'Value columns' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to select the value columns by their name via a filter expression. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

filter.description: Parameter 'Only keep filament if' of type with ID 'expression'. If the filter is left empty or returns TRUE, the filament is kept. Otherwise the vertex is deleted.

radius1.description: Parameter 'Radius 1' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The first radius. Zingman et al suggest that the first radius should be chosen according to the following constraints: maxDistDetails < r1 < minDistIsolatedFeatures, where maxDistDetails is the maximum distance between texture details and minDistIsolatedFeatures is the minimum distance to isolated features.

mode.description:
Parameter 'Mode' of type with ID 'ij1-feature-mfc-2d:mode'. Calculate the lower, upper, or full MFC.
  • Lower MFC calculates MFC- = MAX(0, Closing(r2, Opening(r1, f)) - f)
  • Upper MFC calculates MFC+ = MAX(0, f - Opening(r2, Closing(r1, f)))
  • MFC calculates MFC =MFC+ + MFC-

structure-element.description: Parameter 'Structure element' of type with ID 'ij1-morph:strel'. The shape of the structure element (default is square)

radius2.description: Parameter 'Radius 2' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The second radius. Zingman et al suggest that the second radius should be chosen according to the following constraints: maxFeatureSize < r1 < minTextureRegionsSize, where maxFeatureSize is the maximum size of features and minTextureRegionsSize is the minimum size of texture regions.

urls.description: Parameter 'URLs' of type with ID 'string-list'. List of URLs to download.

ij1-roi-sort-and-extract-statistics-2.name: Sort and extract 2D ROI by statistics

map-line-color.description: Parameter 'Map line color' of type with ID 'optional-color-map'. Allows you to map the ROI line color to the order generated by the sorting. The color is rendered when converting into a RGB visualization.

map-fill-color.description: Parameter 'Map fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color-map'. Allows you to map the ROI fill color to the order generated by the sorting. The color is rendered when converting into a RGB visualization.

selected-indices.description: Parameter 'Selected indices' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. If enabled, the output will only contain the ROI indices specified in by the value. For example, the value '0' returns the first ROI. A value of '0-4' returns the top 5 items.

sort-orders.description: Parameter 'Sort order' of type with ID 'ij1:measurement-column-sort-order-list'. Allows you to determine by which measurement columns to sort by. You can order by multiple columns where the order within this list. If you for example order by 'Area' and then 'Perimeter', the ROI will be ordered by area and if the area is the same by perimeter.

auto-clamp.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing items' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be not error if you select too many items (e.g. if the list only contains 10 items, but you select the top 20)

clear-before-write.description: Parameter 'Clear properties before write' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, all existing ROI properties are deleted before writing the new properties

sigma.description: Parameter 'Sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Standard deviation of the gaussian envelope.

lambd.description: Parameter 'Lambd' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Wavelength of the sinusoidal factor.

psi.description: Parameter 'Psi' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Phase offset.

theta.description: Parameter 'Theta' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Orientation of the normal to the parallel stripes of a Gabor function.

width.description: Parameter 'Width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Size of the filter returned.

gamma.description: Parameter 'Gamma' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Spatial aspect ratio.

height.description: Parameter 'Height' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Size of the filter returned.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. max-volume-pixels.description: Parameter 'Maximum volume (pixels)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The maximum volume of objects (pixel units)

output-32-bit.description: Parameter 'Output 32-bit labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, generate 32-bit labels (support more than 32767 objects)

local-background.description: Parameter 'Local background' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The threshold that is applied to all objects

enable-watershed.description: Parameter 'Enable watershed' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, close spots will be separated

seeds-threshold.description: Parameter 'Seeds threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Determines which seeds will be used

min-volume-pixels.description: Parameter 'Minimum volume (pixels)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The minimum volume of objects (pixel units)

spot-segmentation-settings/segmentation-.description:
Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'ij3d-segmentation-spot3d:spot-segmentation-method'. When the threshold value has been determined, the plugin offers several
methods of voxel clustering that segment the object. The segmentation
proceeds by successive examination of voxels, starting from the seed.

* Classical : All neighbouring voxels with intensity value higher than the
threshold value (Local Background) are clustered to form the object.
* Maximum : This mode operates similarly as the previous one, but with one
additional constraint : the voxels are clustered in the object if their
intensity value is higher than the threshold and lower than the intensity
value of the previously clustered voxels. It can prevent to cluster pixels
from another close spot.
* Block : The same Maximum procedure is applied, but instead of
considering single voxels, block of voxel (all neighbors with value greater
than the local threshold) are examined. All voxels must satisfy the
constraint to be added to the cluster, else no voxel are added.

seed-segmentation-settings/radius.description: Parameter 'Seed radius' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. The radius for the automatically detected seeds

annotations.description: Parameter 'Annotations' of type with ID 'string:string:pair-list'. Use this list to set annotations

data-type.description: Parameter 'Data type' of type with ID 'data-type'. The data type that should be imported.

background-margin.description: Parameter 'Background margin' of type with ID 'inner-margin'. If 'Background color' is enabled, set the distance between the text and the background

center.description: Parameter 'Center at location' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the calculated (x,y) location will be the center of the text

font-size.description: Parameter 'Font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The size of the text

font-style.description: Parameter 'Font style' of type with ID 'font-style'. The style of the text

background-color.description: Parameter 'Background color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, draw a rectangular background ROI behind the text

angle.description: Parameter 'Angle' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Angle of the text

location.description: Parameter 'Location' of type with ID 'fixed-margin'. Determines the location of the text relative to the reference image/bounds of existing ROI

font-family.description: Parameter 'Font family' of type with ID 'font-family-enum'. The font of the text

text.description: Parameter 'Text' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that generates the text

antialiased.description: Parameter 'Antialiased text' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, text will be antialiased

roi-properties/position-t.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Frame)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different frame/time-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all frames.

roi-properties/line-width.description: Parameter 'Line width' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Allows to change the line width when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/position-z.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Z)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different Z-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all slices.

roi-properties/roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to change the ROI name

roi-properties/fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the fill color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/line-color.description: Parameter 'Line color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the line color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/position-c.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Channel)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different channel-position. Please note that 'Channel' refers to an image slice and not to a pixel channel. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all channels.

fill-color-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with fill colors' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Adds the ROI fill colors as annotation. No annotation is generated if the fill color is not explicitly set.

name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with names' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Adds the ROI names as annotation

deduplicate.description: Parameter 'Deduplicate' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, duplicate values for names, locations, etc are removed.

line-color-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with line colors' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Adds the ROI line colors as annotation. No annotation is generated if the fill color is not explicitly set.

line-width-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with line widths' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Adds the ROI line widths as annotation. 0 if not explicity set.

z-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with Z locations' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Adds the ROI Z locations as annotation. The first index is 1. A value of 0 indicates that the ROI is located on all planes.

count-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with list size' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Adds the size of the ROI list as annotation

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge strategy' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how the newly generated annotations are merged with existing annotations.

only-first.description: Parameter 'Only first ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, only global properties and the properties of the first ROI are exported.

t-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with T locations' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Adds the ROI T locations as annotation. The first index is 1. A value of 0 indicates that the ROI is located on all planes.

x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with X locations' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Adds the ROI X locations (top left corner) as annotation

c-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with C locations' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Adds the ROI C locations as annotation. The first index is 1. A value of 0 indicates that the ROI is located on all planes.

y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with Y locations' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Adds the ROI Y locations (top left corner) as annotation

clij-parameters/title.description: Parameter 'Title' of type with ID 'string'. null

ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different channels are compared

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

require-colocalization.description: Parameter 'Only measure if objects co-localize' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, only co-localizing objects are measured

ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different frames are compared

precise-colocalization.description: Parameter 'Precise colocalization' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the object co-localization for the 'Only measure if objects co-localize' setting tests for voxel colocalization (slower). Otherwise, only the bounding boxes are compared (faster).

measurements.description: Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij3d-relation-measurement-set'. The measurements that will be extracted

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

pixels.description: Parameter 'Enlarge/shrink (px)' of type with ID 'expression'. The pixels that determine by how much the ROI is enlarged. Return negative values to shrink the ROI.

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. The measurements to calculate.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

form-data/form:description.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. Description of the element displayed to the user.

form-data/initial-value.description: Parameter 'Initial value' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The initial value

form-data/validation-expression.description: Parameter 'Validation expression' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that is used to validate the user input. There is a variable 'value' available that tests the current number.

form-data/form:tabs/form:tab-annotation.description: Parameter 'Tab annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The annotation that contains the tab name.

form-data/form:tabs/form:tab.description: Parameter 'Tab' of type with ID 'string'. The tab where this form data will appear in.

form-data/form:io/output-annotation.description: Parameter 'Output annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Determines into which annotation the user input is written.

form-data/form:io/merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge output annotation' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how the output annotation is merged with existing values.

form-data/form:io/input-annotation.description: Parameter 'Input annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The annotation used to override the initial value. If the annotation does not exist, the standard initial value is used.

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clij2.plug.name: CLIJ2 Generate Parametric Image From Results Table Column

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

operation.description: Parameter 'Function' of type with ID 'ij1-math-imagecalculator2d:operation'. The function is applied to each pixel pair.

operand/Input 1.description: Parameter 'Input 1' of type with ID 'ij1-math-imagecalculator2d:operand'. null

operand/Input 2.description: Parameter 'Input 2' of type with ID 'ij1-math-imagecalculator2d:operand'. null

export-delay.description: Parameter 'Wait before executing' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Additional waiting time in milliseconds before the macro is executed. Increase this delay if the macro mis-behaves.

code.description:
Parameter 'Code' of type with ID 'ij-macro-code'. The macro code. Images are opened as windows named according to the input slot. You have to select windows with the select() function or comparable functions. You have have one results table input which can be addressed via the global functions. Input ROI are merged into one ROI manager.

You can define variables that are passed from JIPipe to ImageJ. Variables are also created for incoming path-like data, named according to the slot name. Annotations can also be accessed via a function getJIPipeAnnotation(key) or getJIPipeTextAnnotation(key), which returns the string value of the annotation or an empty string if no value was set.

import-delay.description: Parameter 'Wait before importing' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Additional waiting time in milliseconds before results generated by ImageJ are imported back into JIPipe after the execution of the macro. Increase this delay if results are missing or are duplicated.

min-spot-width.description: Parameter 'Minimum spot width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The minimum width of the area around the spot

crop-z.description: Parameter 'Crop Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, crop the depth to the areas where the spots are located

track-id-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with track ID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the track ID is annotated to each ROI

min-spot-height.description: Parameter 'Minimum spot height' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The minimum height of the area around the spot

crop-t.description: Parameter 'Crop frames' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, crop the frames to the areas where the spots are located

crop-xy.description: Parameter 'Crop XY' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, crop the X and Y planes to the areas where the spots are located

tag-to-annotation-importer/tags-to-list-.description: Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, write all tag names into a single annotation (as list). The name of the annotation is determined by the value of this parameter

Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation (filter)' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter out tags that will be included into 'Convert tags into single annotation' This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/destination.description: Parameter 'Destination' of type with ID 'double'. null

expression.description: Parameter 'Keep label if ...' of type with ID 'expression'. This filter expression determines which labels are kept. Annotations are available as variables.

recalibrate.description: Parameter 'Recalibrate afterwards' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Without this setting enabled, the generated image might only be shown as black in ImageJ.

expression.description:
Parameter 'Expression' of type with ID 'expression'. The math expression that calculates the output. Applied per pixel. Please note that most standard JIPipe expression functions are not available. Available variables and operations:
  • Input slot names reference the pixel value at the current coordinate
  • x, y, z, c, t will point to the current location of the pixel
  • width, height, numZ, numC, numT contains the size of the input image(s)
  • You can use annotations as variables (they will be converted to numerics)
  • You can use custom variables (prefix with custom.)
  • Numeric constants like 0.15 can be used
  • You can use brackets ( ) to ensure the correct order
  • [] + [] to add the pixel values
  • [] - [] to subtract the pixel values
  • [] * [] to multiply the pixel values
  • [] / [] to divide the pixel values
  • [] == [] returns 255 (8-bit)/65535 (16-bit)/1 (32-bit) if the left operand is equal to the right operand
  • [] < [] returns 255 (8-bit)/65535 (16-bit)/1 (32-bit) if the left operand is less than the right operand
  • [] > [] returns 255 (8-bit)/65535 (16-bit)/1 (32-bit) if the left operand is more than the right operand
  • [] <= [] returns 255 (8-bit)/65535 (16-bit)/1 (32-bit) if the left operand is less than or equal to the right operand
  • [] >= [] returns 255 (8-bit)/65535 (16-bit)/1 (32-bit) if the left operand is more than or equal to the right operand
  • IF_ELSE([], [], []) returns the second operand if the first operand is larger than zero. Otherwise returns the third operand
  • MIN([], []) to calculate the minimum of the operands
  • MAX([], []) to calculate the maximum of the operands
  • SQR([]) to calculate the square of the operand
  • POW([], []) to calculate the power
  • GAMMA([], []) to calculate EXP(LN( [] ) * [])
  • MOD([], []) to the modulo (values are converted to integers)
  • SQRT([]) to calculate the square root of the operand
  • EXP([]) to calculate the e^operand
  • LN([]) or LOG([]) to calculate the LN(operand)
  • ABS([]) to calculate absolute value
  • SIGNUM([]) to calculate the sign (-1, 0, or 1)
  • FLOOR([]), ROUND([]), CEIL([]) to round the values
  • INVERT([]) calculates the inverted pixel value (for numbers it will return NOT(x))
  • AND([], []), OR([], []), XOR([], []), NOT([]) to calculate bitwise logical operations. Values are automatically converted to integers to allow the operation.
  • SIN([]), SINH([]), ASIN([]), COS([]), COSH([]), ACOS([]), TAN([]), TANH([]), ATAN2([]) are supported trigonometric functions (behavior according to the Java standard library)
  • SLICE_MAX([]), SLICE_MIN([]), SLICE_MEAN([]), SLICE_MEDIAN([]), SLICE_STDDEV([]), SLICE_SKEWNESS([]), SLICE_KURTOSIS([]), SLICE_MODE([]) calculate the given statistic over all pixel values in the current 2D slice

bit-depth.description: Parameter 'Bit depth' of type with ID 'optional-ij-bit-depth'. Allows to force a specific bit depth. If 'None' is selected, JIPipe automatically chooses the highest bit depth based on the input. Please note that 'RGB' will be handled as 32-bit float, as the operations do not support RGB pixel types.

measurements.description: Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-overlap-statistics:set'. The measurements that will be stored in to the output table

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clij2.plug.name: CLIJ2 Mean Of Pixels Above Threshold

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

expression.description:
Parameter 'Expression' of type with ID 'path-query-expression'. Expression that is used to modify the path. Available variables include path, absolute_path, name, and parent and are passed as strings. Additionally, annotations are available as variables if enabled.

If the expression returns a non-string value, the path data will be skipped.

To improve compatibility between operating systems, we recommend to use '/' as path separator.

mode.description: Parameter 'Mode' of type with ID 'ij1-edge-laplacian:mode'. The filter kernel that should be used. The inverted kernels have a positive center value, while non-inverted kernels have a negative value at their center.

remove-negative-values.description: Parameter 'Remove negative values' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, negative values are set to zero

extend-target.description: Parameter 'Extend target to fit source' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, values of the target table are added if there are more rows in the source than in the target.

column-filter.description:
Parameter 'Column filter' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Determines which columns are copied from source to target. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

skip-empty-target.description: Parameter 'Skip empty target conditions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, skips the addition of values from the source if the target does not have an equivalent condition.

row-normalization.description: Parameter 'Row normalization' of type with ID 'table-column-row-normalization'. Determines how missing column values are handled if the input tables have different numbers of rows. You can set it to zero/empty (depending on numeric or string type), to the row number (starting with zero), copy the last value, or cycle.

restore-original-annotations.description: Parameter 'Restore original annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, restore the original annotations of the data

reference-columns.description:
Parameter 'Reference columns' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Columns that should act as reference points for the merging of the rows from different tables. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

weka-training-mask-2d-v2.name: Train Weka model from mask (multiple images 2D)

label-data-annotation.description:
Parameter 'Label data annotation' of type with ID 'data-annotation-query-expression'. Determines which data annotation contains the masks. Please ensure that the appropriate label data is annotated to the raw input data. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

classifier-settings/classifier.description: Parameter 'Classifier' of type with ID 'weka-classifier'. The selected classifier

classifier-settings/balance-classes.description: Parameter 'Balance classes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. The classifier uses by the default all the user traces to train. By selecting this option, we filter first the classes in order to provide a balanced distribution of the samples. This implies that the less numerous classes will duplicate some of their samples and the more populated classes will lose some of their samples for the sake of even distribution. This option is strongly recommended if we want to give the same importance to all classes. An alternative is to use the Weka CostSensitiveClassifier and set a corresponding cost matrix.

feature-settings/use-neighbors.description: Parameter 'Use neighbors' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Set the use of the neighbor features

feature-settings/membrane-patch-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane patch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. this represents the size 𝑛×𝑛 of the field of view for the membrane projection filters. Only available for 2D features.

feature-settings/membrane-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane thickness' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Expected value of the membrane thickness, 1 pixel by default. The more accurate, the more precise the filter will be.

feature-settings/min-sigma.description: Parameter 'Minimum sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Minimum radius of the isotropic filters used to create the features. By default 1 pixel.

feature-settings/training-features.description:
Parameter 'Training features' of type with ID 'weka-feature-set-2d'. Here we can select and deselect the training features, which are the key of the learning procedure. The plugin creates a stack of images —one image for each feature. For instance, if only Gaussian blur is selected as a feature, the classifier will be trained on the original image and some blurred versions to it with different 𝜎 parameters for the Gaussian. If the input image is grayscale, the features will be calculated using double precision (32-bit images). In the case of RGB input images, the features will be RGB as well.
  • Gaussian blur: performs 𝑛 individual convolutions with Gaussian kernels with the normal 𝑛 variations of 𝜎. The larger the radius the more blurred the image becomes until the pixels are homogeneous.
  • Sobel filter: calculates an approximation of the gradient of the image intensity at each pixel. Gaussian blurs with 𝜎 varying as usual are performed prior to the filter.
  • Hessian: Calculates a Hessian matrix 𝐻 at each pixel. Prior to the application of any filters, a Gaussian blur with varying 𝜎 is performed.
  • Difference of gaussians: calculates two Gaussian blur images from the original image and subtracts one from the other. 𝜎 values are varied as usual, so 𝑛(𝑛−1)2 feature images are added to the stack.
  • Membrane projections: enhances membrane-like structures of the image through directional filtering. The initial kernel for this operation is hardcoded as a 19×19 zero matrix with the middle column entries set to 1. Multiple kernels are created by rotating the original kernel by 6 degrees up to a total rotation of 180 degrees, giving 30 kernels. Each kernel is convolved with the image and then the set of 30 images are Z-projected into a single image via 6 methods (sum of the pixels in each image, mean of the pixels in each image, standard deviation of the pixels in each image, median of the pixels in each image, maximum of the pixels in each image, minimum of the pixels in each image). Each of the 6 resulting images is a feature. Hence pixels in lines of similarly valued pixels in the image that are different from the average image intensity will stand out in the Z-projections.
  • Mean, Variance, Median, Minimum, Maximum: the pixels within a radius of 𝜎 pixels from the target pixel are subjected to the pertinent operation (mean/min etc.) and the target pixel is set to that value.
  • Anisotropic diffusion: the anisotropic diffusion filtering from Fiji with 20 iterations, 𝜎 smoothing per iterations, 𝑎1=0.10,0.35, 𝑎2=0.9, and an edge threshold set to the membrane size.
  • Bilateral filter: is very similar to the Mean filter but better preserves edges while averaging/blurring other parts of the image. The filter accomplishes this task by only averaging the values around the current pixel that are close in color value to the current pixel. The ‘closeness’ of other neighborhood pixels to the current pixels is determined by the specified threshold. I.e. for a value of 10 each pixel that contributes to the current mean have to be within 10 values of the current pixel. In our case, we combine spatial radius of 5 and 10, with a range radius of 50 and 100.
  • Lipschitz filter: from Mikulas Stencel plugin. This plugin implements Lipschitz cover of an image that is equivalent to a grayscale opening by a cone. The Lipschitz cover can be applied for the elimination of a slowly varying image background by subtraction of the lower Lipschitz cover (a top-hat procedure). A sequential double scan algorithm is used. We use down and top hats combination, with slope 𝑠=5,10,15,20,25.
  • Kuwahara filter: another noise-reduction filter that preserves edges. This is a version of the Kuwahara filter that uses linear kernels rather than square ones. We use the membrane patch size as kernel size, 30 angles and the three different criteria (Variance, Variance / Mean and Variance / Mean^2).
  • abor filter: at the moment this option may take some time and memory because it generates a very diverse range of Gabor filters (22). ’ This may undergo changes in the future’. The implementation details are included in this script. The Gabor filter is an edge detection and texture filter, which convolves several kernels at different angles with an image. Gabor filters are band-pass filters and therefore implement a frequency transformation.
  • Derivatives filter: calculates high order (4, 6, 8, 10) derivatives of the input image.
  • Laplacian filter: computes the Laplacian of the input image using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎.
  • Structure filter: calculates for all elements in the input image, the eigenvalues (smallest and largest) of the so-called structure tensor using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎 and integration scales 1 and 3.
  • Entropy: draws a circle of radius 𝑟 around each pixel; gets the histogram of that circle split in numBins chunks; then calculates the entropy as ∑𝑝 in histogram−𝑝∗log2(𝑝), where 𝑝 is the probability of each chunk in the histogram. numBins is equal to 32,64,128,256. 𝑟 is equal to 𝜎.
  • Neighbors: shifts the image in 8 directions by an certain number of pixel, 𝜎. Therefore creates 8𝑛 feature images.
More information: https://imagej.net/plugins/tws/

If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

spot-detector-parameters/target-channel.description:
Parameter 'Target channel' of type with ID 'int'. This detector creates spots by importing regions from a label image.

A label image is an image where the pixel values are integers. Each object in a label image is represented by a single common pixel value (the label) that is unique to the object.

This detector reads such an image and create spots from each object. In 2D the contour of a label is imported. In 3D, spherical spots of the same volume that the label are created.

The spot quality stores the object area or volume in pixels.

spot-detector-parameters/simplify-contou.description:
Parameter 'Simplify contours' of type with ID 'boolean'. This detector creates spots by importing regions from a label image.

A label image is an image where the pixel values are integers. Each object in a label image is represented by a single common pixel value (the label) that is unique to the object.

This detector reads such an image and create spots from each object. In 2D the contour of a label is imported. In 3D, spherical spots of the same volume that the label are created.

The spot quality stores the object area or volume in pixels.

filter-function.description: Parameter 'Candidate edge filter' of type with ID 'expression'. Filter expression that determines if an edge is considered as candidate

enable-3d.description: Parameter 'Connect in 3D' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will look for endpoints in other Z planes.

ensure-no-path-exists.description: Parameter 'Prevent duplicate paths' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, new edges will only be creates if there is not already an existing path between two end points.

connect-across-c.description: Parameter 'Connect across channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm considers also endpoints between different channel planes.

exclude-existing-endpoints.description: Parameter 'Exclude existing endpoints' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, existing endpoints are excluded from being new sources or targets.

enforce-edges-within-mask.description: Parameter 'Prevent edges outside mask' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled and a mask is available, check if edges crosses outside the mask boundaries and exclude those from being marked as candidate edge

connect-across-t.description: Parameter 'Connect across frames' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm considers also endpoints between different frame planes.

new-edge-color.description: Parameter 'Color new edges' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to color newly made edges

enforce-same-component.description: Parameter 'Prevent cross-object edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, new edges will never be created across two different objects. Disable this option if there are broken filaments in the input graph.

scoring-function.description: Parameter 'Candidate edge scoring' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that calculates the score of a candidate edge. The higher the score, the more likely the edge is chosen. The default score is the negative of the dot product of the two normalized directions.

minimum-value.description: Parameter 'Minimum value' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The minimum attribute value

connectivity.description: Parameter 'Connectivity' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-2d'. The neighborhood connectivity

attribute.description: Parameter 'Attribute' of type with ID 'ij1-morph-grayscale-attribute-filtering-2d:attribute'. The attribute that is used for filtering

operation.description: Parameter 'Operation' of type with ID 'ij1-morph-grayscale-attribute-filtering-2d:operation'. The operation that should be applied

keep-original-annotations.description: Parameter 'Keep original annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, outputs that were not generated keep their original annotations. Otherwise the merged annotations from the iteration step are used.

size-c.description: Parameter 'Overwrite number of channels (C)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Number of generated channel slices.

size-z.description: Parameter 'Overwrite number of slices (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Number of generated Z slices.

size-t.description: Parameter 'Overwrite number of frames (T)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Number of generated frame slices.

classifier-settings/classifier.description: Parameter 'Classifier' of type with ID 'weka-classifier'. The selected classifier

classifier-settings/balance-classes.description: Parameter 'Balance classes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. The classifier uses by the default all the user traces to train. By selecting this option, we filter first the classes in order to provide a balanced distribution of the samples. This implies that the less numerous classes will duplicate some of their samples and the more populated classes will lose some of their samples for the sake of even distribution. This option is strongly recommended if we want to give the same importance to all classes. An alternative is to use the Weka CostSensitiveClassifier and set a corresponding cost matrix.

feature-settings/use-neighbors.description: Parameter 'Use neighbors' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Set the use of the neighbor features

feature-settings/membrane-patch-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane patch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. this represents the size 𝑛×𝑛 of the field of view for the membrane projection filters. Only available for 2D features.

feature-settings/membrane-size.description: Parameter 'Membrane thickness' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Expected value of the membrane thickness, 1 pixel by default. The more accurate, the more precise the filter will be.

feature-settings/min-sigma.description: Parameter 'Minimum sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Minimum radius of the isotropic filters used to create the features. By default 1 pixel.

feature-settings/training-features.description:
Parameter 'Training features' of type with ID 'weka-feature-set-3d'. Here we can select and deselect the training features, which are the key of the learning procedure. The plugin creates a stack of images —one image for each feature. For instance, if only Gaussian blur is selected as a feature, the classifier will be trained on the original image and some blurred versions to it with different 𝜎 parameters for the Gaussian. If the input image is grayscale, the features will be calculated using double precision (32-bit images). In the case of RGB input images, the features will be RGB as well.
  • Gaussian blur: performs 𝑛 individual 3D convolutions with Gaussian kernels with the normal 𝑛 variations of 𝜎. The larger the radius the more blurred the image becomes until the pixels are homogeneous.
  • Hessian: using FeatureJ it computes for each image element (voxel) the eigenvalues of the Hessian, which can be used for example to discriminate locally between plate-like, line-like, and blob-like image structures. More specifically, it calculates the magnitude of the largest, middle and smallest eigenvalue of the Hessian tensor. It requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater. It uses smoothing scale 𝜎.
  • Derivatives: calculates high order derivatives of the input image (4, 6, 8, 10)
  • Laplacian: computes the Laplacian of the input image using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎.
  • Structure: calculates for all elements in the input image, the eigenvalues (smallest and largest) of the so-called structure tensor using FeatureJ (it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater). It uses smoothing scale 𝜎 and integration scales 1 and 3.
  • Edges: detects edges using Canny edge detection, which involves computation of the gradient magnitude, suppression of locally non-maximum gradient magnitudes, and (hysteresis) thresholding. Again, this feature uses FeatureJ so it requires enabling the ImageScience update site in the updater. It uses smoothing scale 𝜎.
  • Difference of Gaussian: calculates two Gaussian blur images from the original image and subtracts one from the other. 𝜎 values are varied as usual, so 𝑛(𝑛−1)2 feature images are added to the stack.
  • Minimum, Maximum, Mean, Variance, Median: the voxels within a radius of 𝜎 voxels from the target pixel are subjected to the pertinent operation (mean/min etc.) and the target voxel is set to that value.
More information: https://imagej.net/plugins/tws/

feature-settings/max-sigma.description: Parameter 'Maximum sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Maximum radius of the isotropic filters used to create the features. By default 16 pixels.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

spot-tracker-parameters/max-frame-gap.description:
Parameter 'Max frame gap' of type with ID 'int'. This tracker is identical to the sparse LAP tracker present in this trackmate, except that it
proposes fewer tuning options. Namely, only gap closing is allowed, based solely on
a distance and time condition. Track splitting and merging are not allowed, resulting
in having non-branching tracks.

The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

process-environment.description: Parameter 'Process' of type with ID 'process'. The process that should be executed. Click 'Edit' to setup the process.

override-arguments.description: Parameter 'Override arguments' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, override arguments of the environment with the one provided by the expression. Please note that this expression has access to annotations.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

output-output-folder.description: Parameter 'Output output folder' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output folder designated to the current iteration step is also output into a slot 'Output folder'. Please note that any existing slot with this name is replaced by a directory output slot. Data will be not be extracted from the process output.

image-threshold.description: Parameter 'Image threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Clusters the voxels

seeds-threshold.description: Parameter 'Seeds threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Determines which seeds will be used

seed-segmentation-settings/radius.description: Parameter 'Seed radius' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. The radius for the automatically detected seeds

annotate-with-current-data.description: Parameter 'Annotate with current data' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, the current (main) data is converted into an annotation of the specified name

annotation-name.description:
Parameter 'Extracted data annotation' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Determines which annotation is extracted. If multiple match, the first matching annotation column is used. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

keep-other-data-annotations.description: Parameter 'Keep other data annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, existing data annotations that is not the targeted annotation are kept.

keep-current-annotation.description: Parameter 'Keep current data annotation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the targeted data annotation is kept. Otherwise it is removed.

ignore-missing-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing data annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data rows with a missing data annotation are ignored.

max-volume-pixels.description: Parameter 'Maximum volume (pixels)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The maximum volume of objects (pixel units)

gauss-pc.description:
Parameter 'SD Value' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. A factor
which is applied to the standard deviation to define the value of the
threshold (sd value). As a rule of the thumb, a factor 1.17 will bring the
threshold to the full width at half maximum while factors 2 and 3 will fill
about 90% and 99% of the curve surface, respectively.

output-32-bit.description: Parameter 'Output 32-bit labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, generate 32-bit labels (support more than 32767 objects)

enable-watershed.description: Parameter 'Enable watershed' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, close spots will be separated

seeds-threshold.description: Parameter 'Seeds threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Determines which seeds will be used

min-volume-pixels.description: Parameter 'Minimum volume (pixels)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The minimum volume of objects (pixel units)

max-radius.description: Parameter 'Maximum radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. A gaussian fit of the radial distribution is computed in a given radius around the seed (Radius Max, in pixels))

spot-segmentation-settings/segmentation-.description:
Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'ij3d-segmentation-spot3d:spot-segmentation-method'. When the threshold value has been determined, the plugin offers several
methods of voxel clustering that segment the object. The segmentation
proceeds by successive examination of voxels, starting from the seed.

* Classical : All neighbouring voxels with intensity value higher than the
threshold value (Local Background) are clustered to form the object.
* Maximum : This mode operates similarly as the previous one, but with one
additional constraint : the voxels are clustered in the object if their
intensity value is higher than the threshold and lower than the intensity
value of the previously clustered voxels. It can prevent to cluster pixels
from another close spot.
* Block : The same Maximum procedure is applied, but instead of
considering single voxels, block of voxel (all neighbors with value greater
than the local threshold) are examined. All voxels must satisfy the
constraint to be added to the cluster, else no voxel are added.

seed-segmentation-settings/radius.description: Parameter 'Seed radius' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. The radius for the automatically detected seeds

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge strategy' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how annotations that are added in the R script are merged into existing annotations.

script.description:
Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'r-script'. The script that contains the R commands. The script comes with various API functions and variables that allow to communicate with JIPipe:
  • JIPipe.InputSlotRowCounts contains named row counts for each slot. Is always 1 for each slot.
  • JIPipe.TextAnnotations contains the list of text annotations (named strings)
  • JIPipe.Variables contains the list of variables defined by parameters (named values). If a parameter's unique key is a valid variable name, it will also be available as variable.
  • JIPipe.GetInputFolder(slot, row=0) returns the data folder of the specified slot. The data folder contains the input row stored in standardized JIPipe format.
  • JIPipe.GetInputAsDataFrame(slot, row=0) reads the specified input data row as data frame.
  • JIPipe.AddOutputFolder(slot, annotations=list()) adds a new output folder into the specified output slot and returns the folder path. Optionally, you can assign annotations to add as list of named strings. Please note that the folder must contain data according to the slot's data type (an image file or a CSV file respectively)
  • JIPipe.AddOutputDataFrame(data, slot, annotations=list()) adds the specified data frame into the specified output slot. Optionally, you can add annotations to the data.
  • JIPipe.AddOutputPNGImagePath(data, slot, annotations=list()) generates an image file path to be added as output and return the path. Please note that you must use png() or other functions to actually write this file.Optionally, you can add annotations to the data.
  • JIPipe.AddOutputTIFFImagePath(data, slot, annotations=list()) generates an image file path to be added as output and return the path. Please note that you must use tiff() or other functions to actually write this file.Optionally, you can add annotations to the data.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override R environment' of type with ID 'optional-r-environment'. If enabled, a different R environment is used for this Node.

thin-lines.description: Parameter 'Thin lines' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated ROI will have thin lines instead of utilizing the thickness of the involved vertices

with-edges.description: Parameter 'With edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, edges are converted to ROI

ignore-non-2d-edges.description: Parameter 'Non-2D edge behavior' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines the operation if an edge between two Z, C, or T locations is encountered. Either the vertex is copied to both locations, or the edge can be ignored

forced-vertex-radius.description: Parameter 'Override vertex radius' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the radius of vertices. Must be at least one.

forced-line-thickness.description: Parameter 'Override edge thickness' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, set the thickness of edges. Must be at least zero.

with-vertices.description: Parameter 'With vertices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, vertices are converted to ROI
