slice-range.description:
Parameter 'Slice range' of type with ID 'integer-range'. Only the data rows within this range are copied into the output. The first index is zero. Invalid indices are ignored.

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

color.description: Parameter 'Color' of type with ID 'color'. The color of each pixel.

roi:target-area.description: Parameter 'Only apply to ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines where the algorithm is applied to.

exclude-zero.description: Parameter 'Exclude zero' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, do not apply the algorithm to the zero label.

filter.description: Parameter 'Keep label if' of type with ID 'expression'. Determines whether a label is kept or removed

deleted-labels.description: Parameter 'Deleted label value' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Value assigned to labels that are deleted

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. prefer-render-via-overlay.description: Parameter 'Prefer render via overlay' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the rendering via an ImageJ overlay is preferred even if the magnification is 1.0

label-foreground.description: Parameter 'Label foreground' of type with ID 'color'. The text color of the label (if enabled)

label-size.description: Parameter 'Label size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Font size of drawn labels

override-fill-color.description: Parameter 'Override fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, the fill color will be overridden by this value. If a ROI has no fill color, it will always fall back to this color.

draw-outline-mode.description: Parameter 'Draw outline' of type with ID 'roi-element-drawing-mode'. If enabled, draw a white outline of the ROI

magnification.description: Parameter 'Magnification' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Magnification applied during the rendering

label-background.description: Parameter 'Label background' of type with ID 'optional-color'. The background color of the label (if enabled)

override-line-width.description: Parameter 'Override line width' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, the line width will be overridden by this value. If a ROI has a line width equal or less than zero, it will fall back to this value.

drawn-label.description: Parameter 'Draw labels' of type with ID 'roi-label'. Allows to draw labels on top of ROI.

ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI will show outside their channel (C) layer

fill-outline-mode.description: Parameter 'Draw filled outline' of type with ID 'roi-element-drawing-mode'. If enabled, fill the ROI areas

override-line-color.description: Parameter 'Override line color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, the line color will be overridden by this value. If a ROI has no line color, it will always fall back to this color.

ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI will show outside their frame (T) layer

opacity.description: Parameter 'Opacity' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Opacity of the added ROI and labels. If zero, they are not visible. If set to one, they are fully visible.

ignore-z.description: Parameter 'Ignore Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI will show outside their Z layer

apply-per-slice.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If higher dimensional data is provided, generate a histogram for each slice. If disabled, the histogram is generated for the whole image.

normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the values are divided by the maximum value

multi-channel-mode.description: Parameter 'Multi-channel mode' of type with ID 'ij1-analyze-statistics-histogram:multi-channel-mode'. Determines how values are calculated if a multi-channel image is provided. Multi-channel images are converted to RGB automatically.

slice-annotation.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice annotation' of type with ID 'string'. Optional annotation type that generated for each slice output. It contains the string 'slice=[Number]'.

erosion-iterations.description: Parameter 'Erosion iterations' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of erosion iterations after filling holes. If you do not want to change the object sizes, keep this the same as the dilation iterations.

internal-gradient-radius.description: Parameter 'Internal gradient radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Radius for the internal gradient radius calculation

apply-second-clahe.description: Parameter 'Apply second CLAHE' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

apply-gaussian.description: Parameter 'Apply Gaussian' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

gauss-sigma.description: Parameter 'Gauss sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Standard deviation of the Gaussian (pixels)

dilation-iterations.description: Parameter 'Dilation iterations' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of dilation iterations after filling holes

apply-first-clahe.description: Parameter 'Apply first CLAHE' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:algorithm:enabl.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If disabled, this algorithm will be skipped in a run. Please note that this will also disable all algorithms dependent on this algorithm.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:ui-locked.description: Parameter 'Lock location/size' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, lock the location and size of this node. Does not affect automated alignment operations. Will lock the size if the node supports a resize handle. Will prevent deletion of the node by the user. Slots can still be edited/connected and parameters can still be changed.

auto-thresholding/dark-background.description: Parameter 'Dark background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If the background color is dark. Disable this if your image has a bright background.

auto-thresholding/slice-threshold-mode.description:
Parameter 'Multi-slice thresholding' of type with ID 'slice-threshold-mode'. Determines how thresholds are calculated if an image has multiple slices.
  • Apply threshold per slice calculates and applies the threshold for each slice.
  • Combine slice statistics merges the slice histograms into one, which is then used for threshold calculation.
  • Combine thresholds per slice calculates the threshold for each slice. One threshold for all slices is calculated via the math expression.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:bookmarked.description: Parameter 'Bookmark this node' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node is highlighted in the graph editor UI and added into the bookmark list.

auto-thresholding/threshold-annotation-s.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:algorithm:pass-.description:
Parameter 'Pass through' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will pass the input data directly to the output data without any processing. This is different from enabling/disabling the algorithm as this will not disable dependent algorithms.
Please note that setting this parameter via adaptive parameters (if available) does not always yield the expected result. The reason behind this is that pass-through is not trivial for certain nodes. We recommend to use a split node if node execution should be made adaptive.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Custom algorithm name.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:algorithm:runti.description: Parameter 'Partition' of type with ID 'runtime-partition-reference'. Allows to move the node into a different runtime partition, which determine how the workload is executed.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:descriptio.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. A custom description

auto-thresholding/threshold-combine-expr.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

auto-thresholding/source-area.description: Parameter 'Calculate threshold based on ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines from which image areas the pixel values used for calculating the thresholds are extracted from.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:local-paralleli.description: Parameter 'Local parallelization' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the partition- and run-wide parallelization settings and apply parallelization only on this node.

auto-thresholding/threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:data-batch-gene.description: Parameter 'Merge same annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if different values for the same annotation columns are encountered.

Parameter 'Grouping method' of type with ID 'column-matching'. Algorithms with multiple inputs require to match the incoming data to data sets. This allows you to determine how interesting data annotation columns are extracted from the incoming data. Union matches using the union of annotation columns. Intersection intersects the sets of available columns. You can also customize which columns should be included or excluded.

Parameter 'Merge same data annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:data-annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if different values for the same data annotation columns are encountered.

Parameter 'Custom grouping columns' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Only used if 'Grouping method' is set to 'Custom'. Determines which annotation columns are referred to group data sets. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"
Parameter 'Skip incomplete data sets' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, incomplete data sets are silently skipped. Otherwise an error is displayed if such a configuration is detected.
Parameter 'Custom annotation matching method' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression used to compare two annotation sets.
Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. Limits which iteration steps are generated. The first index is zero.
Parameter 'Force flow graph solver' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, disable the faster dictionary-based solver. Use this if you experience unexpected behavior.

Parameter 'Annotation matching method' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-matching-method'. Allows to customize when two annotation sets are considered as equal. By default, non-empty annotation values should match exactly. You can also use a custom expression, instead.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:parameter-slot-.description: Parameter 'Multiple parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be an additional slot that consumes parameter data sets. The algorithm then will be applied for each of this parameter sets.
Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:adaptive-parame.description: Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Enable adaptive parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, you can use custom expressions to generate parameters. Please note that this will disable parallelization.

Parameter 'Overridden parameters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression:string:pair-list'. Here you can override the value of parameters based on annotations. The 'Value' settings should return a value for the parameter type or a JSON string that can be converted into a valid parameter value. The 'Parameter key' setting determines to which parameter this value is written to.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:algorithm:enabled.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If disabled, this algorithm will be skipped in a run. Please note that this will also disable all algorithms dependent on this algorithm.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:node:ui-locked.description: Parameter 'Lock location/size' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, lock the location and size of this node. Does not affect automated alignment operations. Will lock the size if the node supports a resize handle. Will prevent deletion of the node by the user. Slots can still be edited/connected and parameters can still be changed.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:node:bookmarked.description: Parameter 'Bookmark this node' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node is highlighted in the graph editor UI and added into the bookmark list.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:algorithm:pass-th.description:
Parameter 'Pass through' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will pass the input data directly to the output data without any processing. This is different from enabling/disabling the algorithm as this will not disable dependent algorithms.
Please note that setting this parameter via adaptive parameters (if available) does not always yield the expected result. The reason behind this is that pass-through is not trivial for certain nodes. We recommend to use a split node if node execution should be made adaptive.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:node:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Custom algorithm name.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:algorithm:runtime.description: Parameter 'Partition' of type with ID 'runtime-partition-reference'. Allows to move the node into a different runtime partition, which determine how the workload is executed.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:node:description.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. A custom description

clahe-enhancing/max-slope.description: Parameter 'Max slope' of type with ID 'primitive.float'.

clahe-enhancing/fast-mode.description: Parameter 'Fast mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:local-paralleliza.description: Parameter 'Local parallelization' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the partition- and run-wide parallelization settings and apply parallelization only on this node.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:data-batch-genera.description: Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. Limits which iteration steps are generated. The first index is zero.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:parameter-slot-al.description: Parameter 'Multiple parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be an additional slot that consumes parameter data sets. The algorithm then will be applied for each of this parameter sets.
Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

clahe-enhancing/jipipe:adaptive-paramete.description: Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Enable adaptive parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, you can use custom expressions to generate parameters. Please note that this will disable parallelization.

Parameter 'Overridden parameters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression:string:pair-list'. Here you can override the value of parameters based on annotations. The 'Value' settings should return a value for the parameter type or a JSON string that can be converted into a valid parameter value. The 'Parameter key' setting determines to which parameter this value is written to.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will segment in 3D. Otherwise, any 3D image will be processed per-slice. Please note that 3D segmentation requires large amounts of memory.

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Python operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

diameter-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with diameter' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the diameter is attached as annotation. Useful if you want to let Cellpose estimate the object diameters.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description: Parameter 'Average object diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, Cellpose will use the provided average diameter to find objects. Otherwise, Cellpose will estimate the diameter by itself.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose is used.

model.description:
Parameter 'Model' of type with ID 'omnipose-model'. The model type that should be used.

See https://github.com/kevinjohncutler/omnipose for a description of all models

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-parameters/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-parameters/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

output-parameters/output-probabilities.description: Parameter 'Output probabilities' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel

output-parameters/output-roi.description: Parameter 'Output 2D ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output a ROI list containing all detected objects

output-parameters/output-labels.description: Parameter 'Output labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output an image that contains a unique greyscale value for each detected object.

output-parameters/output-flows-xy.description: Parameter 'Output flows XY' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel

output-parameters/output-flows-z.description: Parameter 'Output flows Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)

output-parameters/output-flows-d.description: Parameter 'Output flows d' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)

enhancement-parameters/cluster.description: Parameter 'Cluster' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. DBSCAN clustering. Reduces oversegmentation of thin features

enhancement-parameters/fast-mode.description: Parameter 'Fast mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. disable dynamics on full image (makes algorithm faster for images with large diameters)

enhancement-parameters/anisotropy.description: Parameter 'Anisotropy (3D)' of type with ID 'optional-double'. For 3D segmentation, optional rescaling factor (e.g. set to 2.0 if Z is sampled half as dense as X or Y)

enhancement-parameters/interpolate.description: Parameter 'Interpolate (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Interpolate during 2D dynamics (not available in 3D)

enhancement-parameters/no-resample.description: Parameter 'Disable resample' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Disable dynamics on full image (makes algorithm faster for images with large diameters)

enhancement-parameters/net-average.description: Parameter 'Average all networks' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Runs the 4 built-in networks and averages them if True, runs one network if disabled

threshold-parameters/stitch-threshold.description: Parameter 'Stitch threshold' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Compute masks in 2D then stitch together masks with IoU>0.9 across planes

threshold-parameters/diam-threshold.description: Parameter 'Cell diameter threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Cell diameter threshold for upscaling before mask reconstruction, default 12.

threshold-parameters/mask-threshold.description: Parameter 'Mask threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Mask threshold, default is 0, decrease to find more and larger masks

threshold-parameters/flow-threshold.description: Parameter 'Flow threshold (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Flow error threshold, 0 turns off this optional QC step. Default: 0.4

threshold-parameters/exclude-on-edges.description: Parameter 'Exclude masks on edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Discard masks which touch edges of image

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/radius_x.description: Parameter 'Radius X' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/radius_y.description: Parameter 'Radius Y' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/radius_z.description: Parameter 'Radius Z' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clijx.clij.name: Cli Jx Average Neighbor Distance Map

The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

frame-location.description: Parameter 'Frame' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's frame location (0 = all frames)

roi-comment.description: Parameter 'ROI comment' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's comment field

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

center-y.description: Parameter 'Center (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's center Y location

center-x.description: Parameter 'Center (X)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's center X location

center-z.description: Parameter 'Center (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's center Z location

roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI name

metadata-entries.description: Parameter 'Metadata' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. Allows to set/override additional metadata items

channel-location.description: Parameter 'Channel' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's channel location (0 = all channels)

fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's fill color (you can return a color or a HEX string)

measurements.description: Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij3d-measurement-set'. The measurements to calculate.

cumulative.description: Parameter 'Cumulative' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generate a cumulative histogram. Applied after accumulation.

output-column-bin-max.description: Parameter 'Output column (bin max)' of type with ID 'expression'. The column where the maximum value of a bin will be written

weight-column.description:
Parameter 'Weight column' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Column containing the weight value for each value This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

value-filter.description: Parameter 'Value filter' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to remove values before they are included into the histogram calculations

input-column.description:
Parameter 'Input column' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The column to be used for creating a histogram This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

output-column-bin-count.description: Parameter 'Output column (accumulated)' of type with ID 'expression'. The [normalized/cumulative] accumulated values in the bin

num-bins.description: Parameter 'Number of bins' of type with ID 'expression'. The number of bins. If set to infinite (inf), there will be as many bins as unique values

additional-columns.description: Parameter 'Additional columns' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. Additional columns to be added into the histogram.

normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Normalize the histogram by dividing the accumulated values by the maximum accumulated value. Applied after accumulation and calculating cumulative values (if enabled).

accumulation-function.description: Parameter 'Accumulation function' of type with ID 'expression'. Function that accumulates the values inside a bin

output-column-bin-min.description: Parameter 'Output column (bin min)' of type with ID 'expression'. The column where the minimum value of a bin will be written

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/method.description: Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'string'. null

clij-parameters/method.description: Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'string'. null

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge strategy' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if there is already an annotation with the same column name.

filter.description:
Parameter 'Generate annotation if' of type with ID 'table-cell-value-query-expression'. Filters the columns and/or table cell values. 

This parameter accesses all table cells. Please take a look at the expression builder for more information about the available variables.

row-merging-strategy.description: Parameter 'Row merging strategy' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Important if the rows of the selected columns have different values. Determines how they are merged. Ordered by row index.

apply-internal-gradient.description: Parameter 'Apply internal gradient filter' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, an internal gradient filter is applied to the hessian image.

apply-despeckle.description: Parameter 'Apply despeckle' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a median filter is applied to the thresholded images.

compare-absolute.description: Parameter 'Compare absolute' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines whether eigenvalues are compared in absolute sense

despeckle-iterations.description: Parameter 'Despeckle iterations' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. How many times the despeckle is applied.

eigenvalue-selection.description: Parameter 'Eigenvalue' of type with ID 'ij1:eigenvalue-selection-2d'. Allows you to choose whether the largest or smallest Eigenvalues are chosen

smoothing.description: Parameter 'Smoothing' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The smoothing scale at which the required image derivatives are computed. The scale is equal to the standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel used for differentiation and must be larger than zero. In order to enforce physical isotropy, for each dimension, the scale is divided by the size of the image elements (aspect ratio) in that dimension.

gradient-radius.description: Parameter 'Gradient radius' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Radius of the internal gradient filter.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:algorithm:enabl.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If disabled, this algorithm will be skipped in a run. Please note that this will also disable all algorithms dependent on this algorithm.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:ui-locked.description: Parameter 'Lock location/size' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, lock the location and size of this node. Does not affect automated alignment operations. Will lock the size if the node supports a resize handle. Will prevent deletion of the node by the user. Slots can still be edited/connected and parameters can still be changed.

auto-thresholding/dark-background.description: Parameter 'Dark background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If the background color is dark. Disable this if your image has a bright background.

auto-thresholding/slice-threshold-mode.description:
Parameter 'Multi-slice thresholding' of type with ID 'slice-threshold-mode'. Determines how thresholds are calculated if an image has multiple slices.
  • Apply threshold per slice calculates and applies the threshold for each slice.
  • Combine slice statistics merges the slice histograms into one, which is then used for threshold calculation.
  • Combine thresholds per slice calculates the threshold for each slice. One threshold for all slices is calculated via the math expression.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:bookmarked.description: Parameter 'Bookmark this node' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node is highlighted in the graph editor UI and added into the bookmark list.

auto-thresholding/threshold-annotation-s.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:algorithm:pass-.description:
Parameter 'Pass through' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will pass the input data directly to the output data without any processing. This is different from enabling/disabling the algorithm as this will not disable dependent algorithms.
Please note that setting this parameter via adaptive parameters (if available) does not always yield the expected result. The reason behind this is that pass-through is not trivial for certain nodes. We recommend to use a split node if node execution should be made adaptive.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Custom algorithm name.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:algorithm:runti.description: Parameter 'Partition' of type with ID 'runtime-partition-reference'. Allows to move the node into a different runtime partition, which determine how the workload is executed.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:node:descriptio.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. A custom description

auto-thresholding/threshold-combine-expr.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

auto-thresholding/source-area.description: Parameter 'Calculate threshold based on ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines from which image areas the pixel values used for calculating the thresholds are extracted from.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:local-paralleli.description: Parameter 'Local parallelization' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the partition- and run-wide parallelization settings and apply parallelization only on this node.

auto-thresholding/threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:data-batch-gene.description: Parameter 'Merge same annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if different values for the same annotation columns are encountered.

Parameter 'Grouping method' of type with ID 'column-matching'. Algorithms with multiple inputs require to match the incoming data to data sets. This allows you to determine how interesting data annotation columns are extracted from the incoming data. Union matches using the union of annotation columns. Intersection intersects the sets of available columns. You can also customize which columns should be included or excluded.

Parameter 'Merge same data annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:data-annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if different values for the same data annotation columns are encountered.

Parameter 'Custom grouping columns' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Only used if 'Grouping method' is set to 'Custom'. Determines which annotation columns are referred to group data sets. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"
Parameter 'Skip incomplete data sets' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, incomplete data sets are silently skipped. Otherwise an error is displayed if such a configuration is detected.
Parameter 'Custom annotation matching method' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression used to compare two annotation sets.
Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. Limits which iteration steps are generated. The first index is zero.
Parameter 'Force flow graph solver' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, disable the faster dictionary-based solver. Use this if you experience unexpected behavior.

Parameter 'Annotation matching method' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-matching-method'. Allows to customize when two annotation sets are considered as equal. By default, non-empty annotation values should match exactly. You can also use a custom expression, instead.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:parameter-slot-.description: Parameter 'Multiple parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be an additional slot that consumes parameter data sets. The algorithm then will be applied for each of this parameter sets.
Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

auto-thresholding/jipipe:adaptive-parame.description: Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Enable adaptive parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, you can use custom expressions to generate parameters. Please note that this will disable parallelization.

Parameter 'Overridden parameters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression:string:pair-list'. Here you can override the value of parameters based on annotations. The 'Value' settings should return a value for the parameter type or a JSON string that can be converted into a valid parameter value. The 'Parameter key' setting determines to which parameter this value is written to.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Radius of the filter in pixels

filaments-set-vertex-calibration-from-im.name: Set filament vertex physical sizes from image

min-num-points.description: Parameter 'Minimum number of points' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Minimum number of points needed for a cluster. If set to zero or negative, it will be roughly estimated as the number of dimensions + 1. We recommend to tune this value manually.

epsilon.description: Parameter 'Epsilon' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Maximum radius of the neighborhood to be considered. If set to zero or negative, it will be roughly estimated as 5% of the dataset’s bounding box diagonal. We recommend to tune this value manually.

input-columns.description: Parameter 'Input columns' of type with ID 'table-column-source-list'. The list of columns that will be used to create the points. Please note that string columns and NA/infinite values are automatically replaced with zeroes.

output-settings/output-column-cluster-id.description: Parameter 'Cluster ID column' of type with ID 'optional-string'. The column where the cluster ID will be written to. The first index will be 1.

output-settings/output-column-cluster-si.description: Parameter 'Cluster size column' of type with ID 'optional-string'. The column where the cluster size will be written to

clustering-settings/distance-measure.description: Parameter 'Distance measure' of type with ID 'table-statistics-distance-measures'. The distance measure to use

clustering-settings/max-iterations.description: Parameter 'Maximum iterations' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The maximum number of iterations to run the algorithm for. If negative, no maximum will be used.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

index-column.description: Parameter 'Add ROI index' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, add a column with the ROI index

name-column.description: Parameter 'Add ROI name' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, add a column with the ROI name

id-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with OMERO image ID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, adds the OMERO image ID as annotation

name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with image name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Optional annotation type where the image title is written.

description-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with image description' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Optional annotation type where the image description is written.

annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how newly generated annotations are merged with existing ones

override-credentials.description: Parameter 'Override OMERO credentials' of type with ID 'optional-omero-credentials'. Allows to override the OMERO credentials provided in the JIPipe application settings

tag-to-annotation-importer/tags-to-list-.description: Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, write all tag names into a single annotation (as list). The name of the annotation is determined by the value of this parameter

Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation (filter)' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter out tags that will be included into 'Convert tags into single annotation' This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/fi.description: Parameter 'Keep kv-pair if' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to filter out key-value pairs from being converted into annotations

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/an.description: Parameter 'Annotation name' of type with ID 'expression'. The name of the generated annotation
Parameter 'Annotation value' of type with ID 'expression'. The value of the generated annotation

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/en.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determined if the import of key-value pairs is enabled

ome-exporter-settings/no-lut.description: Parameter 'No LUT' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, no LUT information is saved into the OME TIFF file

ome-exporter-settings/split-z.description: Parameter 'Split Z into files' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each Z is written into its own file

ome-exporter-settings/compression.description: Parameter 'Compression' of type with ID 'ome-tiff-compression'. The compression method that should be applied

ome-exporter-settings/split-c.description: Parameter 'Split channels into files' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each channel is written into its own file

ome-exporter-settings/split-t.description: Parameter 'Split frames into files' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each time frame is written into its own file

method.description:
Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'org.hkijena.jipipe.plugins.imagejalgorithms.nodes.threshold.local.LocalAutoThreshold2DAlgorithm.Method'. Determines the thresholding method:

Mean (threshold is mean local pixel value)
Median (threshold is median local pixel value)
MidGray (threshold is average of min and max pixel values)
Otsu (threshold is the local otsu threshold)

dark-background.description: Parameter 'Dark background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If the background color is dark. Disable this if your image has a bright background.

modifier.description: Parameter 'Modifier' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. This value is subtracted from each calculated local threshold.

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The radius of the circular local window.

sort-order.description: Parameter 'Filters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression:sort-order:pair-list'. Allows you determine by which columns the table is sorted. The order determines the sorting priority. Columns can be matched multiple times.

use-natural-sort-order.description: Parameter 'Sort strings by natural order' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, strings are sorted by natural order (e.g. 1, 2, 15 100, ...). If disabled, strings are sorted lexicographically (e.g. 1, 15, 100, 2)

reverse-sort-order.description: Parameter 'Reverse sort order' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the sort order is reversed.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

rectangles.description: Parameter 'Rectangles' of type with ID 'margin-list'. List of rectangles

split.description: Parameter 'Split output' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each rectangle is put into its own ROI list

order-metadata-key.description: Parameter 'Set order metadata' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, sets the order (1 at the starting point) of the vertex within the line

selector-function.description: Parameter 'Start point selector' of type with ID 'expression'. Applied per linear component. Given are the two end points (first and second). The function should return "first" or "second" depending on which of the end points should be

distance-metadata-key.description: Parameter 'Set distance from start metadata' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, sets the traveled distance from the start point (in pixels)

max-distance-metadata-key.description: Parameter 'Set line length metadata' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, sets the full line length (in pixels)

ignore-input-text-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore input text annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, incoming text annotations from the input folder will not be passed to the output.

data-annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Merge imported data annotations' of type with ID 'jipipe:data-annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens when imported data has the same data annotations as the input folder.

internal-path.description: Parameter 'Internal path' of type with ID 'string'. The path inside the ZIP file that contains the data table directory

ignore-imported-data-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore imported data annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, annotations from imported data annotations are ignored.

ignore-imported-text-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore imported text annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, annotations from imported text annotations are ignored.

text-annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Merge imported text annotations' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens when imported data has the same text annotations as the input folder.

ignore-input-data-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore input data annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, incoming data annotations from the input folder will not be passed to the output.

generated-image-type.description: Parameter 'Output image type' of type with ID 'data-type'. The image type that is generated.

key.description: Parameter 'Project user directory key' of type with ID 'string'. The key of the project user directory. You can configure directories by navigating to Project > Project settings > User directories (or alternatively to Project > Project overview > User directories)

chamfer-weights.description: Parameter 'Chamfer weights' of type with ID 'ij-math-chamfer-distance-map-2d:weights'. Determines the Chamfer weights for this distance transform

normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize weights' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Indicates whether the resulting distance map should be normalized (divide distances by the first chamfer weight)

output-type.description: Parameter 'Output type' of type with ID 'data-type'. Determines the output type. You can choose between an  16-bit or 32-bit.

spot-detector-parameters/target-channel.description:
Parameter 'Target channel' of type with ID 'int'. This detector creates spots from a black and white mask.

More precisely, all the pixels in the designated channel that have a value strictly larger than 0 are considered as part of the foreground, and used to build connected regions. In 2D, spots are created with the (possibly simplified) contour of the region. In 3D, a spherical spot is created for each region in its center, with a volume equal to the region volume.

The spot quality stores the object area or volume in pixels.

spot-detector-parameters/simplify-contou.description:
Parameter 'Simplify contours' of type with ID 'boolean'. This detector creates spots from a black and white mask.

More precisely, all the pixels in the designated channel that have a value strictly larger than 0 are considered as part of the foreground, and used to build connected regions. In 2D, spots are created with the (possibly simplified) contour of the region. In 3D, a spherical spot is created for each region in its center, with a volume equal to the region volume.

The spot quality stores the object area or volume in pixels.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

series-name.description:
Parameter 'Series name' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to generate the series name This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

input-columns/Value.description: Parameter 'Value' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Values to generate a histogram from. (Numeric column)

plot-parameters/value-axis-minimum.description: Parameter 'Value axis minimum' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Minimum of the value axis values. If disabled or infinite, the value is calculated automatically.

plot-parameters/bins.description: Parameter 'Bins' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of bins

plot-parameters/bin-axis-maximum.description: Parameter 'Bin axis maximum' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Maximum of the bin axis values. If disabled or infinite, the value is calculated automatically.

plot-parameters/title-font-size.description: Parameter 'Title font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Font size of the title

plot-parameters/bin-axis-label.description: Parameter 'Bin axis label' of type with ID 'string'. Label of the X-axis

plot-parameters/export-width.description: Parameter 'Export width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Width of the output image generated via an export

draw-over.description: Parameter 'Draw over reference' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, draw the ROI over the reference image.

draw-outline-mode.description: Parameter 'Draw outline' of type with ID 'roi-element-drawing-mode'. If enabled, draw a white outline of the ROI

override-line-width.description: Parameter 'Override line width' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, the line width will be overridden by this value. If a ROI has a line width equal or less than zero, it will fall back to this value.

image-area.description: Parameter 'Image area' of type with ID 'margin'. Allows modification of the output image width and height.

ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI will show outside their channel (C) layer

prefer-associated-image.description: Parameter 'Prefer ROI-associated images' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. ROI can carry a reference to an image (e.g. the thresholding input). With this option enabled, this image is preferred to generating an output based on the pure ROIs.

fill-outline-mode.description: Parameter 'Draw filled outline' of type with ID 'roi-element-drawing-mode'. If enabled, fill the ROI areas

override-fill-color.description: Parameter 'Override fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, the fill color will be overridden by this value. If a ROI has no fill color, it will always fall back to this color.

override-line-color.description: Parameter 'Override line color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, the line color will be overridden by this value. If a ROI has no line color, it will always fall back to this color.

ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI will show outside their frame (T) layer

ignore-z.description: Parameter 'Ignore Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI will show outside their Z layer

clij-parameters/scaling_factor_y.description: Parameter 'Scaling Factor Y' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/scaling_factor_z.description: Parameter 'Scaling Factor Z' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/scale_to_center.description: Parameter 'Scale To Center' of type with ID 'boolean'. null

invert.description: Parameter 'Invert colors' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert colors before applying the filter. This is useful if you look for bright structures within a dark background.

alpha.description: Parameter 'Correction constant' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Adjusts the sensitivity to deviation from a plate-like structure.

scales.description: Parameter 'Scales' of type with ID 'double-list'. List of scales to test. They are also referenced as 'Sigmas'.

module-parameters/minpixelfraction.description: Parameter 'Min Pixel Fraction' of type with ID 'double'.

dark-background.description: Parameter 'Dark background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If the background color is dark. Disable this if your image has a bright background.

slice-threshold-mode.description:
Parameter 'Multi-slice thresholding' of type with ID 'slice-threshold-mode'. Determines how thresholds are calculated if an image has multiple slices.
  • Apply threshold per slice calculates and applies the threshold for each slice.
  • Combine slice statistics merges the slice histograms into one, which is then used for threshold calculation.
  • Combine thresholds per slice calculates the threshold for each slice. One threshold for all slices is calculated via the math expression.

threshold-annotation-strategy.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

threshold-combine-expression.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

source-area.description: Parameter 'Calculate threshold based on ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines from which image areas the pixel values used for calculating the thresholds are extracted from.

threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

operation-dimension-z.description:
Parameter 'Dimension Z' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-split-into-connected-components:dimension-operation'. Operation for the Z (Slice) dimension. There are three different modes:
  • Followed dimensions will be tracked
  • ROI can be split across a dimension. The components are then generated per plane in this dimension.
  • Merging is the opposite of splitting: If a dimension is merged, it will be collapsed during the calculation, meaning that all associated ROI will be put together

graph-postprocessing.description:
Parameter 'Graph postprocessing' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that allows to modify the overlap graph (each node represents a ROI, edges represent an overlap). The connected components of the overlap graph are later converted into their respective connected components.This is applied after all processing steps. If not empty, this expression is executed for each node in the overlap graph. Return one of following variables to determine what should be done with the node:
  • KEEP leaves the node alone (Alternative: Return boolean true)
  • REMOVE removes node (Alternative: Return boolean false)
  • ISOLATE removes all edges of the node

split-at-junctions.description: Parameter 'Split at junctions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, non-unique connections (between multiple objects) are deleted. The algorithm removes all connections to ROI with a higher z/c/t (depending on the mode). This solver can be disabled via the 'Try to solve junctions' parameter.If no solution can be found this way, all connections around the node are removed (making a single component).

operation-dimension-t.description:
Parameter 'Dimension T' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-split-into-connected-components:dimension-operation'. Operation for the T (Time) dimension. There are three different modes:
  • Followed dimensions will be tracked
  • ROI can be split across a dimension. The components are then generated per plane in this dimension.
  • Merging is the opposite of splitting: If a dimension is merged, it will be collapsed during the calculation, meaning that all associated ROI will be put together

try-solve-junctions.description: Parameter 'Try to solve junctions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the 'Split at junctions' function will try to remove connections to ROI with a higher z/c/t (depending on the mode). This is not trivial, as ROIs can also be present in any dimension and dimensions can be merged.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

component-name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with component' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with the numeric component index is generated.

overlap-filter-measurements.description:
Parameter 'Overlap filter measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. Measurements extracted for the overlap filter.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

overlap-filter.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter' of type with ID 'expression'. This filter is applied to any combination of ROIs that have an overlap. Please open the expression builder to see a list of all available variables. If the filter is empty, no filtering is applied.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

columns.description:
Parameter 'Generated parameter columns' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. Each item within this list defines a column in the generated parameter table. You must set the following properties:
  • Key: the internal key of the parameter that is referred to. You can look it up by clicking the (?) icon next to a parameter and reading the technical info in the documentation that will be shown.
  • Type: the data type of the parameter. Please ensure to select the correct type.
  • Values: an expression that should generate a string or an array/list of values. The expected value(s) are dependent on the 'Values are JSON' parameter.
  • Values are JSON: determines whether the values from the 'Values' parameter are in JSON format or already of the expected object type. If 'Values are JSON' is enabled and non-string values are returned (e.g., a map), they will be automatically converted to JSON.
If the columns have different sizes, the last values of the smaller columns will be repeated to fill the whole table. Duplicate keys are overwritten based on the order.

override-credentials.description: Parameter 'Override OMERO credentials' of type with ID 'optional-omero-credentials'. Allows to override the OMERO credentials provided in the JIPipe application settings

dataset-ids.description: Parameter 'Dataset IDs' of type with ID 'long-list'. List of dataset IDs

ignore-missing-roi-indices.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing ROI indices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, missing ROI indices are ignored (a log message is written)

clear-before-write.description: Parameter 'Clear properties before write' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, all existing ROI properties are deleted before writing the new properties

roi-index-column.description:
Parameter 'ROI index' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Determines how the table column is associated to the index of the ROI (zero-based). Defaults to assuming that the row index is the ROI index. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)
