rotate.description: Parameter 'Rotate 90 degrees' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

plane-start-location.description: Parameter 'Start at' of type with ID 'anchor'. Determines where the reslice will start. For example, if you choose the top location, the reslice plane will wander from north to south.

flip-vertical.description: Parameter 'Flip vertically' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

no-interpolation.description: Parameter 'Avoid interpolation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, use 1 pixel spacing if possible.

force-2d.description: Parameter 'Force 2D' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each 2D image slice is processed individually.

integration-scale.description: Parameter 'Integration scale' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel used for smoothing the elements of the structure tensor. Must be larger than zero. In order to enforce physical isotropy, for each dimension, the scale is divided by the size of the image elements (aspect ratio) in that dimension.

isotropic-gaussian.description: Parameter 'Isotropic Gaussian smoothing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use the voxel width, height, and depth specified in the image properties (calibration). That is, each dimension is assigned its own smoothing scale, computed by dividing the scale as specified in the plugin dialog by the specified pixel/voxel size for that dimension.

smoothing-scale.description: Parameter 'Smoothing scale' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The standard deviation of the Gaussian derivative kernels used for computing the elements of the structure tensor. Must be larger than zero. In order to enforce physical isotropy, for each dimension, the scale is divided by the size of the image elements (aspect ratio) in that dimension.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

annotate-with-channel-index.description: Parameter 'Annotate with channel-index' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output slot name is added as annotation

ignore-missing-channels.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm silently skips invalid assignments like extracting the 4th channel of a 2-channel image. If disabled, an error will be thrown if such a condition is detected.

slot-name-annotation-column.description: Parameter 'Slot name annotation column' of type with ID 'string'. The annotation name that is used if 'Annotate with slot names' is enabled

channel-index-annotation-column.description: Parameter 'Channel index annotation column' of type with ID 'string'. The annotation name that is used if 'Annotate with channel index' is enabled

annotate-with-slot-name.description: Parameter 'Annotate with slot names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the output slot name is added as annotation

clij-parameters/sigmaX.description: Parameter 'Sigma X' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/sigmaY.description: Parameter 'Sigma Y' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/sigmaZ.description: Parameter 'Sigma Z' of type with ID 'double'. null

split-by-track.description: Parameter 'Split by track' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, split the filaments by their track ID (if available)

track-annotation-name.description: Parameter 'Annotate with track ID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, add the track ID as annotation to the generated filaments

z-annotation-name.description: Parameter 'Annotate with Z' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, add the Z of the track as annotation. Only used when generating filaments per Z.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override environment' of type with ID 'optional-tsoax-environment'. If enabled, override the TSOAX environment for this node

convergence/check-period.description: Parameter 'Check period' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Specifies the cycle of checking for convergence in number of iterations. A value of 100 means a snake is checked for convergence every 100 iterations. (see the last sentence of Section 2.1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015)

convergence/maximum-iterations.description: Parameter 'Maximum iterations' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Specifies the maximum number of iterations allowed in each snake evolution

convergence/change-threshold.description: Parameter 'Change threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (in pixels) specifies the threshold of change for a snake to be converged. A value of 0.05 means a snake is converged if every snake point drifts less than 0.05 pixels since last check for convergence (see the last sentence of Section 2.1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015)

convergence/minimum-snake-length.description: Parameter 'Minimum snake length' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. (in pixels) specifies the minimum length of a resultant snake. Increase the value to eliminate hair-like snake structures as well as to avoid the snakes picking up actin patches in yeast images

evolution/radial-near.description: Parameter 'Radial near' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Together with other radial parameters, define the local annulus from which magnitude of stretching forces (see Section 2.1.1 and Figure 3 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015) and local image SNR are computed.

evolution/overlap-threshold.description: Parameter 'Overlap threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The distance threshold that snakes are considered overlapping

evolution/c.description: Parameter 'c (track continuity)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight by which snakes detected at nearby locations in space are assigned to the same track, as a function of the number of time frames separating them. The value of 1/c is the number of frames beyond which the probability of assigning snakes to the same track decreases exponentially with frame number separation. This parameter should be increased (up to order 1) to improve track continuity over successive frames.

evolution/damp-z.description: Parameter 'Damp Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Toggles the suppression of snake evolution along the z-axis. This may be useful when anisotropy in PSF along z becomes a problem

evolution/background-z-xy-ratio.description: Parameter 'Background Z/XY ratio' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Defines the anisotropy of the PSF of microscope. It is the spreading of PSF along z-axis relative to that of x and y-axis. Set this parameter to fix the anisotropy in the background intensity calculation

evolution/delta.description: Parameter 'Delta' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The delta (number of snake points apart) for computing snake tip tangent using finite difference. Must be a positive integer

evolution/stretch-factor.description: Parameter 'Stretch factor (bridging)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight of stretching force (see Equation 2 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015). Increasing this value to stretch snakes more in case of under-segmentation.

evolution/external-factor.description: Parameter 'External factor' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight of image forces (image gradient) (see Equation 2 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015). Increasing this value to make snakes follow more closely the local shape of filaments

evolution/grouping.description: Parameter 'Grouping' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Enables the grouping process of snakes at detected junctions prior to tracking. This option can be enabled for tracking of intersecting elongating filaments and disabled for tracking the movement of filament segments in between junction points.

evolution/minimum-angle-for-soac-linking.description: Parameter 'Minimum angle for SOAC linking' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (in radians) is the angular threshold for grouping snakes. The angle between the tangent directions of two snake branches in a clustered junction must be greater than this value to be grouped. Default is 2π/3

evolution/association-threshold.description: Parameter 'Association threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (in pixels). Not documented by original developers.

evolution/grouping-distance-threshold.description: Parameter 'Grouping distance threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The maximum distance that two T-junctions formed after snake evolution can be clustered into one clustered junction for grouping. Large values may help collapsing unwanted T-junctions.

evolution/alpha.description: Parameter 'Alpha (elongation penalty)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight of first order continuity of snake (see Equation 1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015). This term describes the energy penalty to elongate snakes. For images with dim linear structures and bright spots, one may want to use small value of alpha

evolution/number-of-background-radial-se.description: Parameter 'Number of radial sectors' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Together with other radial parameters, define the local annulus from which magnitude of stretching forces (see Section 2.1.1 and Figure 3 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015) and local image SNR are computed.

evolution/grouping-delta.description: Parameter 'Grouping delta' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Specifies the delta (number of snake points apart) for computing tip tangents of dissected snake segments using finite difference. Must be a positive integer

evolution/radial-far.description: Parameter 'Radial far' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Together with other radial parameters, define the local annulus from which magnitude of stretching forces (see Section 2.1.1 and Figure 3 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015) and local image SNR are computed.

evolution/gamma.description: Parameter 'Gamma (evolution step size)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Controls the step size of the iterative process of snake evolution. The smaller gamma is, the faster snakes converge but the result is less accurate (see Equation 6 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015)

evolution/beta.description: Parameter 'Beta (bending penalty)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight of second order continuity of snake (see Eq.1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015). This term describes the snake bending energy penalty. Use larger value to make snakes more straight

initialization/snake-point-spacing.description: Parameter 'Snake point spacing' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (in pixels) specifies the spacing between consecutive snake points (see the end of Section 2.1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015).

initialization/init-z.description: Parameter 'Init z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Toggles the initialization of snakes along z-axis. Uncheck it to eliminate snakes that are perpendicular to filaments due to anisotropic PSF with larger spreading along z-axis.

initialization/ridge-threshold.description: Parameter 'Ridge threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (also “grad-diff”) controls the number of initialized snakes (see Section 2.2.1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015). Decrease this value to initialize more snakes

initialization/intensity-scaling.description: Parameter 'Intensity scaling' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Multiplies the intensity of every pixel such that the range of rescaled intensities lie roughly between 0.0 and 1.0. This allows using a standard range of α, β and other parameters below. Leave fixed for a given set of images. Set this value to 0 for automatic scaling, where the maximum intensity is scaled to exact 1.0.

initialization/minimum-foreground.description: Parameter 'Minimum foreground' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Specifies the range of intensities intended for extraction. Snakes are not initialized where the image intensity is below “background” or above “foreground”. During evolution, stretching force is zero when the intensity at a snake tip is outside this range.

initialization/maximum-foreground.description: Parameter 'Maximum foreground' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Specifies the range of intensities intended for extraction. Snakes are not initialized where the image intensity is below “background” or above “foreground”. During evolution, stretching force is zero when the intensity at a snake tip is outside this range.

initialization/gaussian-std.description: Parameter 'Gaussian std' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (in pixels) controls the amount of Gaussian smoothing in the computation of image gradient (See Section 2.1 and Equation 3 in Xu, T., Vavylonis, D. & Huang, X. 3D actin network centerline extraction with multiple active contours. Medical Image Analysis 18, 272–284 (2014).). Set σ < 0.01 to disable smoothing

clij-parameters/number_of_erotions_and_d.description: Parameter 'Number Of Erotions And Dilations' of type with ID 'double'. null

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge strategy' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how annotations that are added in the Python script are merged into existing annotations.

code.description:
Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'. The Python script to be executed. The script comes with various API functions and variables that allow to communicate with JIPipe:
  • jipipe_inputs is a dict of input slots.
  • jipipe_outputs is a dict of output slots.
  • jipipe_annotations is a dict of annotation variables of the current iteration step.
  • jipipe_variables is a dict of variables passed from the script parameters.
The script is designed to be used with the JIPipe Python API (supplied automatically by default). You can find the full API documentation here: https://jipipe.hki-jena.de/apidocs/python-current/index.html

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Python operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. overlap-filter-measurements.description:
Parameter 'Overlap filter measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. Measurements extracted for the overlap filter.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

roi1/consume-overlap.description: Parameter 'Consume on overlap' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are consumed if an overlap is detected, meaning that no other tested ROI can overlap with it. This is useful if you want to prevent duplicate overlaps (e.g., if you compare automated vs manually segmented blobs).

roi1/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are stored into the output if they do not overlap.

roi1/overlap-filter.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. This filter is applied to any combination of ROIs that have an overlap. You will have three sets of measurements: ROI1, Overlap, and ROI2.'ROI1'and 'ROI2' correspond to a ROI from the input slots,respectively. 'Overlap' is the overlap between these ROI. Please open the expression builder to see a list of all available variables. If the filter is empty, no filtering is applied.

roi1/fast-mode.description: Parameter 'Fast mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, only the bounding box is used to calculate overlaps. This includes overlaps generated with 'Output overlapping regions'. This is faster than the logical AND operation applied by default, but will lead to problems if the ROI are close to each other or very non-box-like.

roi1/output-overlaps.description: Parameter 'Output overlapping regions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the overlapping regions, instead of the ROI are extracted.

roi1/ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different frames are compared

roi1/ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different channels are compared

roi1/enforce-overlap.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter: enforce overlap' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a pair of ROI is not considered for custom filtering if it does not overlap. Disable this setting if you want to implement special behavior.

roi1/enabled.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. You can use this setting to disable generating this output.

roi1/measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available. Measurements will be in the physical sizes of the reference image.

roi2/consume-overlap.description: Parameter 'Consume on overlap' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are consumed if an overlap is detected, meaning that no other tested ROI can overlap with it. This is useful if you want to prevent duplicate overlaps (e.g., if you compare automated vs manually segmented blobs).

roi2/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are stored into the output if they do not overlap.

roi2/overlap-filter.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. This filter is applied to any combination of ROIs that have an overlap. You will have three sets of measurements: ROI1, Overlap, and ROI2.'ROI1'and 'ROI2' correspond to a ROI from the input slots,respectively. 'Overlap' is the overlap between these ROI. Please open the expression builder to see a list of all available variables. If the filter is empty, no filtering is applied.

roi2/fast-mode.description: Parameter 'Fast mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, only the bounding box is used to calculate overlaps. This includes overlaps generated with 'Output overlapping regions'. This is faster than the logical AND operation applied by default, but will lead to problems if the ROI are close to each other or very non-box-like.

roi2/output-overlaps.description: Parameter 'Output overlapping regions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the overlapping regions, instead of the ROI are extracted.

roi2/ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different frames are compared

roi2/ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different channels are compared

roi2/enforce-overlap.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter: enforce overlap' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a pair of ROI is not considered for custom filtering if it does not overlap. Disable this setting if you want to implement special behavior.

roi2/enabled.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. You can use this setting to disable generating this output.

roi2/measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available. Measurements will be in the physical sizes of the reference image.

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Omnipose will train in 3D. Otherwise, JIPipe will prepare the data by splitting 3D data into planes.

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Python operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

label-data-annotation.description:
Parameter 'Label data annotation' of type with ID 'data-annotation-query-expression'. Determines which data annotation contains the labels. Please ensure that the appropriate label data is annotated to the raw input data. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

train-size-model.description: Parameter 'Train size model' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, also train a size model

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description:
Parameter 'Mean diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. The cell diameter. Depending on the model, you can choose following values:
  • Cytoplasm: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 30 pixels.
  • Nuclei: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 17 pixels.
  • Custom: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter appropriate for the model.
  • None: This will train from scratch. You can freely set the diameter. You also can set the diameter to 0 to disable scaling.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Omnipose is used.

epochs.description: Parameter 'Epochs' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of epochs that should be trained.

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-settings/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-settings/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

tweaks-settings/batch-size.description: Parameter 'Batch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'.

tweaks-settings/learning-rate.description: Parameter 'Learning rate' of type with ID 'primitive.double'.

tweaks-settings/concatenate-downsampled-.description: Parameter 'Concatenate downsampled layers' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Concatenate downsampled layers with upsampled layers (off by default which means they are added)

tweaks-settings/use-style-vector.description: Parameter 'Use style vector' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

tweaks-settings/min-train-masks.description: Parameter 'Minimum number of labels per image' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Minimum number of masks an image must have to use in training set. This value is by default 5 in the original Cellpose tool.

tweaks-settings/use-residual-connections.description: Parameter 'Use residual connections' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

tweaks-settings/generate-connected-compo.description: Parameter 'Generate connected components' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, JIPipe will apply a connected component labeling to the annotated masks. If disabled, Cellpose is provided with the labels as-is, which might result in issues with the training.

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clij2.plug.name: CLIJ2 Touch Matrix To Adjacency Matrix

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clij2.plug.name: CLIJ2 Multiply Image Stack With Scalars

clij-parameters/scalars.description: Parameter 'Scalars' of type with ID 'double'. null

file-name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with file name' of type with ID 'optional-string'. Attach the file name of the table file to the output

override-credentials.description: Parameter 'Override OMERO credentials' of type with ID 'optional-omero-credentials'. Allows to override the OMERO credentials provided in the JIPipe application settings

tag-to-annotation-importer/tags-to-list-.description: Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, write all tag names into a single annotation (as list). The name of the annotation is determined by the value of this parameter

Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation (filter)' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter out tags that will be included into 'Convert tags into single annotation' This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/fi.description: Parameter 'Keep kv-pair if' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to filter out key-value pairs from being converted into annotations

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/an.description: Parameter 'Annotation name' of type with ID 'expression'. The name of the generated annotation
Parameter 'Annotation value' of type with ID 'expression'. The value of the generated annotation

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/en.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determined if the import of key-value pairs is enabled

edges-are-background.description: Parameter 'Edges are background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Whether out-of-image pixels are considered background

background-value.description: Parameter 'Background value' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Pixels in the input with this value are interpreted as background.

sigma.description: Parameter 'Sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Gaussian standard deviation. If it is non-positive, it is computed from the size as sigma = 0.3*((ksize-1)*0.5 - 1) + 0.8.

size.description: Parameter 'Size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Size of the filter returned.

clij-parameters/max_distance.description: Parameter 'Max Distance' of type with ID 'double'. null

plot-name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate plots with name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, generated plots are annotated by their name.

input-masks.description: Parameter 'Restrict to 2D ROI/mask' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Allows to change whether to restrict the calculations to a 2D ROI or to a mask

channel2-name.description: Parameter 'Channel 2 name' of type with ID 'string'. The name of the second channel in the results

histogram-name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate histograms with name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, generated histograms are annotated by their name.

channel1-name.description: Parameter 'Channel 1 name' of type with ID 'string'. The name of the first channel in the results

output-warnings.description: Parameter 'Output warnings' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Coloc 2 warnings will be generated as text data

coloc2-settings/li-histogram-channel-2.description: Parameter 'Li Histogram Channel 2' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

coloc2-settings/li-histogram-channel-1.description: Parameter 'Li Histogram Channel 1' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

coloc2-settings/threshold-regression.description: Parameter 'Threshold regression' of type with ID 'ij1-colocalization-coloc2:auto-threshold-regression-implementation'.

coloc2-settings/costes-significance-test.description: Parameter 'Costes' Significance Test' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

coloc2-settings/costes-randomizations.description: Parameter 'Costes randomizations' of type with ID 'primitive.int'.

num-threads.description: Parameter 'Number of threads' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of threads for parallel processing

kernel-y.description: Parameter 'Radius (Y)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the Y direction

kernel-x.description: Parameter 'Radius (X)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the X direction

kernel-z.description: Parameter 'Radius (Z)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the Z direction

prefer-isotropic-filter.description: Parameter 'Prefer isotropic filter (faster)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If possible, utilize a faster implementation using a rolling ball algorithm. Requires that the radii are the same. Not supported on all filters. If isotropic filtering is not possible or disabled, the node will fall back to a parallelized algorithm.

false-positive-error-annotation.description: Parameter 'Calculate false positive error' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the false positive error will be annotated to the data.

total-overlap-annotation.description: Parameter 'Calculate total overlap' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the total overlap will be annotated to the data.

jaccard-index-annotation.description: Parameter 'Calculate Jaccard Index' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the Jaccard Index will be annotated to the data.

volume-similarity-annotation.description: Parameter 'Calculate volume similarity' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the volume similarity will be annotated to the data.

false-negative-error-annotation.description: Parameter 'Calculate false negative error' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the false negative error will be annotated to the data.

dice-coefficient-annotation.description: Parameter 'Calculate Dice Coefficient' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the Dice Coefficient will be annotated to the data.

override-vertex-color.description: Parameter 'Override vertex color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Overrides the mesh color of vertices

force-mesh-length-unit.description: Parameter 'Force mesh length' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If this option and 'Physical dimensions' are enabled, scale the mesh to that 1 unit in its coordinate system is of the unit specified in 'Mesh length'.

split-into-connected-components.description: Parameter 'Split into connected components' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, one mesh group is created per connected component

override-edge-color.description: Parameter 'Override edge color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Overrides the mesh color of edges

physical-sizes.description: Parameter 'Physical sizes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the physical voxel size is considered during the generation of the mesh

override-vertex-radius.description: Parameter 'Override vertex radius' of type with ID 'optional-float'. If enabled, set the radius of vertices.

mesh-name-prefix.description: Parameter 'Mesh name' of type with ID 'string'. The prefix of the mesh

override-edge-radius.description: Parameter 'Override edge radius' of type with ID 'optional-float'. If enabled, set the radius of edge meshes. Otherwise, the radius will interpolate between the radii of the two vertices

mesh-length-unit.description: Parameter 'Mesh length' of type with ID 'quantity-image-unit'. If 'Force mesh length' and 'Physical dimensions' are enabled, scale the mesh to that 1 unit in its coordinate system is of the specified unit.

with-vertices.description: Parameter 'With vertices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, convert vertices into 3D meshes

filter.description:
Parameter 'Only keep data if' of type with ID 'annotate-split-by-annotation:filter'. The filter is an expression that should return a boolean value that indicates whether a data item should be put into the corresponding output.Annotation values are available as variables. If an annotation has spaces special characters, use $ to access its value. Examples: 
#Dataset CONTAINS "Raw" AND condition EQUALS "mock"
TO_NUMBER($"my column") < 10
The expression result will be converted to a string. All existing annotations are available as variables that can be accessed directly, or if they contain special characters or spaces via the $ operator.

enable-filter.description: Parameter 'Enable filter' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines if the filter is enabled

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description:
Parameter 'Mean diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. The cell diameter. Depending on the model, you can choose following values:
  • Cytoplasm: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 30 pixels.
  • Nuclei: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 17 pixels.
  • Custom: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter appropriate for the model.
  • None: This will train from scratch. You can freely set the diameter. You also can set the diameter to 0 to disable scaling.

pretrained-model.description:
Parameter 'Model' of type with ID 'cellpose-pretrained-model'. The pretrained model that should be used. You can either choose one of the models provided by Cellpose, a custom model, or train from scratch. The pre-trained model has influence on the diameter and how the input images should be prepared:
  • Cytoplasm: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 30 pixels.
  • Nuclei: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 17 pixels.
  • Custom: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter appropriate for the model. Don't forget to set the diameter value.
  • None: This will train from scratch. You can freely set the diameter. You also can set the diameter to 0 to disable scaling.
See https://cellpose.readthedocs.io/en/latest/models.html for a description of all models

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose is used.

epochs.description: Parameter 'Epochs' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of epochs that should be trained.

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-settings/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-settings/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

tweaks-settings/batch-size.description: Parameter 'Batch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'.

tweaks-settings/learning-rate.description: Parameter 'Learning rate' of type with ID 'primitive.double'.

tweaks-settings/concatenate-downsampled-.description: Parameter 'Concatenate downsampled layers' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Concatenate downsampled layers with upsampled layers (off by default which means they are added)

tweaks-settings/use-style-vector.description: Parameter 'Use style vector' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

tweaks-settings/min-train-masks.description: Parameter 'Minimum number of labels per image' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Minimum number of masks an image must have to use in training set. This value is by default 5 in the original Cellpose tool.

tweaks-settings/use-residual-connections.description: Parameter 'Use residual connections' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

tweaks-settings/weight-decay.description: Parameter 'Weight decay' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight decay

tweaks-settings/generate-connected-compo.description: Parameter 'Generate connected components' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, JIPipe will apply a connected component labeling to the annotated masks. If disabled, Cellpose is provided with the labels as-is, which might result in issues with the training.

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. The measurements to calculate.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

series-name.description:
Parameter 'Series name' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to generate the series name This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

input-columns/Group.description: Parameter 'Group' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Group for each value. Bars are colored by this column. (String column)

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

output-type.description:
Parameter 'Output type' of type with ID 'ij1-feature-maxima-local-2d:output-type'. Available output types:
SinglePointsOutputs single points
InToleranceOutputs all points around the maximum within the tolerance
SegmentedOutputs a watershed-segmented image

strict.description: Parameter 'Is strict' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. When off, the global maximum is accepted even if all other pixel are less than 'tolerance' below this level. With 'Exclude on Edges' enabled, 'strict' also means that the surrounding of a maximum within 'tolerance' must not include an edge pixel (otherwise, it is enough that there is no edge pixel with the maximum value).

Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

expression.description:
Parameter 'Expression' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. The expression is executed per ROI. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

reverse-sort-order.description: Parameter 'Reverse sort order' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the sort order is reversed

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. Measurements to be included. Please open the expression builder to see the variables generated by the measurements.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

apply-per-slice.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, 3D data is split into 2D slices and the watershed algorithm is applied per slice.

get-dams.description: Parameter 'Get dams' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the dams instead of the wells are returned

connectivity.description: Parameter 'Connectivity' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-2d-3d'. Determines the pixel neighborhood for the flood fill algorithm.

target-area.description: Parameter 'Only apply to ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines where the watershed is applied.

radius1.description: Parameter 'Radius 1' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The first radius.

structure-element.description: Parameter 'Structure element' of type with ID 'ij1-morph:strel'. The shape of the structure element (default is square)

radius2.description: Parameter 'Radius 2' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The second radius.

keep-original-annotations.description: Parameter 'Keep original annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, outputs that were not generated keep their original annotations. Otherwise the merged annotations from the iteration step are used.

size-c.description: Parameter 'Overwrite number of channels (C)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Number of generated channel slices.

size-z.description: Parameter 'Overwrite number of slices (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Number of generated Z slices.

size-t.description: Parameter 'Overwrite number of frames (T)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Number of generated frame slices.

The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

force-2d.description: Parameter 'Force 2D' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated 3D ROI will be 2D. 2D objects will not be connected into 3D objects.

fast.description: Parameter 'Fast mode / Enable 3D' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, multiple ROIs at once will be converted to a mask and passed to the 2D-to-3D converter. This will work only as expected if you do not have overlapping ROIs. Must be enabled if you want 3D ROI.

neighborhood.description: Parameter 'Neighborhood' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-3d'. Determines which neighborhood is used to find connected components.

relative-to-project-dir.description: Parameter 'Output relative to project directory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, outputs will be preferably generated relative to the project directory. Otherwise, JIPipe will store the results in an automatically generated directory. Has no effect if an absolute path is provided.

output-directory.description: Parameter 'Output directory' of type with ID 'path'. Can be a relative or absolute directory. All collected files will be put into this directory. If relative, it is relative to the output slot's output directory that is generated based on the current run's output path.

split-by-slot-name.description: Parameter 'Split by output name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the exporter will attempt to split data by their output name. Has no effect if the exported data table is not an output of a node.

exporter/make-filesystem-compatible.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

exporter/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

exporter/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

hough-array-nms-neighborhood-size.description: Parameter 'Neighborhood size (H)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Neighborhood size for the non-maximum suppression in the space of the hough array (the X axis containing the theta values, Y containing the rho values). Defaults to [width / 50, height / 50] with a minimum of 3 if not enabled. Must be positive odd integers.

roi-name-expression.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'expression'. The name of the generated line ROI is calculated from this expression

fast-segment-detection.description: Parameter 'Fast line segment detection' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, use the classical algorithm for line segment detection that first sorts all points and proceeds to iteratively build each segment. This algorithm may not always have the expected results for all line configurations. If disabled, use a more advanced algorithm based on graph components.

thetas.description: Parameter 'Thetas' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that generates the tested theta angles.

nms-algorithm.description: Parameter 'Non-maximum suppression' of type with ID 'ij1-hough-nms-algorithm'. Determines the algorithm used for the non-maximum suppression. The classic method erases peaks within the Hough array, which is fast, but may not always yield the expected results. Alternatively, detected lines can be erased within the input mask (slower, as the Hough array needs to be regenerated)

num-peaks.description: Parameter 'Number of peaks' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The maximum number of peaks/line segments that will be detected.

fill-gap.description: Parameter 'Fill gap' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Distance between two line segments associated with the same Hough transform bin

peak-threshold.description: Parameter 'Peak threshold' of type with ID 'expression'. Threshold for the Hough array H peak detection. Defaults to 0.5 * MAX(H).

input-nms-neighborhood-size.description: Parameter 'Neighborhood size (I)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Neighborhood size for the non-maximum suppression on the input mask. Set to zero or lower to turn off.

min-length.description: Parameter 'Minimum length' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Minimum line length

slice-threshold-mode.description:
Parameter 'Multi-slice thresholding' of type with ID 'slice-threshold-mode'. Determines how thresholds are calculated if an image has multiple slices.
  • Apply threshold per slice calculates and applies the threshold for each slice.
  • Combine slice statistics calculates statistics for all pixels in the image.
  • Combine thresholds per slice calculates the threshold for each slice. One threshold for all slices is calculated via another math expression.

access-pixels.description: Parameter 'Pixels are available as variables' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the list of all pixels that are considered for the statistics are available as variable 'pixels'. Please note that this will slow down the performance due to necessary conversions.

threshold-annotation-strategy.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

threshold-combine-expression.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

source-area.description: Parameter 'Calculate threshold based on ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines from which image areas the pixel values used for calculating the thresholds are extracted from.

threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

thresholding-function.description: Parameter 'Thresholding function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed for each set of slices. The expression should return a number that will be used as threshold. A pixel is set to 255 if its value is larger than this threshold.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

output-matched-templates.description: Parameter 'Output matched templates' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the measurements are also returned as data table containing the actual template images.

with-non-maxima-suppression.description: Parameter 'Enable non-maxima suppression' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Enables the non-maxima-suppression algorithm that removes bounding boxes that overlap too much.

assemble-templates.description: Parameter 'Assemble templates' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, all matched templates are put at their matched located within the original image. You can choose to overlay them over the original image or generate an empty image.

restrict-to-roi.description: Parameter 'Restrict to ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the template matching is restricted to the bounding box of the supplied ROI.

multi-object-max-bounding-box-overlap.description:
Parameter 'Maximum overlap (N>1)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Ranges from 0.0 to 1.0. Typically in the range 0.1-0.5.
This parameter is for the Non-Maxima Suppression (NMS). It must be adjusted to prevent overlapping detections while keeping detections of close objects. This is the maximal value allowed for the ratio of the Intersection Over Union (IoU) area between overlapping bounding boxes.
If 2 bounding boxes are overlapping above this threshold, then the lower score one is discarded.

template-matching-method.description:
Parameter 'Scoring method' of type with ID 'ij-multi-template-matching:matching-method'. This is the formula used to compute the probability map. The choice is limited to normalised scores to be able to compare different correlation maps when multiple templates are used.
  • Normalised Square Difference (NSD): The pixels in the probability map are computed as the sum of difference between the gray level of pixels from the image patch and from the template normalised by the square root of the sum of the squared pixel values. Therefore a high probability to find the object corresponds to a low score value (not as good as the correlation scores usually).
  • Normalised Cross-Correlation (NCC) : The pixels in the probability map are computed as the sum of the pixel/pixel product between the template and current image patch, also normalized for the difference. a high probability to find the object corresponds to a high score value.
  • 0-mean Normalized Cross-Correlation (0-mean NCC) : The mean value of the template and the image patch is substracted to each pixel value before computing the cross-correlation as above. Like the correlation method, a high probability to find the object corresponds to a high score value. (usually this method is most robust to change of illumination)
Please take a look at the OpenCV documentation for more information: https://www.docs.opencv.org/2.4/doc/tutorials/imgproc/histograms/template_matching/template_matching.html

assemble-templates-output.description: Parameter 'Assemble templates output' of type with ID 'optional-data-type'. If enabled, override the type of the generated assembly. If disabled, it has the same type as the input image.

template-rotations.description:
Parameter 'Additional template rotations' of type with ID 'integer-range'. It is possible to provide a list of clockwise rotations in degrees e.g.: 45,90,180.
As with flipping, performing searches with rotated version of the template increases the probability to find the object if it is expected to be rotated.
If flipping is selected, both the original and flipped versions of the template will be rotated.


NOTE: The template must be of rectangular shape, i.e. for angles not corresponding to "square rotations" (not a multiple of 90°) the rotated template will have some background area which are filled either with the modal gray value of the template (Fiji) or with the pixel at the border in the initial template (KNIME). For higher performance, the non square rotations can be manually generated before calling the plugin and saved as templates.

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

expected-number-of-objects.description: Parameter 'Expected number of objects (N)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. This is the expected number of object expected in each image. The plugin will return N or less predicted locations of the object.

flip-template-horizontally.description:
Parameter 'Flip template horizontally' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Performing additional searches with the transformed template allows to maximize the probability to find the object, if the object is expected to have different orientations in the image.
This is due to the fact that the template matching only looks for translated version of the templates provided.
Possible transformations include flipping (also called mirroring), rotation (below). Scaling is not proposed in the interface but several templates at different scale can be provided in the multiple template version of the plugin.
If vertical and horizontal flipping are selected, then the plugin generates 2 additional templates for the corresponding transformation.

flip-template-vertically.description:
Parameter 'Flip template vertically' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Performing additional searches with the transformed template allows to maximize the probability to find the object, if the object is expected to have different orientations in the image.
This is due to the fact that the template matching only looks for translated version of the templates provided.
Possible transformations include flipping (also called mirroring), rotation (below). Scaling is not proposed in the interface but several templates at different scale can be provided in the multiple template version of the plugin.
If vertical and horizontal flipping are selected, then the plugin generates 2 additional templates for the corresponding transformation.

multi-object-score-threshold.description:
Parameter 'Score threshold (N>1)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Ranges from 0.0 to 1.0. Used for the extrema detection on the score map(s).
If the difference-score is used, only minima below this threshold are collected before NMS (i.e. increase to evaluate more hits).
If a correlation-score is used, only maxima above this threshold are collected before NMS (i.e. decrease to evaluate more hits).

assemble-templates-background.description: Parameter 'Assemble templates background' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, 'Assemble templates' will be put to an image of the given background. Please note that

filaments-set-vertex-intensity-from-imag.name: Set filament vertex value from image

backup-old-value-to-metadata.description: Parameter 'Store old value to metadata' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, store the old value to the specified metadata key

backup-old-value.description: Parameter 'Backup old value' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, backup the value to the specified storage. The backup storage is separate from the metadata storage.

vertex-filter/mask.description: Parameter 'Vertex mask' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Apply the operation to a specific set of vertices

physical-dimension-t.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (T)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image. Please note that only the unit is supported.

physical-dimension-y.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image

physical-dimension-z.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image

physical-dimension-x.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (X)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image

physical-dimension-value.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (Value)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image. Please note that only the unit is supported.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.
