row-selector.description: Parameter 'Match table row if ...' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that matches the ROI to its correct table row. The first match (in table order) is used. If no match is found, the ROI is not changed.

roi-comment.description: Parameter 'ROI comment' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's comment field

center-y.description: Parameter 'Center (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's center Y location

metadata-entries.description: Parameter 'Metadata' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. Allows to set/override additional metadata items

channel-location.description: Parameter 'Channel' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's channel location (0 = all channels)

fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, override the ROI's fill color (you can return a color or a HEX string)

channel-name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with channel name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, create an annotation that contains the channel name as defined by the output slot

ignore-missing-images.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing images' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will silently skip all images where no channel assignment is found. Otherwise, the pipeline/partition/loop will stop.

ignore-annotation-columns-on-trivial-mat.description: Parameter 'Ignore 'Annotation columns' on trivial match' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled and an image is matched to a table with exactly one row, annotation matching is not applied and the singular row is used.

annotation-column-filter.description: Parameter 'Annotation columns' of type with ID 'expression'. Determines the table columns that are matched to the text annotations of the input images. Please ensure that the annotations in the table exactly match the one in your data. By default, all columns starting with a '#' are selected. Use column_name IN ARRAY(...) to set a predefined list of columns.

ignore-missing-channels.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm silently skips invalid assignments like extracting the 4th channel of a 2-channel image. If disabled, an error will be thrown if such a condition is detected.

ignore-missing-outputs.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing outputs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will silently ignore missing output slots. Otherwise, the pipeline/partition/loop will stop.

channel-column-filter.description: Parameter 'Channel columns' of type with ID 'expression'. Determines the table columns that are matched to the output slots of this node. The numeric value of these columns determine which channel index is put to which output. Channel indices are zero-based (0 is the first channel). By default, all columns NOT starting with a '#' are selected. Use column_name IN ARRAY(...) to set a predefined list of columns.

channel-index-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with channel index' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, create an annotation that contains the channel index (starting with zero)

column-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with well column' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the well column

row-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with well row' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the well row

type-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with well type' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the well type

color-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with well color' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the well color

annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how newly generated annotations are merged with existing ones

override-credentials.description: Parameter 'Override OMERO credentials' of type with ID 'optional-omero-credentials'. Allows to override the OMERO credentials provided in the JIPipe application settings

tag-to-annotation-importer/tags-to-list-.description: Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, write all tag names into a single annotation (as list). The name of the annotation is determined by the value of this parameter

Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation (filter)' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter out tags that will be included into 'Convert tags into single annotation' This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/fi.description: Parameter 'Keep kv-pair if' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to filter out key-value pairs from being converted into annotations

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/an.description: Parameter 'Annotation name' of type with ID 'expression'. The name of the generated annotation
Parameter 'Annotation value' of type with ID 'expression'. The value of the generated annotation

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/en.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determined if the import of key-value pairs is enabled

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clijx.clij.name: Cli Jx Pull To Results Table Column

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/column_name.description: Parameter 'Column Name' of type with ID 'string'. null

clij-parameters/append_new_rows.description: Parameter 'Append New Rows' of type with ID 'boolean'. null

If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. slice-threshold-mode.description:
Parameter 'Multi-slice thresholding' of type with ID 'slice-threshold-mode'. Determines how thresholds are calculated if an image has multiple slices.
  • Apply threshold per slice calculates and applies the threshold for each slice.
  • Combine slice statistics calculates statistics for all pixels in the image.
  • Combine thresholds per slice calculates the threshold for each slice. One threshold for all slices is calculated via another math expression.

access-pixels.description: Parameter 'Pixels are available as variables' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the list of all pixels that are considered for the statistics are available as variable 'pixels'. Please note that this will slow down the performance due to necessary conversions.

threshold-annotation-strategy.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

threshold-combine-expression.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

source-area.description: Parameter 'Calculate threshold based on ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines from which image areas the pixel values used for calculating the thresholds are extracted from.

threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

thresholding-function.description: Parameter 'Thresholding function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed for each set of slices. The expression should return a number that will be used as threshold. A pixel is set to 255 if its value is larger than this threshold.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

clij-parameters/filename.description: Parameter 'Filename' of type with ID 'string'. null

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge same annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if an annotation already exists.

generated-annotation.description: Parameter 'Generated annotation' of type with ID 'string'. Select which annotation type is generated for each data row

base-name.description: Parameter 'Base name' of type with ID 'string'. Prefix for the generated annotation. Can be empty.

image-title.description: Parameter 'Image title' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the image title

compare-absolute.description: Parameter 'Compare absolute' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines whether eigenvalues are compared in absolute sense

eigenvalue-selection.description: Parameter 'Eigenvalue' of type with ID 'ij1:eigenvalue-selection-2d'. Allows you to choose whether the largest or smallest Eigenvalues are chosen

smoothing.description: Parameter 'Smoothing' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The smoothing scale at which the required image derivatives are computed. The scale is equal to the standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel used for differentiation and must be larger than zero. In order to enforce physical isotropy, for each dimension, the scale is divided by the size of the image elements (aspect ratio) in that dimension.

draw-over.description: Parameter 'Draw over reference' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, draw the ROI over the reference image.

line-thickness.description: Parameter 'Line thickness' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Only relevant if 'Draw outline' is enabled. Sets the outline thickness.

fill-outline.description: Parameter 'Draw filled outline' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, fill the ROI areas

draw-outline.description: Parameter 'Draw outline' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, draw a white outline of the ROI

original-size-t-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original frame number' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, add the original number of frames as annotation

equal-hyperstack-dimensions.description: Parameter 'Equalize hyperstack dimensions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the hyperstack dimensions are equalized

restore-original-annotations.description: Parameter 'Restore original annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, restore the original annotations of the data

original-width-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original width' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, add the original width as annotation

copy-slices.description: Parameter 'Newly generated slices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how new slices are generated, if needed. You can either repeat the last available slice or make new slices zero/black.

original-size-c-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original channel number' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, add the original number of channels as annotation

original-size-z-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original depth' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, add the original depth (Z) as annotation

original-height-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original height' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, add the original height as annotation

equal-width-and-height.description: Parameter 'Equalize width and height' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the width and height are equalized

scale-algorithm/jipipe:algorithm:enabled.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If disabled, this algorithm will be skipped in a run. Please note that this will also disable all algorithms dependent on this algorithm.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:node:ui-locked.description: Parameter 'Lock location/size' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, lock the location and size of this node. Does not affect automated alignment operations. Will lock the size if the node supports a resize handle. Will prevent deletion of the node by the user. Slots can still be edited/connected and parameters can still be changed.

scale-algorithm/avoid-unnecessary-scalin.description: Parameter 'Avoid unnecessary scaling' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the ImageJ resize method is not called if the image already has the correct size.

scale-algorithm/scale-mode.description: Parameter 'Scale mode' of type with ID 'ij1-transform:scale-mode'. Determines how the image is fit into the output. You can either stretch the image to the new dimensions, fit it inside the boundaries, cut off parts to cover the whole output, or leave the image unchanged (no resize) and just copy it into the output image with a given size

scale-algorithm/jipipe:node:bookmarked.description: Parameter 'Bookmark this node' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node is highlighted in the graph editor UI and added into the bookmark list.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:algorithm:pass-th.description:
Parameter 'Pass through' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will pass the input data directly to the output data without any processing. This is different from enabling/disabling the algorithm as this will not disable dependent algorithms.
Please note that setting this parameter via adaptive parameters (if available) does not always yield the expected result. The reason behind this is that pass-through is not trivial for certain nodes. We recommend to use a split node if node execution should be made adaptive.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:node:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Custom algorithm name.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:algorithm:runtime.description: Parameter 'Partition' of type with ID 'runtime-partition-reference'. Allows to move the node into a different runtime partition, which determine how the workload is executed.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:node:description.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. A custom description

scale-algorithm/background-color.description: Parameter 'Background' of type with ID 'color'. Used if the scale mode is 'Fit'/'Cover'/'Paste'. Determines the background color of the output

scale-algorithm/y-axis.description: Parameter 'Y axis' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. How the Y axis should be scaled. If disabled, the aspect ratio is kept.

scale-algorithm/anchor.description: Parameter 'Placement' of type with ID 'anchor'. Used if the scale mode is 'Fit'/'Cover'/'Paste'. Determines where the image is placed.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:local-paralleliza.description: Parameter 'Local parallelization' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the partition- and run-wide parallelization settings and apply parallelization only on this node.

scale-algorithm/x-axis.description: Parameter 'X axis' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. How the X axis should be scaled. If disabled, the aspect ratio is kept.

scale-algorithm/interpolation-method.description: Parameter 'Interpolation' of type with ID 'ij1-interpolation-method'. The interpolation method

scale-algorithm/jipipe:data-batch-genera.description: Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. Limits which iteration steps are generated. The first index is zero.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:parameter-slot-al.description: Parameter 'Multiple parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be an additional slot that consumes parameter data sets. The algorithm then will be applied for each of this parameter sets.
Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:adaptive-paramete.description: Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Enable adaptive parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, you can use custom expressions to generate parameters. Please note that this will disable parallelization.

Parameter 'Overridden parameters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression:string:pair-list'. Here you can override the value of parameters based on annotations. The 'Value' settings should return a value for the parameter type or a JSON string that can be converted into a valid parameter value. The 'Parameter key' setting determines to which parameter this value is written to.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/constant.description: Parameter 'Constant' of type with ID 'double'. null

annotation-c.description: Parameter 'Annotate with C location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the channel slice of the ROI. The first index is 1. A value of zero indicates that the ROI is located on all C slices.

annotation-merging.description: Parameter 'Annotation merging' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how generated annotations are merged with existing annotations

annotation-width.description: Parameter 'Annotate with ROI width' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the width of the ROI

annotation-height.description: Parameter 'Annotate with ROI height' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the height of the ROI

annotation-t.description: Parameter 'Annotate with T location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the frame slice of the ROI. The first index is 1. A value of zero indicates that the ROI is located on all T slices.

outside-color.description: Parameter 'Set color outside ROI' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, the area outside the ROI (e.g. if you have a circular ROI) is set to this color. Otherwise, the bounding area of the ROI is taken as-is.

annotation-y.description: Parameter 'Annotate with Y location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the top-left Y coordinate of the ROI bounding box.

annotation-x.description: Parameter 'Annotate with X location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the top-left X coordinate of the ROI bounding box.

xy-anchor.description: Parameter 'X/Y location anchor' of type with ID 'anchor'. Determines which X and Y locations are extracted as location.

annotation-z.description: Parameter 'Annotate with Z location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the generated image is annotated with the Z slice of the ROI. The first index is 1. A value of zero indicates that the ROI is located on all Z slices.

series-name.description:
Parameter 'Series name' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to generate the series name This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

rectangle-mode.description: Parameter 'Rectangle processing' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-extract-profile:rectangle-mode'. Determines how rectangular ROIs are handled. RowAverage calculates the average intensity over all y values in the rectangle at each x value. ColumnAverage calculates the average intensity over all x values in the rectangle at each y value. Auto selects RowAverage or ColumnAverage based on the aspect ratio of the rectangle: RowAverage is chosen of the height of the rectangle is greater than its width.

rename-function.description:
Parameter 'Rename function' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. The function is applied to the annotation name to determine its new name. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

annotation-filter.description:
Parameter 'Annotation filter' of type with ID 'annotation-query-expression'. Determines which annotations will be combined. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

annotation-removal-mode.description: Parameter 'Combined annotations' of type with ID 'simplify-annotations:removal-mode'. The operation to apply with annotations that have been combined.

generated-annotation-name.description: Parameter 'Generated annotation' of type with ID 'string'. The name of the generated annotation.

combination-function.description: Parameter 'Combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. Function that determines how annotation names and values are combined.

source-area.description: Parameter 'Extract values from ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines from which image areas the pixel values used for extracting the values

count-column-name.description: Parameter 'Column name: count' of type with ID 'optional-string'. Column name for the count of values

border-distance.description: Parameter 'Border distance' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The maximum distance to the border (defaults to zero)

split-by-slot-name.description: Parameter 'Split by output name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the exporter will attempt to split data by their output name. Has no effect if the exported data table is not an output of a node.

exporter/make-filesystem-compatible.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

exporter/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

exporter/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

code.description: Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'. Access to the iteration step is done via a variable 'data_batch' that provides access to all input and output data, as well as annotations.Input slots can be accessed from variables 'input_slots' (array), 'input_slots_map' (map from name to slot). The first (and only) input slot is also accessible via the 'input_slot' variable. Output slots can be accessed from variables 'output_slots' (array), 'output_slots_map' (map from name to slot). A variable 'progress_info' provides the current progress logger instance.

force-2d.description: Parameter 'Force 2D' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each 2D image slice is processed individually.

compare-absolute.description: Parameter 'Compare absolute' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines whether eigenvalues are compared in absolute sense

isotropic-gaussian.description: Parameter 'Isotropic Gaussian smoothing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use the voxel width, height, and depth specified in the image properties (calibration). That is, each dimension is assigned its own smoothing scale, computed by dividing the scale as specified in the plugin dialog by the specified pixel/voxel size for that dimension.

smoothing-scale.description: Parameter 'Smoothing scale' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The smoothing scale at which the required image derivatives are computed. The scale is equal to the standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel used for differentiation and must be larger than zero. In order to enforce physical isotropy, for each dimension, the scale is divided by the size of the image elements (aspect ratio) in that dimension.

data-annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Data annotation merge mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:data-annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if there is already an existing data annotation with the same name

data-annotation-filter.description:
Parameter 'Data annotation filter' of type with ID 'data-annotation-query-expression'. Allows to filter only specific data annotations. Set to false to completely ignore all data annotations. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

text-annotation-filter.description:
Parameter 'Text annotation filter' of type with ID 'annotation-query-expression'. Allows to filter only specific text annotations. Set to false to completely ignore all text annotations. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

text-annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Text annotation merge mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if there is already an existing text annotation with the same name

roi:target-area.description: Parameter 'Get statistics from ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines where the algorithm is applied to.

annotations.description: Parameter 'Generated annotations' of type with ID 'table-column-expression-generator-column-processor-list'. Use these expressions to generate the annotations. The expressions contain statistics, as well as incoming annotations of the current image.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

link.description: Parameter 'Create link' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the provided Ilastik model is only linked instead of copied. This is required if the model does contain relative paths.

column.description:
Parameter 'Column' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The column that contains the paths This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

method.description:
Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'ij1-color-set-to-content-aware-2d:method'. The fill method. Currently available are:
  • Closest pixel: finds the closest pixel to the each masked pixel and uses that value.
  • Mean pixel value: finds the mean pixel value outside the mask (per channel for color images)
  • Median pixel value: finds the median pixel value outside the mask (per channel for color images)

The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

center.description: Parameter 'Center at location' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the calculated (x,y) location will be the center of the object

arc-width.description: Parameter 'Arc width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. If set to a value larger than zero, a rounded rectangle is created

arc-height.description: Parameter 'Arc height' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. If set to a value larger than zero, a rounded rectangle is created

definitions.description: Parameter 'Rectangles' of type with ID 'margin-list'. The rectangular ROI to be drawn

roi-properties/position-t.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Frame)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different frame/time-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all frames.

roi-properties/line-width.description: Parameter 'Line width' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Allows to change the line width when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/position-z.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Z)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different Z-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all slices.

roi-properties/roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to change the ROI name

roi-properties/fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the fill color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/line-color.description: Parameter 'Line color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the line color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/position-c.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Channel)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different channel-position. Please note that 'Channel' refers to an image slice and not to a pixel channel. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all channels.

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:algorithm:enab.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If disabled, this algorithm will be skipped in a run. Please note that this will also disable all algorithms dependent on this algorithm.

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:node:ui-locked.description: Parameter 'Lock location/size' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, lock the location and size of this node. Does not affect automated alignment operations. Will lock the size if the node supports a resize handle. Will prevent deletion of the node by the user. Slots can still be edited/connected and parameters can still be changed.

gaussian-algorithm/sigma-y.description: Parameter 'Sigma (Y)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Standard deviation of the Gaussian (pixels) in Y direction. If zero or less, sigma in X direction is automatically used instead.

gaussian-algorithm/sigma-x.description: Parameter 'Sigma (X)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Standard deviation of the Gaussian (pixels) in X direction.

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:node:bookmarke.description: Parameter 'Bookmark this node' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node is highlighted in the graph editor UI and added into the bookmark list.

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:algorithm:pass.description:
Parameter 'Pass through' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will pass the input data directly to the output data without any processing. This is different from enabling/disabling the algorithm as this will not disable dependent algorithms.
Please note that setting this parameter via adaptive parameters (if available) does not always yield the expected result. The reason behind this is that pass-through is not trivial for certain nodes. We recommend to use a split node if node execution should be made adaptive.

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:node:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Custom algorithm name.

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:algorithm:runt.description: Parameter 'Partition' of type with ID 'runtime-partition-reference'. Allows to move the node into a different runtime partition, which determine how the workload is executed.

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:node:descripti.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. A custom description

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:local-parallel.description: Parameter 'Local parallelization' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the partition- and run-wide parallelization settings and apply parallelization only on this node.

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:data-batch-gen.description: Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. Limits which iteration steps are generated. The first index is zero.

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:parameter-slot.description: Parameter 'Multiple parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be an additional slot that consumes parameter data sets. The algorithm then will be applied for each of this parameter sets.
Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

gaussian-algorithm/jipipe:adaptive-param.description: Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Enable adaptive parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, you can use custom expressions to generate parameters. Please note that this will disable parallelization.

Parameter 'Overridden parameters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression:string:pair-list'. Here you can override the value of parameters based on annotations. The 'Value' settings should return a value for the parameter type or a JSON string that can be converted into a valid parameter value. The 'Parameter key' setting determines to which parameter this value is written to.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

output-key-column.description: Parameter 'Output column (keys)' of type with ID 'string'. The table column where the keys will be written to

output-foreground-column.description: Parameter 'Output column (integrated foreground)' of type with ID 'string'. The table column where the integrated foreground values will be written to

output-background-column.description: Parameter 'Output column (integrated background)' of type with ID 'string'. The table column where the integrated background values will be written to

invert-threshold.description: Parameter 'Thresholding mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how the thresholding behaves.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

ignore-input-text-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore input text annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, incoming text annotations from the input folder will not be passed to the output.

node-id.description: Parameter 'Node alias ID' of type with ID 'string'. The unique identifier of the node that contains the output. You can either use the 'Set output slot' button to auto-configure this value or look up the node ID in the help of the node. Please note that this is not the node type ID.

data-annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Merge imported data annotations' of type with ID 'jipipe:data-annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens when imported data has the same data annotations as the input folder.

slot-name.description: Parameter 'Slot name' of type with ID 'string'. The name of the output slot within the targeted node. You can use the 'Set output slot' button to auto-configure or just type in the name of the output slot.

ignore-imported-data-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore imported data annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, annotations from imported data annotations are ignored.

ignore-imported-text-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore imported text annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, annotations from imported text annotations are ignored.

compartment-id.description: Parameter 'Compartment alias ID' of type with ID 'string'. The ID of the compartment, where the data is located. You can use the 'Set output slot' button to auto-configure or just type in the name of the output slot.

text-annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Merge imported text annotations' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens when imported data has the same text annotations as the input folder.

ignore-input-data-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore input data annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, incoming data annotations from the input folder will not be passed to the output.

columns.description: Parameter 'Columns' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. The number of columns to generate. If disabled, columns are generated automatically.

scale.description: Parameter 'Scale' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Scale to apply to each tile

font-size.description: Parameter 'Font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The size of labels

rows.description: Parameter 'Rows' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. The number of rows to generate. If disabled, rows are generated automatically.

background-color.description: Parameter 'Background color' of type with ID 'color'. The color of backgrounds

foreground-color.description: Parameter 'Foreground color' of type with ID 'color'. The color of labels

border-width.description: Parameter 'Border width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Distance between each tile

canvas-equalizer/background-color.description: Parameter 'Background color' of type with ID 'color'. The color of the outside canvas

canvas-equalizer/y-axis-expression.description: Parameter 'Y axis' of type with ID 'expression'. Defines the size of the output canvas

canvas-equalizer/anchor.description: Parameter 'Anchor' of type with ID 'anchor'. From which point to expand the canvas

canvas-equalizer/x-axis-expression.description: Parameter 'X axis' of type with ID 'expression'. Defines the size of the output canvas

sorting-label-generator/make-filesystem-.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

sorting-label-generator/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

sorting-label-generator/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

label-generator/make-filesystem-compatib.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

label-generator/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

label-generator/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

module-parameters/toaxis.description: Parameter 'To Axis' of type with ID 'int'.

module-parameters/fromaxis.description: Parameter 'From Axis' of type with ID 'int'.

magnification.description: Parameter 'Magnification' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Magnification applied during the rendering

track-visualization/stroke-color-track-f.description: Parameter 'Stroke color track feature' of type with ID 'trackmate-track-feature'. Determines the feature that is utilized for coloring the stroke. Only takes effect if 'Stroke color mode' is set to 'Color per track'

track-visualization/fade-track-range.description: Parameter 'Fade track range' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. If the display mode is set to local track surroundings, sets the fading range

track-visualization/stroke-width.description: Parameter 'Stroke width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Width of the spot stroke

track-visualization/stroke-color-edge-fe.description: Parameter 'Stroke color edge feature' of type with ID 'trackmate-edge-feature'. Determines the feature that is utilized for coloring the stroke. Only takes effect if 'Stroke color mode' is set to 'Color per edge'

track-visualization/track-display-mode.description: Parameter 'Display mode' of type with ID 'trackmate-track-display-mode'. Determines how tracks are displayed

track-visualization/stroke-color.description: Parameter 'Stroke uniform color' of type with ID 'color'. The default color of a spot. Has no effect if the color is calculated from a feature.

track-visualization/stroke-color-mode.description: Parameter 'Stroke color mode' of type with ID 'trackmate-track-drawer:stroke-color'. Determines how the stroke is colored

split-by-track.description: Parameter 'Split by track' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, split the filaments by their track ID (if available)

track-annotation-name.description: Parameter 'Annotate with track ID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, add the track ID as annotation to the generated filaments

Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/minimumValue.description: Parameter 'Minimum Value' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/maximumValue.description: Parameter 'Maximum Value' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/seed.description: Parameter 'Seed' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clij2.plug.name: CLIJ2 Close Index Gaps In Label Map

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

spot-tracker-parameters/max-frame-gap.description:
Parameter 'Max frame gap' of type with ID 'int'. This tracker is an extended version of the Kalman tracker, that adds the possibility to customize linking costs and detect track fusion (segments merging) and track division (segments splitting).

This tracker is especially well suited to objects that move following a nearly constant velocity vector. The velocity vectors of each object can be completely different from one another. But for the velocity vector of one object need not to change too much from one frame to another.

In the frame-to-frame linking step, the classic Kalman tracker infer most likely spot positions in the target frame from growing tracks and link all extrapolated positions against all spots in the target frame, based on the square distance. This advanced version of the tracker allows for penalizing links to spots with different features values using the same framework that of the LAP tracker in TrackMate. Also, after the frame-to-frame linking step, track segments are post-processed to detect splitting and merging events, and perform gap-closing. This is again based on the LAP tracker implementation.

output-directory.description: Parameter 'Output directory' of type with ID 'path'. Can be a relative or absolute directory. All collected files will be put into this directory. If relative, it is relative to the output slot's output directory that is generated based on the current run's output path.

exporter/make-filesystem-compatible.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

exporter/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

exporter/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

ome-exporter-settings/zero-padding.description: Parameter 'Zero-pad file names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, file names are zero-padded

ome-exporter-settings/stack-order.description: Parameter 'Stack order' of type with ID 'import-imagej-bioformats:order'. In which order stacks are saved

ome-exporter-settings/no-lut.description: Parameter 'No LUT' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, no LUT information is saved into the OME TIFF file

ome-exporter-settings/save-roi.description: Parameter 'Save 2D ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are saved into the OME TIFF file

ome-exporter-settings/split-z.description: Parameter 'Split Z into files' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each Z is written into its own file

ome-exporter-settings/compression.description: Parameter 'Compression' of type with ID 'ome-tiff-compression'. The compression method that should be applied

ome-exporter-settings/split-c.description: Parameter 'Split channels into files' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each channel is written into its own file

ome-exporter-settings/split-t.description: Parameter 'Split frames into files' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each time frame is written into its own file

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/scalar.description: Parameter 'Scalar' of type with ID 'double'. null

hollow-vertices.description: Parameter 'Hollow vertices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, draw vertices as hollow spheres

with-edges.description: Parameter 'With edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, edges are converted to ROI

forced-vertex-radius.description: Parameter 'Override vertex radius' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the radius of vertices. Must be at least one.

ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ignore the channel (C) location of each vertex

ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ignore the frame (T) location of each vertex

ignore-z.description: Parameter 'Ignore Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the Z location of each each vertex will be ignored

forced-line-thickness.description: Parameter 'Override edge thickness' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, set the thickness of edges. Must be at least zero.

with-vertices.description: Parameter 'With vertices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, vertices are converted to ROI

processors.description: Parameter 'Processors' of type with ID 'integrating-table-column-processor-list'. Defines which columns are processed

scale-x.description: Parameter 'Scale X' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Scales the ROI. Please note that the scale will not be saved inside the ROI.

scale-y.description: Parameter 'Scale Y' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Scales the ROI. Please note that the scale will not be saved inside the ROI.

center-scale.description: Parameter 'Center scale' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, each ROI is scaled relative to its center.

matrix.description: Parameter 'Matrix' of type with ID 'matrix2d-float'. The convolution matrix

normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize kernel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

filter.description: Parameter 'Keep label if' of type with ID 'expression'. Filtering expression. This is applied per label. Click the 'Edit' button to see all available variables you can test for (note: requires from you to enable the corresponding measurement!).An example for an expression would be 'Area > 200 AND Mean > 10'.Annotations are available as variables.

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. The measurements to calculate.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

concatenate.description: Parameter 'Concatenate compatible series' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Allows multiple image series to be joined end to end.  Example: You want to join two sequential timelapse series.

split-channels.description: Parameter 'Split channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Each channel is opened as a separate stack.  This option is especially useful if you want to merge the channels into a specific order, rather than automatically assign channels to the order of RGB. The bit depth is preserved.

series-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with series' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, attempt to extract the series number (starting with 1) from the last part of the image title. The algorithm will take a look at the end of the title and expects a string '#[series]'. If none could be found, 1 will be returned.

split-time-points.description: Parameter 'Split time points' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Timelapse data will be opened as a separate stack for each timepoint.

stack-order.description: Parameter 'Stack order' of type with ID 'import-imagej-bioformats:order'.

color-mode.description:
Parameter 'Color mode' of type with ID 'import-imagej-bioformats:color-mode'. Color mode - Visualizes channels according to the specified scheme.  Possible choices are:
Default - Display channels as closely as possible to how they are stored in the file.
Composite - Open as a merged composite image. Channels are colorized according to metadata present in the dataset (if any), or in the following default order: 1=red, 2=green, 3=blue, 4=gray, 5=cyan, 6=magenta, 7=yellow.
Colorized - Open with each channel in a separate plane, colorized according to metadata present in the dataset (if any), or in the default order (see Composite above).
Grayscale - Open with each channel in a separate plane, displayed in plain grayscale.
Custom - Same as Colorized, except that you can explicitly choose how to colorize each channel.
Note that ImageJ can only composite together 7 or fewer channels. With more than 7 channels, some of the modes above may not work.

open-all-series.description: Parameter 'Open all series' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, all series of a multi-series file are imported. This will override the series filter.

stitch-tiles.description: Parameter 'Stitch tiles' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Stitch tiles - Performs very simple stitching of tiles.  The overlap is assumed to be 0%, and the stage coordinates are used to determine the proper placement of the tiles. This is useful for seeing a quick preview of what the stitched image might look like, but is not a substitute for proper stitching plugins such as the 2D/3D Stitching plugin.

crop-regions.description: Parameter 'Crop regions' of type with ID 'rectangle-list'.

swap-dimensions.description: Parameter 'Swap dimensions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Allows reassignment of dimensional axes (e.g., channel, Z and time).  Bio-Formats is supposed to be smart about handling multidimensional image data, but in some cases gets things wrong. For example, when stitching together a dataset from multiple files using the Group files with similar names option, Bio-Formats may not know which dimensional axis the file numbering is supposed to represent. It will take a guess, but in case it guesses wrong, you can use Swap dimensions to reassign which dimensions are which.

auto-scale.description: Parameter 'Auto scale' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Stretches the channel histograms to each channel's global minimum and maximum value throughout the stack. Does not alter underlying values in the image.  If unselected, all channel histograms are scaled to the image's digitization bit depth. Note that this range may be narrower than the bit depth of the file. For example, a 16-bit file may contain intensities digitized to 16-bits (0-65,535), to 12-bits (0-4,095), to 10-bits (0-1,023), etc.  Note that you can use the Brightness & Contrast or Window/Level controls to adjust the contrast range regardless of whether this option is used. The histogram will provide min/max values in the stack.

title-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with title' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Optional annotation type where the image title is written.

series-to-import.description: Parameter 'Series to import' of type with ID 'int-list'. Some images can contain multiple sub-images (series) that do not necessarily have the same size and other properties. Use this list to control which series should be imported. Overridden by 'Open all series'. The first index is zero.

extract-rois.description: Parameter 'Extract 2D ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROIs are extracted from OME data.

split-focal-planes.description: Parameter 'Split focal planes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Each focal plane is opened as a separate stack.

crop.description: Parameter 'Crop images' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Image planes may be cropped during import to conserve memory. Use the 'Crop regions' parameter to define which regions should be cropped.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

annotate-max-hue.description: Parameter 'Max threshold annotation (H)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the max hue threshold

hue-threshold.description: Parameter 'Hue threshold' of type with ID 'number-range-integer'. Thresholds the hue channel (Channel 1)

threshold-annotation-strategy.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

brightness-threshold.description: Parameter 'Brightness threshold' of type with ID 'number-range-integer'. Thresholds the brightness channel (Channel 3)

annotate-max-saturation.description: Parameter 'Max threshold annotation (S)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the max saturation threshold

saturation-threshold.description: Parameter 'Saturation threshold' of type with ID 'number-range-integer'. Thresholds the saturation channel (Channel 2)

annotation-name.description:
Parameter 'Annotation name' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. The name of the generated annotation. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

annotation-value.description:
Parameter 'Annotation value' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. The value of the generated annotation. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

ome-exporter-settings/zero-padding.description: Parameter 'Zero-pad file names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, file names are zero-padded

ome-exporter-settings/stack-order.description: Parameter 'Stack order' of type with ID 'import-imagej-bioformats:order'. In which order stacks are saved

ome-exporter-settings/no-lut.description: Parameter 'No LUT' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, no LUT information is saved into the OME TIFF file

ome-exporter-settings/save-roi.description: Parameter 'Save 2D ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are saved into the OME TIFF file

ome-exporter-settings/split-z.description: Parameter 'Split Z into files' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each Z is written into its own file

ome-exporter-settings/compression.description: Parameter 'Compression' of type with ID 'ome-tiff-compression'. The compression method that should be applied

ome-exporter-settings/split-c.description: Parameter 'Split channels into files' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each channel is written into its own file

ome-exporter-settings/split-t.description: Parameter 'Split frames into files' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, each time frame is written into its own file

high-threshold.description: Parameter 'High threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Determines the object cores (for values above)

low-threshold.description: Parameter 'Low threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Determines the background (for values below)

labeling.description: Parameter 'Generate labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, generate a label image instead of a mask

column-normalization.description: Parameter 'Column length normalization' of type with ID 'table-column-row-normalization'. Determines how to fill in missing values if multiple columns are created
