connectivity.description: Parameter 'Connectivity' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-3d'. Determines the neighborhood around each pixel that is checked for connectivity

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clijx.piv..name: Cli Jx Fast Particle Image Velocimetry 3D

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/maxDelta.description: Parameter 'Max Delta' of type with ID 'double'. null

apply-per-slice.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If higher dimensional data is provided, generate a table for each slice. If disabled, a table is generated for the whole image.

slice-annotation.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice annotation' of type with ID 'string'. Optional annotation type that generated for each slice output. It contains the string 'slice=[Number]'.

prefer-isotropic-filter.description: Parameter 'Prefer isotropic filter (faster)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If possible, utilize a faster implementation using a rolling ball algorithm. Requires that the radii are the same. Not supported on all filters. If isotropic filtering is not possible or disabled, the node will fall back to a parallelized algorithm.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

filters.description:
Parameter 'Filters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Filter expression that is used to find columns to be converted. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

limit.description:
Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'integer-range'. Determines which row indices are passed to the output. The first index is zero.

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

prefix.description: Parameter 'Prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Optional string that is prepended to file name.

suffix.description: Parameter 'Suffix' of type with ID 'string'. Optional string that is appended to file name.

If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/n.description: Parameter 'N' of type with ID 'double'. null

languages.description: Parameter 'Recognized language(s)' of type with ID 'dynamic-string-set'. Select the languages/scripts that Tesseract OCR will be able to recognize.

engine-mode.description: Parameter 'OCR engine mode' of type with ID 'tesseract-ocr-oem'. The OCR engine mode to use

override-char-allow-list.description: Parameter 'Specify recognized characters' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to specify the set of recognized characters (provided as string)

page-segmentation-method.description: Parameter 'Page segmentation method' of type with ID 'tesseract-ocr-psm'. By default Tesseract expects a page of text when it segments an image. If you’re just seeking to OCR a small region, try a different segmentation mode, using the --psm argument. Note that adding a white border to text which is too tightly cropped may also help

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Tesseract OCR environment' of type with ID 'optional-tesseract-ocr-environment'. Allows to override the Tesseract OCR environment

override-dpi.description: Parameter 'Specify DPI' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to specify the DPI of the input image

generated-image-type.description: Parameter 'Generated image type' of type with ID 'data-type'. The image type that is generated.

remove-overlay.description: Parameter 'Remove overlays' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, remove overlay ROIs from the imported image

remove-lut.description: Parameter 'Remove LUT' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, remove the LUT information if present

force-native-import.description: Parameter 'Force native ImageJ importer' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, always use the native ImageJ file importer, even if the file looks like it can only be read by Bio-Formats

title-annotation.description: Parameter 'Title annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Optional annotation type where the image title is written.

neighborhood.description: Parameter 'Neighborhood' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-2d'. The neighborhood of each pixel that is checked

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. clij-parameters/maxDeltaX.description: Parameter 'Max Delta X' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/maxDeltaY.description: Parameter 'Max Delta Y' of type with ID 'double'. null

equal-hyperstack-dimensions.description: Parameter 'Equalize hyperstack dimensions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the hyperstack dimensions are equalized

copy-slices.description: Parameter 'Newly generated slices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how new slices are generated, if needed. You can either repeat the last available slice or make new slices zero/black.

equal-width-and-height.description: Parameter 'Equalize width and height' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the width and height are equalized

scale-algorithm/jipipe:algorithm:enabled.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If disabled, this algorithm will be skipped in a run. Please note that this will also disable all algorithms dependent on this algorithm.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:node:ui-locked.description: Parameter 'Lock location/size' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, lock the location and size of this node. Does not affect automated alignment operations. Will lock the size if the node supports a resize handle. Will prevent deletion of the node by the user. Slots can still be edited/connected and parameters can still be changed.

scale-algorithm/avoid-unnecessary-scalin.description: Parameter 'Avoid unnecessary scaling' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the ImageJ resize method is not called if the image already has the correct size.

scale-algorithm/scale-mode.description: Parameter 'Scale mode' of type with ID 'ij1-transform:scale-mode'. Determines how the image is fit into the output. You can either stretch the image to the new dimensions, fit it inside the boundaries, cut off parts to cover the whole output, or leave the image unchanged (no resize) and just copy it into the output image with a given size

scale-algorithm/jipipe:node:bookmarked.description: Parameter 'Bookmark this node' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node is highlighted in the graph editor UI and added into the bookmark list.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:algorithm:pass-th.description:
Parameter 'Pass through' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will pass the input data directly to the output data without any processing. This is different from enabling/disabling the algorithm as this will not disable dependent algorithms.
Please note that setting this parameter via adaptive parameters (if available) does not always yield the expected result. The reason behind this is that pass-through is not trivial for certain nodes. We recommend to use a split node if node execution should be made adaptive.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:node:name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. Custom algorithm name.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:algorithm:runtime.description: Parameter 'Partition' of type with ID 'runtime-partition-reference'. Allows to move the node into a different runtime partition, which determine how the workload is executed.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:node:description.description: Parameter 'Description' of type with ID 'html-text'. A custom description

scale-algorithm/background-color.description: Parameter 'Background' of type with ID 'color'. Used if the scale mode is 'Fit'/'Cover'/'Paste'. Determines the background color of the output

scale-algorithm/y-axis.description: Parameter 'Y axis' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. How the Y axis should be scaled. If disabled, the aspect ratio is kept.

scale-algorithm/anchor.description: Parameter 'Placement' of type with ID 'anchor'. Used if the scale mode is 'Fit'/'Cover'/'Paste'. Determines where the image is placed.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:local-paralleliza.description: Parameter 'Local parallelization' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the partition- and run-wide parallelization settings and apply parallelization only on this node.

scale-algorithm/x-axis.description: Parameter 'X axis' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. How the X axis should be scaled. If disabled, the aspect ratio is kept.

scale-algorithm/interpolation-method.description: Parameter 'Interpolation' of type with ID 'ij1-interpolation-method'. The interpolation method

scale-algorithm/jipipe:data-batch-genera.description: Parameter 'Limit' of type with ID 'optional-integer-range'. Limits which iteration steps are generated. The first index is zero.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:parameter-slot-al.description: Parameter 'Multiple parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be an additional slot that consumes parameter data sets. The algorithm then will be applied for each of this parameter sets.
Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

scale-algorithm/jipipe:adaptive-paramete.description: Parameter 'Attach parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach the parameter values as annotations.

Parameter 'Attach only non-default parameter annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If multiple parameters are allowed, attach only parameter annotations that have different values from the current settings. Requires 'Attach parameter annotations' to be enabled.
Parameter 'Parameter annotation prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Text prefixed to generated parameter annotations.
Parameter 'Enable adaptive parameters' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, you can use custom expressions to generate parameters. Please note that this will disable parallelization.

Parameter 'Overridden parameters' of type with ID 'string-query-expression:string:pair-list'. Here you can override the value of parameters based on annotations. The 'Value' settings should return a value for the parameter type or a JSON string that can be converted into a valid parameter value. The 'Parameter key' setting determines to which parameter this value is written to.
Parameter 'Parameter annotations use internal names' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Generated parameter annotations use their internal unique names.

Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

file-format.description:
Parameter 'File format' of type with ID 'ij1-export-image-to-web:file-format'. The file format that should be used.
  • PNG, JPEG, BMP: 2D images only
  • AVI: 2D or 3D images only
  • TIFF: All images supported

movie-animated-dimension.description: Parameter 'Animated dimension' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. Only used if the format is AVI. Determines which dimension is animated

relative-to-project-dir.description: Parameter 'Output relative to project directory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, outputs will be preferably generated relative to the project directory. Otherwise, JIPipe will store the results in an automatically generated directory. Has no effect if an absolute path is provided.

output-directory.description: Parameter 'Output directory' of type with ID 'path'. Can be a relative or absolute directory. All collected files will be put into this directory. If relative, it is relative to the output slot's output directory that is generated based on the current run's output path.

movie-fps.description: Parameter 'Animation FPS' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Only used if the format is AVI. The number of frames shown per second.

avi-compression.description: Parameter 'AVI compression' of type with ID 'avi-compression'. Only used if the format is AVI. Determines how the frames are compressed.

jpeg-quality.description: Parameter 'AVI JPEG quality' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Only used if the format is AVI and the compression is JPEG. Value from 0-100 that determines the JPEG quality (100 is best).

exporter/make-filesystem-compatible.description: Parameter 'Make compatible to file systems' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the generated name/path is compatible to common file systems

exporter/force-name.description: Parameter 'Force name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, the output files are forced to use the generated name. Otherwise, internal names are utilized.

exporter/file-name.description: Parameter 'File name' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is used to generate the file names. You have all metadata available as variables. By default, it will summarize all variables (annotations) into a long string. If you do not want to customize the file name, you can create you own string based on available annotations. For example, you can insert #Dataset + "_" + Threshold to store the data set and a threshold annotation. You can use the following function to automatically generate the name from annotations: SUMMARIZE_ANNOTATIONS_MAP(annotations, "#")

render-settings/label-font-size.description: Parameter 'Compartment name font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The font size of the rendered compartment name

render-settings/max-edge-width.description: Parameter 'Maximum edge width' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. The maximum width of edge strokes. If disabled, the width is calculated based on the scale factor.

render-settings/draw-shadow.description: Parameter 'Draw shadow' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, draw a shadow around each compartment

render-settings/render-label.description: Parameter 'Draw compartment name' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, render the name of the compartment

clij-parameters/transform.description: Parameter 'Transform' of type with ID 'string'. null

num-threads.description: Parameter 'Number of threads' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of threads for parallel processing

kernel-y.description: Parameter 'Radius (Y)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the Y direction

kernel-x.description: Parameter 'Radius (X)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the X direction

kernel-z.description: Parameter 'Radius (Z)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the Z direction

prefer-isotropic-filter.description: Parameter 'Prefer isotropic filter (faster)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If possible, utilize a faster implementation using a rolling ball algorithm. Requires that the radii are the same. Not supported on all filters. If isotropic filtering is not possible or disabled, the node will fall back to a parallelized algorithm.

The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

filter.description:
Parameter 'Only match data if' of type with ID 'annotate-split-by-annotation:filter'. The filter is an expression that should return a boolean value that indicates whether a data item should be put into the corresponding output.Annotation values are available as variables. If an annotation has spaces special characters, use $ to access its value. Examples: 
#Dataset CONTAINS "Raw" AND condition EQUALS "mock"
TO_NUMBER($"my column") < 10
The expression result will be converted to a string. All existing annotations are available as variables that can be accessed directly, or if they contain special characters or spaces via the $ operator.

font-size.description: Parameter 'Font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The size of the text

background-color.description: Parameter 'Background color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, draw a rectangular background ROI behind the text

column-z.description:
Parameter 'Column 'Z'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Table column that determines the Z location. For one-based positions, 0 indicates that the ROI is present in all Z-slices. For zero-based positions the value is -1 or lower. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-y.description:
Parameter 'Column 'Y'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the Y coordinate. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-x.description:
Parameter 'Column 'X'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the X coordinate. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

one-based-positions.description: Parameter 'Use one-based positions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the first slice is 1. Otherwise, the first slice is zero.

column-metadata.description:
Parameter 'ROI metadata' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that contains ROI metadata in as JSON object (string keys, string values). If not valid JSON, the metadata will be stored into a field 'metadata_'. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.

  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

slice-filter.description: Parameter 'Limit to slices' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to limit the montage to specific slices

montage-parameters/tile-width.description: Parameter 'Tile width' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that determines the width of the tiles

montage-parameters/force-columns.description: Parameter 'Force number of columns' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, forces the number of columns

montage-parameters/rgb-use-render.description: Parameter 'Render to RGB' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled 'Render to RGB' is used instead of a plain conversion to RGB. Only applicable if images are converted to RGB.

montage-parameters/sorting-label-express.description: Parameter 'Custom sorting label' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Optional expression that generates dedicated labels that are only used for sorting. Applied per image.

montage-parameters/label-expression.description: Parameter 'Label' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that generates the labels. Applied per image.

montage-parameters/force-rgb.description: Parameter 'Force RGB colors' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, force rendering to RGB colors

montage-parameters/force-rows.description: Parameter 'Force number of rows' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, forces the number of rows

montage-parameters/label-parameters/avoi.description: Parameter 'Avoid drawing label on image' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, avoid that that label is drawn on top of the image

montage-parameters/label-parameters/draw.description: Parameter 'Draw label' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, draw a label for each image

montage-parameters/label-parameters/labe.description: Parameter 'Label background' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, draw a background behind the label
Parameter 'Label font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The font size of the drawn label
Parameter 'Label location' of type with ID 'anchor'. The location of the label
Parameter 'Label font family' of type with ID 'font-family-enum'. The font of the label
Parameter 'Label color' of type with ID 'color'. The color of the label

montage-parameters/label-parameters/limi.description: Parameter 'Limit with ellipsis' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, long labels will be limited with an ellipsis. Otherwise, the text is just cut off.

montage-parameters/canvas-parameters/can.description: Parameter 'Canvas image location' of type with ID 'anchor'. Location of the image inside the canvas

montage-parameters/canvas-parameters/bor.description: Parameter 'Border size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Size of the border drawn around the image canvas
Parameter 'Border color' of type with ID 'color'. The color of the border

montage-parameters/canvas-parameters/can.description: Parameter 'Canvas background color' of type with ID 'color'. The background color of the canvas

montage-parameters/image-parameters/scal.description: Parameter 'Scale mode' of type with ID 'ij1-transform:scale-mode'. The way how the image is scaled (if enabled)

montage-parameters/image-parameters/avoi.description: Parameter 'Avoid scaling' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, images that are small enough will not be scaled

montage-parameters/image-parameters/inte.description: Parameter 'Interpolation method' of type with ID 'ij1-interpolation-method'. The interpolation method if images are scaled

red-threshold.description: Parameter 'Red threshold' of type with ID 'number-range-integer'. Thresholds the red channel (Channel 1)

green-threshold.description: Parameter 'Green threshold' of type with ID 'number-range-integer'. Thresholds the green channel (Channel 2)

threshold-annotation-strategy.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

annotate-max-red.description: Parameter 'Max threshold annotation (R)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the max red threshold

annotate-min-blue.description: Parameter 'Min threshold annotation (B)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the min blue threshold

annotate-min-red.description: Parameter 'Min threshold annotation (R)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the min red threshold

annotate-max-blue.description: Parameter 'Max threshold annotation (B)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the max blue threshold

annotate-min-green.description: Parameter 'Min threshold annotation (G)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the min green threshold

blue-threshold.description: Parameter 'Blue threshold' of type with ID 'number-range-integer'. Thresholds the blue channel (Channel 3)

annotate-max-green.description: Parameter 'Max threshold annotation (G)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the max green threshold

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge same annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if an annotation already exists.

generated-annotation.description: Parameter 'Generated annotation' of type with ID 'string'. Select which annotation type is generated for each data row

position-t.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Frame)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different frame/time-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all frames. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

line-width.description: Parameter 'Line width' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Allows to change the line width when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

position-x.description: Parameter 'Location (X)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The X location. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

position-y.description: Parameter 'Location (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. The Y location. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

position-z.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different Z-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all slices. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

position-c.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Channel)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different channel-position. Please note that 'Channel' refers to an image slice and not to a pixel channel. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all channels. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

scale-x.description: Parameter 'Scale X' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Allows to scale the ROI. Please note that the scale will not be saved inside the ROI. Must be a number.

scale-y.description: Parameter 'Scale Y' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Allows to scale the ROI. Please note that the scale will not be saved inside the ROI. Must be a number.

roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Allows to change the ROI name

center-scale.description: Parameter 'Center scale' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If the annotation is true, each ROI is scaled relative to its center. Defaults to false. Must be true or false

fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Allows to change the fill color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The color can be a common color name (e.g. 'red'), a hex color (e.g. '#ff00ff'), or an RGB color (e.g. '0,128,255').

line-color.description: Parameter 'Line color' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Allows to change the line color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The color can be a common color name (e.g. 'red'), a hex color (e.g. '#ff00ff'), or an RGB color (e.g. '0,128,255').

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

crop-c.description: Parameter 'Crop C' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, crop the ROI list in the channel axis (locations with a value of zero are skipped)

crop-z.description: Parameter 'Crop Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, crop the ROI list in the Z axis (locations with a value of zero are skipped)

crop-t.description: Parameter 'Crop T' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, crop the ROI list in the T axis (locations with a value of zero are skipped)

crop-xy.description: Parameter 'Crop X/Y' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, crop the ROI list in the X and Y axis

columns.description:

If the column name has special characters or spaces, use the $ operator. Example:

replace-existing.description: Parameter 'Replace existing data' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If the target column exists, replace its content

ensure-min-number-of-rows.description: Parameter 'Ensure minimum number of rows' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Ensures that the table has at least the specified number of rows prior to adding columns.

indices-z.description:
Parameter 'Indices (Z)' of type with ID 'integer-range'. Array of Z indices to be included in the final image. All indices begin with zero. Indices outside the available range are automatically wrapped. Return an empty array to skip a slice.

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

indices-c.description:
Parameter 'Indices (Channel)' of type with ID 'integer-range'. Array of channel/C indices to be included in the final image.. All indices begin with zero. Indices outside the available range are automatically wrapped. Return an empty array to skip a slice.

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

annotate-c.description: Parameter 'Annotate with C indices' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the output is annotated with the source channel slices (zero-based)

annotate-z.description: Parameter 'Annotate with Z indices' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the output is annotated with the source Z slices (zero-based)

annotate-t.description: Parameter 'Annotate with T indices' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the output is annotated with the source frame slices (zero-based)

indices-t.description:
Parameter 'Indices (Frames)' of type with ID 'integer-range'. Array of frame/T indices to be included in the final image.. All indices begin with zero. Indices outside the available range are automatically wrapped. Return an empty array to skip a slice.

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge strategy' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how annotations that are added in the R script are merged into existing annotations.

script.description:
Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'r-script'. The script that contains the R commands. The script comes with various API functions and variables that allow to communicate with JIPipe:
  • JIPipe.InputSlotRowCounts contains named row counts for each slot
  • JIPipe.TextAnnotations contains the list of annotations (named strings)
  • JIPipe.Variables contains the list of variables defined by parameters (named values). If a parameter's unique key is a valid variable name, it will also be available as variable.
  • JIPipe.GetInputFolder(slot, row=0) returns the data folder of the specified slot. The data folder contains the input row stored in standardized JIPipe format.
  • JIPipe.GetInputAsDataFrame(slot, row=0) reads the specified input data row as data frame.
  • JIPipe.AddOutputFolder(slot, annotations=list()) adds a new output folder into the specified output slot and returns the folder path. Optionally, you can assign annotations to add as list of named strings. Please note that the folder must contain data according to the slot's data type (an image file or a CSV file respectively)
  • JIPipe.AddOutputDataFrame(data, slot, annotations=list()) adds the specified data frame into the specified output slot. Optionally, you can add annotations to the data.
  • JIPipe.AddOutputPNGImagePath(data, slot, annotations=list()) generates an image file path to be added as output and return the path. Please note that you must use png() or other functions to actually write this file.Optionally, you can add annotations to the data.
  • JIPipe.AddOutputTIFFImagePath(data, slot, annotations=list()) generates an image file path to be added as output and return the path. Please note that you must use tiff() or other functions to actually write this file.Optionally, you can add annotations to the data.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override R environment' of type with ID 'optional-r-environment'. If enabled, a different R environment is used for this Node.

data-annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Data annotation merge mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:data-annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if there is already an existing data annotation with the same name

data-annotation-filter.description:
Parameter 'Data annotation filter' of type with ID 'data-annotation-query-expression'. Allows to filter only specific data annotations. Set to false to completely ignore all data annotations. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

text-annotation-filter.description:
Parameter 'Text annotation filter' of type with ID 'annotation-query-expression'. Allows to filter only specific text annotations. Set to false to completely ignore all text annotations. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

radius-x.description: Parameter 'Radius (X)' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Filter radius (pixels) in X direction. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

radius-z.description: Parameter 'Radius (Z)' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Filter radius (pixels) in Z direction. If zero or less, radius in X direction is automatically used instead. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

radius-y.description: Parameter 'Radius (Y)' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Filter radius (pixels) in Y direction. If zero or less, radius in X direction is automatically used instead. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

multiplier.description: Parameter 'Transform multiplier' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Determines the relative amount of the transform. Zero means that no transform is applied. One (default) that the transform is applied without any additional changes.

absolute-coordinates.description: Parameter 'Absolute coordinates' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the vector field is assumed to have absolute coordinates. For polar coordinates, the origin is assumed to be (0, 0).

vector-dimension.description: Parameter 'Vector dimension' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. Determines which dimension stores the vector coordinates. This dimension will have the same vector field across all slices of it. The vector dimension is ignored if the vector field has exactly two planes. Here, the same field is applied to all slices.

wrap-mode.description: Parameter 'Wrap mode' of type with ID 'ij1-transform:wrap-mode'. Determines what to do with source/target pixels that are outside the image. 'None' means that the pixel is skipped.

invert-transform.description: Parameter 'Invert transform' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the transform is reversed.

polar-coordinates.description: Parameter 'Use polar coordinates' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the vector field is assumed to be provided in polar coordinates (r, phi) instead of (x, y).

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Radius of the filter kernel. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

num-threads.description: Parameter 'Number of threads' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of threads for parallel processing

kernel-x.description: Parameter 'Radius (X)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the X direction

kernel-z.description: Parameter 'Radius (Z)' of type with ID 'quantity'. Radius of the ellipsoidal kernel in the Z direction

prefer-isotropic-filter.description: Parameter 'Prefer isotropic filter (faster)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If possible, utilize a faster implementation using a rolling ball algorithm. Requires that the radii are the same. Not supported on all filters. If isotropic filtering is not possible or disabled, the node will fall back to a parallelized algorithm.

series-name.description:
Parameter 'Series name' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to generate the series name This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example:

input-columns/Value.description: Parameter 'Value' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Values displayed in the Y axis (Numeric column)

Parameter 'Renaming expression' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Determines how each column should be renamed. There is a variable 'value' available that contains the current column. If you return an empty string, the column will be deleted. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clijx.clij.name: Cli Jx Arg Maximum Z Projection

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

min-threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Min threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Annotation that contains the minimum pixel value. If disabled, this assumes the negative infinity.

order-expression.description: Parameter 'Order function' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that should return an ordered list of workbook sheets as array of strings. If a name is missing, the sheet is placed at the end of the list. If a string is returned, the sheet with the name is set as the first sheet.

sheet-name-expression.description: Parameter 'Sheet name function' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that determines the name of the sheet. Please note that there are certain restrictions on the naming of sheets that are automatically enforced by JIPipe (see https://poi.apache.org/apidocs/dev/org/apache/poi/ss/usermodel/Workbook.html#createSheet--).

file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

missing-value-replacement.description: Parameter 'Replace missing mappings' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, replace mappings missing from the table by the value defined by this expression. If disabled, missing mappings are ignored and affected labels are not changed.

old-label-column.description:
Parameter 'Old label column' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Table column that contains the old label This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

ignore-zero.description: Parameter 'Ignore zero label' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the label value '0' is always ignored, regardless of mapping settings.

new-label-column.description:
Parameter 'New label column' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Table column that contains the new label This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/bidirectional.description: Parameter 'Bidirectional' of type with ID 'boolean'. null

table-columns.description: Parameter 'Table columns' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. For each parameter, add an entry that describes the generated parameter key/type and the source table column.

table-column.name: Table column
table-column.description:
The table column This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

draw-over.description: Parameter 'Draw over reference' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, draw the ROI over the reference image.

prefer-render-via-overlay.description: Parameter 'Prefer render via overlay' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the rendering via an ImageJ overlay is preferred even if the magnification is 1.0

label-foreground.description: Parameter 'Label foreground' of type with ID 'color'. The text color of the label (if enabled)

label-size.description: Parameter 'Label size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Font size of drawn labels

override-fill-color.description: Parameter 'Override fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, the fill color will be overridden by this value. If a ROI has no fill color, it will always fall back to this color.

draw-outline-mode.description: Parameter 'Draw outline' of type with ID 'roi-element-drawing-mode'. If enabled, draw a white outline of the ROI

magnification.description: Parameter 'Magnification' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Magnification applied during the rendering

label-background.description: Parameter 'Label background' of type with ID 'optional-color'. The background color of the label (if enabled)

override-line-width.description: Parameter 'Override line width' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, the line width will be overridden by this value. If a ROI has a line width equal or less than zero, it will fall back to this value.

drawn-label.description: Parameter 'Draw labels' of type with ID 'roi-label'. Allows to draw labels on top of ROI.

ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI will show outside their channel (C) layer

label-font-family.description: Parameter 'Label font family' of type with ID 'font-family-enum'. The font family of the label

fill-outline-mode.description: Parameter 'Draw filled outline' of type with ID 'roi-element-drawing-mode'. If enabled, fill the ROI areas

override-line-color.description: Parameter 'Override line color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, the line color will be overridden by this value. If a ROI has no line color, it will always fall back to this color.

ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI will show outside their frame (T) layer

opacity.description: Parameter 'Opacity' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Opacity of the added ROI and labels. If zero, they are not visible. If set to one, they are fully visible.

ignore-z.description: Parameter 'Ignore Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI will show outside their Z layer

render-greyscale-images.description: Parameter 'Render greyscale images to RGB' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, greyscale images are fully rendered to RGB, including their LUT. Otherwise, the greyscale images are only converted to RGB; the LUT is ignored.

normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize kernel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

position-t.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Frame)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different frame/time-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all frames. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

line-width.description: Parameter 'Line width' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the line width when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

row-selector.description: Parameter 'Match table row if ...' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that matches the ROI to its correct table row. The first match (in table order) is used. If no match is found, the ROI is not changed.

position-x.description: Parameter 'Location (X)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. The X location. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

position-y.description: Parameter 'Location (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. The Y location. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

position-z.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different Z-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all slices. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

scale-x.description: Parameter 'Scale X' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Scales the ROI. Please note that the scale will not be saved inside the ROI. Must evaluate to a number.

scale-y.description: Parameter 'Scale Y' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Scales the ROI. Please note that the scale will not be saved inside the ROI. Must evaluate to a number.

roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the ROI name

line-color.description: Parameter 'Line color' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the line color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The color can be a common color name (e.g. 'red'), a hex color (e.g. '#ff00ff'), or an RGB color (e.g. '0,128,255').

position-c.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Channel)' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different channel-position. Please note that 'Channel' refers to an image slice and not to a pixel channel. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all channels. The annotation value is converted to an integer.

center-scale.description: Parameter 'Center scale' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If true, each ROI is scaled relative to its center. Must evaluate to a boolean.

fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the fill color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ. The color can be a common color name (e.g. 'red'), a hex color (e.g. '#ff00ff'), or an RGB color (e.g. '0,128,255').

expression.description: Parameter 'Expression' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed for each pixel. It provides the pixel components in the original color space, as well as in other color spaces. The expression must return a number that will be stored as greyscale value.

output-type.description: Parameter 'Output type' of type with ID 'data-type'. Determines which data type is generated. Please note that the generic greyscale output is 32-bit float.

connectivity.description: Parameter 'Connectivity' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-3d'. Determines the neighborhood around each pixel that is checked for connectivity

dynamic.description: Parameter 'Dynamic' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The minimal difference between a maxima and its boundary

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge same annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines which strategy is applied if an annotation already exists.

detailed-data-string.description: Parameter 'Prefer detailed string' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a more detailed string information (if available) is used instead of the string shown in the UI.

generated-annotation.description: Parameter 'Generated annotation' of type with ID 'string'. Select which annotation type is generated for each data row

clij-parameters/value.description: Parameter 'Value' of type with ID 'double'. null

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

measurements.description: Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij3d-measurement-set'. The measurements to generate

physical-dimension-t.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (T)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image. Please note that only the unit is supported.

physical-dimension-y.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image

physical-dimension-z.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image

physical-dimension-value.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (Value)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image. Please note that only the unit is supported.

table-rows-fuzzy-kmeans-clustering.name: Apply Fuzzy K-Means clustering on rows

fuzziness.description: Parameter 'Fuzziness' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Determines the level of cluster fuzziness, larger values lead to fuzzier clusters

k.description: Parameter 'K' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of clusters to split the data into

input-columns.description: Parameter 'Input columns' of type with ID 'table-column-source-list'. The list of columns that will be used to create the points. Please note that string columns and NA/infinite values are automatically replaced with zeroes.

output-settings/output-column-cluster-id.description: Parameter 'Cluster ID column' of type with ID 'optional-string'. The column where the best-matching cluster ID will be written to. The first index will be 1.

output-settings/output-column-distance-t.description: Parameter 'Distance to cluster center column' of type with ID 'optional-string'. The column where the distance to the best-matching cluster center will be written to

output-settings/output-column-cluster-si.description: Parameter 'Cluster size column' of type with ID 'optional-string'. The column where the best-matching cluster size will be written to

output-settings/membership-prefix.description: Parameter 'Membership prefix' of type with ID 'string'. Prefix for the membership columns that are added into the original table

clustering-settings/epsilon.description: Parameter 'Epsilon' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The convergence criteria, default is 1e-3

clustering-settings/distance-measure.description: Parameter 'Distance measure' of type with ID 'table-statistics-distance-measures'. The distance measure to use

clustering-settings/max-iterations.description: Parameter 'Maximum iterations' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The maximum number of iterations to run the algorithm for. If negative, no maximum will be used.

merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if there is already an existing annotation with the same name

num-rows-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of rows' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate the data table with its number of rows

num-text-annotation-columns-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of text annotation columns' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate the data table with the number of text annotation columns

num-data-annotation-columns-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of data annotation columns' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate the data table with the number of data annotation columns

The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/num_slices.description: Parameter 'Num Slices' of type with ID 'double'. null

overlap-x.description: Parameter 'Overlap (X)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Sets the overlap of the tiles. Please note that the size of the tiles will increase.

overlap-y.description: Parameter 'Overlap (Y)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Sets the overlap of the tiles. Please note that the size of the tiles will increase.

tile-x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with tile X' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate each tile with its X location (in tile coordinates)

tile-original-width-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original width' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate the tile with the width of the original image

tile-real-y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original Y location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate the tile with its location within the original image. Only works if the scaling anchor is top left

num-tile-x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of X tiles' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate each tile with the number of tiles in the image

tile-original-height-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original height' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate the tile with the height of the original image

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge existing annotations' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how existing annotations are merged

tile-y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with tile Y' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate each tile with its Y location (in tile coordinates)

tile-inset-y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with tile inset (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, each tile is annotated with its inset, meaning the overlap in the Y direction. This value can be utilized to remove the overlap at a later point.

tile-inset-x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with tile inset (X)' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, each tile is annotated with its inset, meaning the overlap in the X direction. This value can be utilized to remove the overlap at a later point.

tile-x.description: Parameter 'Tile width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The width of a tile

tile-y.description: Parameter 'Tile height' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The height of a tile

num-tile-y-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with number of Y tiles' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate each tile with the number of tiles in the image

border-mode.description:
Parameter 'Border mode' of type with ID 'ij1-border-mode'. Determines how the image is expanded with borders. Only applicable if an overlap is set.
  • Constant: Set the border to a constant value (zero/black unless configurable)
  • Repeat: Repeat the nearest non-border pixel
  • Mirror: Mirror the pixel values across the border
  • Tile: Tile the image

tile-real-x-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with original X location' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If true, annotate the tile with its location within the original image. Only works if the scaling anchor is top left

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Annotation merge strategy' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how annotations that are added in the Python script are merged into existing annotations.

code.description:
Parameter 'Script' of type with ID 'python-code'. The Python script to be executed. The script comes with various API functions and variables that allow to communicate with JIPipe:
  • jipipe_inputs is a dict of input slots.
  • jipipe_outputs is a dict of output slots.
  • jipipe_variables is a dict of variables passed from the script parameters.
The script is designed to be used with the JIPipe Python API (supplied automatically by default). You can find the full API documentation here: https://jipipe.hki-jena.de/apidocs/python-current/index.html

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Python operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Python is used.

backup-filter.description:
Parameter 'Backup' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Selects the backup by its key This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

missing-value.description: Parameter 'Missing value' of type with ID 'expression'. Used if the backup is missing

backup-old-value.description: Parameter 'Backup old value' of type with ID 'optional-string'. If enabled, backup the value to the specified storage

vertex-filter/mask.description: Parameter 'Vertex mask' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Apply the operation to a specific set of vertices

convert-data-annotation-to-string-annota.name: Convert data annotation to string annotation

annotation-merge-strategy.description: Parameter 'Merge same annotation values' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines how the newly created string annotations are merged into existing ones.

name-filter.description:
Parameter 'Name filter' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Determines which data annotations are converted. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

keep-data-annotations.description: Parameter 'Keep data annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data annotations are not deleted after converting them to a string annotation.

The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.
