clij-parameters/model_filename.description: Parameter 'Model Filename' of type with ID 'string'. null

clij-parameters/feature_definitions.description: Parameter 'Feature Definitions' of type with ID 'string'. null

clij-parameters/numberOfAutoContextItera.description: Parameter 'Number Of Auto Context Iterations' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/numberOfTrees.description: Parameter 'Number Of Trees' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/numberOfFeatures.description: Parameter 'Number Of Features' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/maxDepth.description: Parameter 'Max Depth' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/tileWidth.description: Parameter 'Tile Width' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/tileHeight.description: Parameter 'Tile Height' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/tileDepth.description: Parameter 'Tile Depth' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/marginWidth.description: Parameter 'Margin Width' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/marginHeight.description: Parameter 'Margin Height' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/marginDepth.description: Parameter 'Margin Depth' of type with ID 'double'. null

split-by-track.description: Parameter 'Split by track' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, split the filaments by their track ID (if available)

track-annotation-name.description: Parameter 'Annotate with track ID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, add the track ID as annotation to the generated filaments

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override environment' of type with ID 'optional-tsoax-environment'. If enabled, override the TSOAX environment for this node

convergence/check-period.description: Parameter 'Check period' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Specifies the cycle of checking for convergence in number of iterations. A value of 100 means a snake is checked for convergence every 100 iterations. (see the last sentence of Section 2.1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015)

convergence/maximum-iterations.description: Parameter 'Maximum iterations' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Specifies the maximum number of iterations allowed in each snake evolution

convergence/change-threshold.description: Parameter 'Change threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (in pixels) specifies the threshold of change for a snake to be converged. A value of 0.05 means a snake is converged if every snake point drifts less than 0.05 pixels since last check for convergence (see the last sentence of Section 2.1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015)

convergence/minimum-snake-length.description: Parameter 'Minimum snake length' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. (in pixels) specifies the minimum length of a resultant snake. Increase the value to eliminate hair-like snake structures as well as to avoid the snakes picking up actin patches in yeast images

evolution/radial-near.description: Parameter 'Radial near' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Together with other radial parameters, define the local annulus from which magnitude of stretching forces (see Section 2.1.1 and Figure 3 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015) and local image SNR are computed.

evolution/overlap-threshold.description: Parameter 'Overlap threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The distance threshold that snakes are considered overlapping

evolution/c.description: Parameter 'c (track continuity)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight by which snakes detected at nearby locations in space are assigned to the same track, as a function of the number of time frames separating them. The value of 1/c is the number of frames beyond which the probability of assigning snakes to the same track decreases exponentially with frame number separation. This parameter should be increased (up to order 1) to improve track continuity over successive frames.

evolution/damp-z.description: Parameter 'Damp Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Toggles the suppression of snake evolution along the z-axis. This may be useful when anisotropy in PSF along z becomes a problem

evolution/background-z-xy-ratio.description: Parameter 'Background Z/XY ratio' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Defines the anisotropy of the PSF of microscope. It is the spreading of PSF along z-axis relative to that of x and y-axis. Set this parameter to fix the anisotropy in the background intensity calculation

evolution/delta.description: Parameter 'Delta' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The delta (number of snake points apart) for computing snake tip tangent using finite difference. Must be a positive integer

evolution/stretch-factor.description: Parameter 'Stretch factor (bridging)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight of stretching force (see Equation 2 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015). Increasing this value to stretch snakes more in case of under-segmentation.

evolution/external-factor.description: Parameter 'External factor' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight of image forces (image gradient) (see Equation 2 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015). Increasing this value to make snakes follow more closely the local shape of filaments

evolution/grouping.description: Parameter 'Grouping' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Enables the grouping process of snakes at detected junctions prior to tracking. This option can be enabled for tracking of intersecting elongating filaments and disabled for tracking the movement of filament segments in between junction points.

evolution/minimum-angle-for-soac-linking.description: Parameter 'Minimum angle for SOAC linking' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (in radians) is the angular threshold for grouping snakes. The angle between the tangent directions of two snake branches in a clustered junction must be greater than this value to be grouped. Default is 2π/3

evolution/association-threshold.description: Parameter 'Association threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (in pixels). Not documented by original developers.

evolution/grouping-distance-threshold.description: Parameter 'Grouping distance threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The maximum distance that two T-junctions formed after snake evolution can be clustered into one clustered junction for grouping. Large values may help collapsing unwanted T-junctions.

evolution/alpha.description: Parameter 'Alpha (elongation penalty)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight of first order continuity of snake (see Equation 1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015). This term describes the energy penalty to elongate snakes. For images with dim linear structures and bright spots, one may want to use small value of alpha

evolution/number-of-background-radial-se.description: Parameter 'Number of radial sectors' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Together with other radial parameters, define the local annulus from which magnitude of stretching forces (see Section 2.1.1 and Figure 3 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015) and local image SNR are computed.

evolution/grouping-delta.description: Parameter 'Grouping delta' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Specifies the delta (number of snake points apart) for computing tip tangents of dissected snake segments using finite difference. Must be a positive integer

evolution/radial-far.description: Parameter 'Radial far' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Together with other radial parameters, define the local annulus from which magnitude of stretching forces (see Section 2.1.1 and Figure 3 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015) and local image SNR are computed.

evolution/gamma.description: Parameter 'Gamma (evolution step size)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Controls the step size of the iterative process of snake evolution. The smaller gamma is, the faster snakes converge but the result is less accurate (see Equation 6 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015)

evolution/beta.description: Parameter 'Beta (bending penalty)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight of second order continuity of snake (see Eq.1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015). This term describes the snake bending energy penalty. Use larger value to make snakes more straight

initialization/snake-point-spacing.description: Parameter 'Snake point spacing' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (in pixels) specifies the spacing between consecutive snake points (see the end of Section 2.1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015).

initialization/init-z.description: Parameter 'Init z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Toggles the initialization of snakes along z-axis. Uncheck it to eliminate snakes that are perpendicular to filaments due to anisotropic PSF with larger spreading along z-axis.

initialization/ridge-threshold.description: Parameter 'Ridge threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (also “grad-diff”) controls the number of initialized snakes (see Section 2.2.1 in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2013.10.015). Decrease this value to initialize more snakes

initialization/intensity-scaling.description: Parameter 'Intensity scaling' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Multiplies the intensity of every pixel such that the range of rescaled intensities lie roughly between 0.0 and 1.0. This allows using a standard range of α, β and other parameters below. Leave fixed for a given set of images. Set this value to 0 for automatic scaling, where the maximum intensity is scaled to exact 1.0.

initialization/minimum-foreground.description: Parameter 'Minimum foreground' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Specifies the range of intensities intended for extraction. Snakes are not initialized where the image intensity is below “background” or above “foreground”. During evolution, stretching force is zero when the intensity at a snake tip is outside this range.

initialization/maximum-foreground.description: Parameter 'Maximum foreground' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Specifies the range of intensities intended for extraction. Snakes are not initialized where the image intensity is below “background” or above “foreground”. During evolution, stretching force is zero when the intensity at a snake tip is outside this range.

initialization/gaussian-std.description: Parameter 'Gaussian std' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. (in pixels) controls the amount of Gaussian smoothing in the computation of image gradient (See Section 2.1 and Equation 3 in Xu, T., Vavylonis, D. & Huang, X. 3D actin network centerline extraction with multiple active contours. Medical Image Analysis 18, 272–284 (2014).). Set σ < 0.01 to disable smoothing

The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

clij-parameters/feature_definitions.description: Parameter 'Feature Definitions' of type with ID 'string'. null

method.description:
Parameter 'Method' of type with ID 'ij1-labels-to-roi:method'. The algorithm responsible for converting labels into ROI
  • Floodfill: Original implementation by Waisman et al. that applies flood filling for each pixel and removes all labels within the detected area. This is a fast algorithm suitable to detect labels that are not nested.
  • Protected floodfill: Modified algorithm that utilizes an additional connected components operation to prevent accidental removals. These can happen if one label is encased within another (e.g., structures within a tissue). Please note that this algorithm is considerably slower than the 'Floodfill' method, especially for images with many different labels.

label-name-expression.description: Parameter 'Label name' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression for the generation of the label name

connectivity.description: Parameter 'Connectivity' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-2d'. The connectivity for the connected components algorithm

outline.description: Parameter 'Outline method' of type with ID 'ij1:roi-outline'. The outlining method that should be applied.

error-behavior.description: Parameter 'Error handling' of type with ID 'ij1:invalid-roi-outline-behavior'. What to do if a ROI could not be processed

line-length.description: Parameter 'Line length' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The approximated length of the structuring element

type.description: Parameter 'Type' of type with ID 'ij1-feature-directional-filter:type'. Specifies how to combine the results for each oriented filter

n-directions.description: Parameter 'Number of directions' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of oriented structuring elements to consider. To be increased if the length of line is large.

operation.description: Parameter 'Operation' of type with ID 'ij1-feature-directional-filter:operation'. The operation to apply using each oriented structuring element

min-particle-sphericity.description: Parameter 'Min particle sphericity' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The minimum sphericity. The value range is from 0-1. If an object is not within the sphericity range, it is removed from the results.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

min-particle-size.description: Parameter 'Min particle size' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The minimum particle size in the specified unit of the input image. If no unit is available, the unit is 'pixels'. If an object is not within the size range, it is removed from the results.

black-background.description: Parameter 'Black background' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the background is assumed to be black. If disabled, black pixels are extracted as ROI.

max-particle-sphericity.description: Parameter 'Max particle sphericity' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The maximum sphericity. The value range is from 0-1. If an object is not within the sphericity range, it is removed from the results.

neighborhood.description: Parameter 'Neighborhood' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-3d'. Determines which neighborhood is used to find connected components.

exclude-edges.description: Parameter 'Exclude edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, objects that are connected to the image edges are removed.

max-particle-size.description: Parameter 'Max particle size' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The maximum particle size in the specified unit of the input image. If no unit is available, the unit is 'pixels'. If an object is not within the size range, it is removed from the results.

clij-parameters/start_z.description: Parameter 'Start Z' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/end_z.description: Parameter 'End Z' of type with ID 'double'. null

id-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with OMERO image ID' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, adds the OMERO image ID as annotation

title-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with image title' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Optional annotation type where the image title is written.

override-credentials.description: Parameter 'Override OMERO credentials' of type with ID 'optional-omero-credentials'. Allows to override the OMERO credentials provided in the JIPipe application settings

tag-to-annotation-importer/tags-to-list-.description: Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, write all tag names into a single annotation (as list). The name of the annotation is determined by the value of this parameter

Parameter 'Convert tags into single annotation (filter)' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter out tags that will be included into 'Convert tags into single annotation' This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

image-import-parameters/split-time-point.description: Parameter 'Split time points' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Timelapse data will be opened as a separate stack for each timepoint.

image-import-parameters/stitch-tiles.description: Parameter 'Stitch tiles' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Stitch tiles - Performs very simple stitching of tiles.  The overlap is assumed to be 0%, and the stage coordinates are used to determine the proper placement of the tiles. This is useful for seeing a quick preview of what the stitched image might look like, but is not a substitute for proper stitching plugins such as the 2D/3D Stitching plugin.

image-import-parameters/concatenate.description: Parameter 'Concatenate compatible series' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Allows multiple image series to be joined end to end.  Example: You want to join two sequential timelapse series.

image-import-parameters/extract-rois.description: Parameter 'Extract ROIs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROIs are extracted from OME data.

image-import-parameters/stack-order.description: Parameter 'Stack order' of type with ID 'import-imagej-bioformats:order'.

image-import-parameters/split-channels.description: Parameter 'Split channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Each channel is opened as a separate stack.  This option is especially useful if you want to merge the channels into a specific order, rather than automatically assign channels to the order of RGB. The bit depth is preserved.

image-import-parameters/crop-regions.description: Parameter 'Crop regions' of type with ID 'rectangle-list'.

image-import-parameters/color-mode.description:
Parameter 'Color mode' of type with ID 'import-imagej-bioformats:color-mode'. Color mode - Visualizes channels according to the specified scheme.  Possible choices are:
Default - Display channels as closely as possible to how they are stored in the file.
Composite - Open as a merged composite image. Channels are colorized according to metadata present in the dataset (if any), or in the following default order: 1=red, 2=green, 3=blue, 4=gray, 5=cyan, 6=magenta, 7=yellow.
Colorized - Open with each channel in a separate plane, colorized according to metadata present in the dataset (if any), or in the default order (see Composite above).
Grayscale - Open with each channel in a separate plane, displayed in plain grayscale.
Custom - Same as Colorized, except that you can explicitly choose how to colorize each channel.
Note that ImageJ can only composite together 7 or fewer channels. With more than 7 channels, some of the modes above may not work.

image-import-parameters/split-focal-plan.description: Parameter 'Split focal planes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Each focal plane is opened as a separate stack.

image-import-parameters/swap-dimensions.description: Parameter 'Swap dimensions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Allows reassignment of dimensional axes (e.g., channel, Z and time).  Bio-Formats is supposed to be smart about handling multidimensional image data, but in some cases gets things wrong. For example, when stitching together a dataset from multiple files using the Group files with similar names option, Bio-Formats may not know which dimensional axis the file numbering is supposed to represent. It will take a guess, but in case it guesses wrong, you can use Swap dimensions to reassign which dimensions are which.

image-import-parameters/crop.description: Parameter 'Crop images' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Image planes may be cropped during import to conserve memory. Use the 'Crop regions' parameter to define which regions should be cropped.

image-import-parameters/auto-scale.description: Parameter 'Auto scale' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Stretches the channel histograms to each channel's global minimum and maximum value throughout the stack. Does not alter underlying values in the image.  If unselected, all channel histograms are scaled to the image's digitization bit depth. Note that this range may be narrower than the bit depth of the file. For example, a 16-bit file may contain intensities digitized to 16-bits (0-65,535), to 12-bits (0-4,095), to 10-bits (0-1,023), etc.  Note that you can use the Brightness & Contrast or Window/Level controls to adjust the contrast range regardless of whether this option is used. The histogram will provide min/max values in the stack.

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/fi.description: Parameter 'Keep kv-pair if' of type with ID 'expression'. Allows to filter out key-value pairs from being converted into annotations

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/an.description: Parameter 'Annotation name' of type with ID 'expression'. The name of the generated annotation
Parameter 'Annotation value' of type with ID 'expression'. The value of the generated annotation

key-value-pair-to-annotation-importer/en.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determined if the import of key-value pairs is enabled

parameter-table.description: Parameter 'Parameters' of type with ID 'parameter-table'. Parameters that are generated

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/flip_x.description: Parameter 'Flip X' of type with ID 'boolean'. null

output-image-type.description: Parameter 'Output image type' of type with ID 'data-type'. The image type that is generated.

custom-c-size.description: Parameter 'Custom channel size' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, the number of channel slices is determined by this parameter. Otherwise, it is inferred from the table.

custom-height.description: Parameter 'Custom height' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, the image height is determined by this parameter. Otherwise, it is inferred from the table.

recursive.description: Parameter 'Recursive' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the search is recursive.

enable-3d.description: Parameter 'Connect in 3D' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm will look for endpoints in other Z planes.

limit-connections.description: Parameter 'Limit created edges (source/target)' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, limit the number of created edges per vertex. Uses a scoring function to determine an order.

source-vertex-filter.description: Parameter 'Source vertex filter' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to pre-select source vertices

enforce-edges-within-mask.description: Parameter 'Prevent edges outside mask' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled and a mask is available, check if edges crosses outside the mask boundaries and exclude those from being marked as candidate edge

require-direction.description: Parameter 'Require direction' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Only considers vertices that have a degree of 1, which allows the calculation of a direction

target-vertex-filter.description: Parameter 'Target vertex filter' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to pre-select target vertices

connect-across-t.description: Parameter 'Connect across frames' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm considers also endpoints between different frame planes.

find-path.description: Parameter 'Find path between candidate vertices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, find the path between the candidate vertices. Impacts the performance.

new-edge-metadata.description: Parameter 'Set new edges metadata' of type with ID 'string:string:pair-list'. Allows to set the metadata of newly created edges

filter-function.description: Parameter 'Candidate edge filter' of type with ID 'expression'. Filter expression that determines if an edge is considered as candidate

ignore-limit-connections-for-target.description: Parameter 'Bypass connection limit for targets' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the 'Limit connections' parameter does not apply to target vertices.

connect-across-c.description: Parameter 'Connect across channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the algorithm considers also endpoints between different channel planes.

ignore-limit-connections-for-source.description: Parameter 'Bypass connection limit for sources' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the 'Limit connections' parameter does not apply to source vertices.

new-edge-color.description: Parameter 'Color new edges' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to color newly made edges

scoring-function.description: Parameter 'Scoring function' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression executed per edge candidate to generate a score for limited connections. Higher scores are selected first.

z-axis.description: Parameter 'Z axis' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. How the Z axis should be scaled

y-axis.description: Parameter 'Y axis' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. How the Y axis should be scaled

x-axis.description: Parameter 'X axis' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. How the X axis should be scaled

interpolation-method.description: Parameter 'Interpolation' of type with ID 'ij1-interpolation-method'. The interpolation method

radius-x.description: Parameter 'Radius (X)' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Filter radius (pixels) in X direction. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

radius-z.description: Parameter 'Radius (Z)' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Filter radius (pixels) in Z direction. If zero or less, radius in X direction is automatically used instead. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

radius-y.description: Parameter 'Radius (Y)' of type with ID 'primitive.float'. Filter radius (pixels) in Y direction. If zero or less, radius in X direction is automatically used instead. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

roi-name-expression.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'expression'. The name of the generated line ROI is calculated from this expression

max-theta.description: Parameter 'Theta step count' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of thetas to check (the step size is automatically determined as PI/step_count)

select-top-n.description: Parameter 'Select top N lines' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, only the top N lines according to their scores are selected.

neighborhood-size.description: Parameter 'Neighborhood size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The size of the neighbourhood in which to search for other local maxima

pixel-threshold.description: Parameter 'Pixel threshold' of type with ID 'optional-float'. If enabled (default), points are only counted if they are above the specified threshold. This this mode is deactivated, each pixel is counted, but weighted by its intensity.

accumulator-threshold.description: Parameter 'Accumulator threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The percentage threshold above which lines are determined from the hough array

border-color.description: Parameter 'Border color' of type with ID 'expression'. Only applicable if the mode is 'Constant'. The value of the border. Return a number to return a greyscale value. Return an array to set the border to an RGB color.

margin-right.description: Parameter 'Margin right' of type with ID 'expression'. Pixels to add to the right of the image

margin-left.description: Parameter 'Margin left' of type with ID 'expression'. Pixels to add to the left of the image

border-mode.description:
Parameter 'Mode' of type with ID 'ij1-border-mode'. Determines how border values are generated
  • Constant: Set the border to a constant value (zero/black unless configurable)
  • Repeat: Repeat the nearest non-border pixel
  • Mirror: Mirror the pixel values across the border
  • Tile: Tile the image

margin-top.description: Parameter 'Margin top' of type with ID 'expression'. Pixels to add to the top of the image

margin-bottom.description: Parameter 'Margin bottom' of type with ID 'expression'. Pixels to add to the bottom of the image

renaming-entries.description: Parameter 'Renaming entries' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. You can rename one or multiple columns.

source-column.name: Column to be renamed
source-column.description:
The column to be renamed This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

new-name.name: New name

Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

iteration-mode.description:
Parameter 'Iteration mode' of type with ID 'graph-wrapper:iteration-mode'. Determines how the loop is iterated:
  • Pass through: Disables looping. The node behaves as a regular IO interface.
  • The loop can be executed per iteration step. Here you can choose between an iterative iteration step (one item per slot) or a merging iteration step (multiple items per slot).

Automatically assumed to be 'Pass through', if the node is set to 'Pass through'

metadata.description: Parameter 'Metadata' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. Allows to set/override vertex metadata values

color.description: Parameter 'Color' of type with ID 'expression'. The color of the vertex

centroid-c.description: Parameter 'Centroid channel' of type with ID 'expression'. The channel/c location of the centroid

physical-size-y.description: Parameter 'Physical voxel size Y' of type with ID 'expression'. The physical size of a voxel (Y). Must return a string in the format '[Value] [Unit]'

physical-size-x.description: Parameter 'Physical voxel size X' of type with ID 'expression'. The physical size of a voxel (X). Must return a string in the format '[Value] [Unit]'

physical-size-z.description: Parameter 'Physical voxel size Z' of type with ID 'expression'. The physical size of a voxel (Y). Must return a string in the format '[Value] [Unit]'

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius' of type with ID 'expression'. The radius of the vertex

value.description: Parameter 'Value' of type with ID 'expression'. The value of the vertex

centroid-t.description: Parameter 'Centroid frame' of type with ID 'expression'. The frame/t location of the centroid

centroid-x.description: Parameter 'Centroid X' of type with ID 'expression'. The X location of the centroid

centroid-y.description: Parameter 'Centroid Y' of type with ID 'expression'. The Y location of the centroid

centroid-z.description: Parameter 'Centroid Z' of type with ID 'expression'. The Z location of the centroid

vertex-filter/mask.description: Parameter 'Vertex mask' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Apply the operation to a specific set of vertices

file-format.description: Parameter 'File format' of type with ID 'imp-image-output-format'. The file format that should be used.

file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

exclude-edge-roi.description: Parameter 'Exclude edge ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, edge ROI are filtered out

threshold-annotation-strategy.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation mode' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens if annotations are already present.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

threshold-combine-expression.description: Parameter 'Threshold combination function' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression combines multiple thresholds into one numeric threshold.

thresholds.description:
Parameter 'Thresholds' of type with ID 'integer-range'. Determines which thresholds should be tested

The format is the following: [range];[range];... with [range] either being a single integer or a range [from]-[to] (both inclusive). Negative values must be enclosed in parentheses. Example: 0-5;1;(-1)-10. If you want more customization options use the expression mode and functions such as MAKE_SEQUENCE.

threshold-annotation.description: Parameter 'Threshold annotation' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. Puts the generated threshold(s) into an annotation.

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. The measurements to calculate.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

scoring/score.description: Parameter 'Score function' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, assigns a score to each filtered ROI that is accumulated. This score is maximized to find the best threshold. If disabled, the first threshold is applied where the criteria match.

scoring/score-accumulation.description: Parameter 'Score accumulation function' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that determines how the scores are accumulated

filtering/threshold-criteria.description: Parameter 'Threshold criteria' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is applied for each threshold after the ROI were filtered.

filtering/roi-filter.description: Parameter 'ROI filter' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is applied for each ROI and determines whether the ROI fulfills the required criteria.

decimals.description: Parameter 'Number of decimals' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of decimals. Set to zero to round to integer numbers. Negative numbers will round to the closest integer.

If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

annotation-name.name: Annotation name
annotation-name.description: An expression that generates the annotation name

annotation-value.name: Annotation value
annotation-value.description:
An expression that generates the annotation value This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

column-name.name: Column name
column-name.description: The name of the output column.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/transform.description: Parameter 'Transform' of type with ID 'string'. null

fill-outline.description: Parameter 'Fill outline' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the ROI is filled with the label value

label-assignment.description: Parameter 'Label assignment' of type with ID 'parameter-collection-list'. Add items into the list to assign ROI names to labels.

draw-outline.description: Parameter 'Draw outline' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the label value is drawn as outline

min-score.description: Parameter 'Min score' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Minimum score for a circle to be detected

min-radius.description: Parameter 'Min radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Minimum radius of circles in pixels

max-radius.description: Parameter 'Max radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Maximum radius of circles in pixels

radius.description: Parameter 'Radius' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Radius of the filter kernel. See ImageJ>Process>Filters>Show Circular Masks for a reference.

custom-h-min.description: Parameter 'Custom H-Min' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Allows to customize the H-Min parameter of the watershed

apply-per-slice.description: Parameter 'Apply per slice' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, 3D data is split into 2D slices and the watershed algorithm is applied per slice.

connectivity.description: Parameter 'Connectivity' of type with ID 'ij1-neighborhood-2d-3d'. Determines the pixel neighborhood for the flood fill algorithm.

target-area.description: Parameter 'Only apply to ...' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-target'. Determines where the watershed is applied.

custom-h-max.description: Parameter 'Custom H-Max' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Allows to customize the H-Max parameter of the watershed

subfolder.description: Parameter 'Subfolder' of type with ID 'string'. Optional. If non-empty, all files are extracted from the provided sub-folder. The sub-folder navigation is applied before recursive search (if 'Recursive' is enabled).

recursive-follows-links.description: Parameter 'Recursive search follows links' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a recursive search follows symbolic links. (Only Windows) Please note that Windows does not create symbolic links by default.

filters.description: Parameter 'Keep file if ...' of type with ID 'path-query-expression'. Filter expression that allows to filter the files. Click the [X] button to see all available variables. An example for an expression would be '".tif" IN name', which would test if there is '.tif' inside the file name. Annotations are available as variables.

recursive.description: Parameter 'Recursive' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the search is recursive.

columns.description: Parameter 'Columns' of type with ID 'table-column-expression-generator-column-processor-list'. Columns to be generated

generated-rows.description: Parameter 'Generated rows' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Determines how many rows to generate

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

output-empty-lists.description: Parameter 'Output empty lists' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will also output empty lists.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

filter.description: Parameter 'Only keep ROI if' of type with ID 'expression'. Filtering expression. This is applied per ROI. Click the 'f' button to see all available variables you can test for (note: requires from you to enable the corresponding measurement!).An example for an expression would be 'Area > 200 AND Mean > 10'.Annotations are available as variables.

measurements.description: Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij3d-measurement-set'. The measurements to calculate.

filter.description: Parameter 'Remove if' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed per ROI property and should return true if the property should be removed

preprocessing-script.description: Parameter 'Preprocessing' of type with ID 'python-code'. CLIJ2 requires some information about the output image(s) and the memory that is allocated by the kernel operation. This script is executed to generate those values. There are following variables: 'cl_output_buffers', 'cl_dimensions', 'cl_global_sizes'. 'cl_output_buffers' is a dict that should contain the pre-allocated output images for each output slot. 'cl_dimensions' is a list of integers that contains the dimensions of the output image. 'cl_global_sizes' is a list of integers that contains the global size where the tasks are executed over (usually the output size). A variable 'clij2' is available that provides access to the CLIJ2 API. A variable 'data_batch' provides access to the current iteration step. Input slots can be accessed from variables 'input_slots' (array), 'input_slots_map' (map from name to slot). Output slots can be accessed from variables 'output_slots' (array), 'output_slots_map' (map from name to slot). You can change the executed program by setting a variable 'cl_program'. Otherwise the one generated from parameter values is used.

kernel-program-name.description: Parameter 'Kernel program name' of type with ID 'string'. The program that should be executed. Might be overwritten by the preprocessing script.

kernel.description: Parameter 'Kernel' of type with ID 'clij2:opencl-kernel'. The OpenCL kernel script.

exporter-type.description: Parameter 'Exporter type' of type with ID 'imagej-exporter'. Please select the exporter type

export-parameters/name.description: Parameter 'Name' of type with ID 'string'. The name associated to this data

export-parameters/no-windows.description: Parameter 'Avoid creating windows' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, no windows should be created

export-parameters/activate.description: Parameter 'Activate' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the associated window(s) are put into the foreground

export-parameters/duplicate.description: Parameter 'Duplicate data' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, a duplicate is exported if possible

export-parameters/append.description: Parameter 'Append data' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is appended if possible

ij1-roi-sort-and-extract-statistics.name: Sort and extract ROI by statistics

map-line-color.description: Parameter 'Map line color' of type with ID 'optional-color-map'. Allows you to map the ROI line color to the order generated by the sorting. The color is rendered when converting into a RGB visualization.

map-fill-color.description: Parameter 'Map fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color-map'. Allows you to map the ROI fill color to the order generated by the sorting. The color is rendered when converting into a RGB visualization.

selection.description: Parameter 'Selection (top n)' of type with ID 'int-modification'. Allows you to select an exact amount or a fraction of ROI. The top N items will be selected.

sort-orders.description: Parameter 'Sort order' of type with ID 'ij1:measurement-column-sort-order-list'. Allows you to determine by which measurement columns to sort by. You can order by multiple columns where the order within this list. If you for example order by 'Area' and then 'Perimeter', the ROI will be ordered by area and if the area is the same by perimeter.

auto-clamp.description: Parameter 'Ignore missing items' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, there will be not error if you select too many items (e.g. if the list only contains 10 items, but you select the top 20)

file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

manual-order.description: Parameter 'Manual order' of type with ID 'string-list'. Columns are first sorted according to this manual order. All other columns will be sorted after this list.

use-natural-sort-order.description: Parameter 'Sort strings by natural order' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, strings are sorted by natural order (e.g. 1, 2, 15 100, ...). If disabled, strings are sorted lexicographically (e.g. 1, 15, 100, 2)

reverse-sort-order.description: Parameter 'Reverse sort order' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the sort order is reversed.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

annotation-name-filter.description:
Parameter 'Annotation column filter' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Allows to filter for specific annotation columns. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

index-meshes.description: Parameter 'Index/simplify meshes' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, meshes are automatically indexed (simplified), which reduces the size of the output file, but needs additional processing time

file-path.description:
Parameter 'File path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the output file path This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

clij-parameters/filename.description: Parameter 'Filename' of type with ID 'string'. null

columns.description: Parameter 'Columns' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of columns.

rows.description: Parameter 'Rows' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The number of rows.

border-width.description: Parameter 'Border width' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Distance between each tile

chamfer-weights.description: Parameter 'Chamfer weights' of type with ID 'ij-math-chamfer-distance-map-23:weights'. Determines the Chamfer weights for this distance transform

normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize weights' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Indicates whether the resulting distance map should be normalized (divide distances by the first chamfer weight)

output-type.description: Parameter 'Output type' of type with ID 'data-type'. Determines the output type. You can choose between an  16-bit or 32-bit.

extend-target.description: Parameter 'Extend target to fit source' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, values of the target table are added if there are more rows in the source than in the target.

column-filter.description:
Parameter 'Column filter' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Determines which columns are copied from source to target. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

skip-empty-target.description: Parameter 'Skip empty target conditions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, skips the addition of values from the source if the target does not have an equivalent condition.

row-normalization.description: Parameter 'Row normalization' of type with ID 'table-column-row-normalization'. Determines how missing column values are handled if the input tables have different numbers of rows. You can set it to zero/empty (depending on numeric or string type), to the row number (starting with zero), copy the last value, or cycle.

reference-columns.description:
Parameter 'Reference columns' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Columns that should act as reference points for the merging of the rows from different tables. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

avoid-unnecessary-scaling.description: Parameter 'Avoid unnecessary scaling' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the ImageJ resize method is not called if the image already has the correct size.

scale-mode.description: Parameter 'Scale mode' of type with ID 'ij1-transform:scale-mode'. Determines how the image is fit into the output. You can either stretch the image to the new dimensions, fit it inside the boundaries, cut off parts to cover the whole output, or leave the image unchanged (no resize) and just copy it into the output image with a given size

background-color.description: Parameter 'Background' of type with ID 'color'. Used if the scale mode is 'Fit'/'Cover'/'Paste'. Determines the background color of the output

y-axis.description: Parameter 'Y axis' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. How the Y axis should be scaled. If disabled, the aspect ratio is kept.

anchor.description: Parameter 'Placement' of type with ID 'anchor'. Used if the scale mode is 'Fit'/'Cover'/'Paste'. Determines where the image is placed.

x-axis.description: Parameter 'X axis' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. How the X axis should be scaled. If disabled, the aspect ratio is kept.

interpolation-method.description: Parameter 'Interpolation' of type with ID 'ij1-interpolation-method'. The interpolation method

renaming-entries.description: Parameter 'Renaming entries' of type with ID 'string-query-expression:string:pair-list'. You can rename one or multiple columns.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

annotation.description:
Parameter 'Annotation' of type with ID 'annotation-query-expression'. An expression that determines which annotation is used. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

ignore-input-text-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore input text annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, incoming text annotations from the input folder will not be passed to the output.

data-annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Merge imported data annotations' of type with ID 'jipipe:data-annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens when imported data has the same data annotations as the input folder.

ignore-imported-data-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore imported data annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, annotations from imported data annotations are ignored.

ignore-imported-text-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore imported text annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, annotations from imported text annotations are ignored.

text-annotation-merge-mode.description: Parameter 'Merge imported text annotations' of type with ID 'jipipe:annotation-merge-strategy'. Determines what happens when imported data has the same text annotations as the input folder.

ignore-input-data-annotations.description: Parameter 'Ignore input data annotations' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, incoming data annotations from the input folder will not be passed to the output.

physical-dimension-t.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (Time)' of type with ID 'optional-quantity'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image. Please note that only the unit is supported.

physical-dimension-y.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (Y)' of type with ID 'optional-quantity'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image

physical-dimension-z.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (Z)' of type with ID 'optional-quantity'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image

physical-dimension-x.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (X)' of type with ID 'optional-quantity'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image

physical-dimension-value.description: Parameter 'Physical dimension (Value)' of type with ID 'optional-quantity'. If enabled, sets the physical dimension of the image. Please note that only the unit is supported.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available. The calibration of the reference image is preferred.

measurements.description:
Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-set'. The measurements that should be extracted from the labels. Please note that due to technical limitations, some measurements will not work and instead yield measurements over the whole image.

Area

Measures ROI areas. Area of selection in square pixels. Area is in calibrated units if available. They are stored as 'Area'.


Area fraction

The percentage of non-zero pixels. Values are stored in '%Area'


Bounding rectangle

The smallest straight rectangle enclosing the selection. Uses the headings BX, BY, Width and Height, where BX and BY are the coordinates of the upper left corner of the rectangle. Please note that the rectangle is not rotated


Minimum bounding rectangle

(Part of the Bounding rectangle measurements). The smallest rotated rectangle enclosing the selection. RBWidth, RBHeight are the width/major axis and the height/minor axis. RBX1, RBX2, RBX3, RBX4, and RBY1, RBY2, RBY3, RBY4 are the four corner point's X and Y coordinates respectively.


Center of mass

Measures the brightness-weighted average of the x and y coordinates all pixels in the image or selection. They are stored into 'XM' and 'YM' respectively. These coordinates are the first order spatial moments.


Centroid

The center point of the selection. This is the average of the x and y coordinates of all of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'X' and 'Y' respectively.


Feret's diameter

The longest distance between any two points along the selection boundary, also known as maximum caliper. Uses the Feret heading. FeretAngle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the Feret's diameter and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. MinFeret is the minimum caliper diameter. The starting coordinates of the Feret's diameter (FeretX and FeretY) are also generated.


Fit ellipse

Fit an ellipse to the selection. Uses the headings Major, Minor and Angle. Major and Minor are the primary and seconday axis of the best fitting ellipse. Angle (0-180 degrees) is the angle between the primary axis and a line parallel to the x-axis of the image. The coordinates of the center of the ellipse are displayed as X and Y if Centroid is checked. Note that ImageJ cannot calculate the major and minor axis lengths if Pixel Aspect Ratio is not 1.0.


Integrated density

Calculates and displays two values: 'IntDen' (the product of Area and Mean Gray Value) and 'RawIntDen' (the sum of the values of the pixels in the image or selection).


Min & max pixel values

Measures the minimum and maximum greyscale pixel values. They are stored into 'Min' and 'Max' respectively.


Perimeter

The length of the outside boundary of the selection. Values are stored in 'Perim.'


Pixel values kurtosis

The fourth order moment about the greyscale pixel value mean. Values are stored in 'Kurt'


Pixel values mean

Mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Mean'.


Pixel values median

The median value of the pixels in the image or selection. Values are stored in 'Median'


Pixel values modal

Most frequently occurring gray value within the selection. Values are stored in 'Mode'.


Pixel values skewness

The third order moment about the mean of grayscale pixel values. Values are stored in 'Skew'.


Pixel values standard deviation

Measures the standard deviation of greyscale pixel values. They are stored as 'StdDev'.


Shape descriptors

Measures the following shape descriptors:

  • Circ. (circularity): 4π*area/perimeter^2. A value of 1.0 indicates a perfect circle. As the value approaches 0.0, it indicates an increasingly elongated shape. Values may not be valid for very small particles.
  • AR (fitted ellipse aspect ratio): major_axis/minor_axis of the ellipse fitted to the ROI (measurement inherited from ImageJ)
  • rAR (fitted rectangle aspect ratio): fits a rotated rectangle around the ROI and calculates the aspect ratio (major_axis/minor_axis) of this rectangle
  • Round (roundness): 4*area/(π*major_axis^2), or the inverse of the aspect ratio.
  • Solidity: area/convex area.


Stack position

The current position (channel, slice and frame) in the stack or hyperstack. Uses the headings 'Ch', 'Slice' and 'Frame'. Please note that JIPipe algorithms sometimes handle stacks differently compared to their ImageJ equivalents. We recommend to not rely on this measurement.

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

map-fill-color.description: Parameter 'Map fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color-map'. Allows you to map the ROI fill color to the order generated by the sorting. The color is rendered when converting into a RGB visualization.

fill-measurement.description: Parameter 'Fill measurement' of type with ID 'ij3d-measurement-column'. The measurement to extract for the filling color.

file-name-generator.description:
Parameter 'Table name' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. Expression that generates the name of the uploaded table. Please note that the directory will be ignored and 'unnamed' will be assumed if no name is provided. This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. clij-parameters/num_slices.description: Parameter 'Num Slices' of type with ID 'double'. null

annotate-with-roi-index.description: Parameter 'Annotate with ROI index' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate the output image with the mirror ROI index

mode.description: Parameter 'Mode' of type with ID 'ij1-transform-line-mirror:mode'. The way how the mirror operation should be applied given the two pixel values. You can either set both pixels to the min/max value (per channel on RGB) or only copy data from a specific side.

annotate-with-roi-name.description: Parameter 'Annotate with ROI name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate the output image with the mirror ROI name

default-scoring-method.description: Parameter 'Default scoring method' of type with ID 'ij1-dimensions-extended-depth-of-focus:scoring-method'. The scoring method that is applied per 2D plane if no custom score image is provided. Variance applies a local variance filter, while Tenengrad calculates the Sobel gradient.

projected-axis.description: Parameter 'Projected axis' of type with ID 'ij1-hyperstack-dimension'. The axis that should be projected

default-scoring-radius.description: Parameter 'Default scoring radius' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Determines the radius of the local variance filter. Only applicable if the default scoring method is set to 'Variance'.

default-scoring-sigma.description: Parameter 'Default scoring sigma' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Determines the sigma if the Gaussian blur that is applied to the generated scoring images (if applicable). If set to zero or a negative value, no blurring is applied

selection-method.description: Parameter 'Pixel selection method' of type with ID 'ij1-dimensions-extended-depth-of-focus:selection-method'. Determines if the pixel with the minimum or the maximum score is chosen

replacements.description: Parameter 'Replacements' of type with ID 'integer-range:int:pair-list'. A list of replacement values. You can match multiple labels per rule.

preprocessing-script.description: Parameter 'Preprocessing' of type with ID 'python-code'. CLIJ2 requires some information about the output image(s) and the memory that is allocated by the kernel operation. This script is executed to generate those values. There are following variables: 'cl_output_buffers', 'cl_dimensions', 'cl_global_sizes'. 'cl_output_buffers' is a dict that should contain the pre-allocated output images for each output slot. 'cl_dimensions' is a list of integers that contains the dimensions of the output image. 'cl_global_sizes' is a list of integers that contains the global size where the tasks are executed over (usually the output size). A variable 'clij2' is available that provides access to the CLIJ2 API. A variable 'data_batch' provides access to the current iteration step. Input slots can be accessed from variables 'input_slots' (array), 'input_slots_map' (map from name to slot). The first (and only) input slot is also accessible via the 'input_slot' variable. Output slots can be accessed from variables 'output_slots' (array), 'output_slots_map' (map from name to slot). You can change the executed program by setting a variable 'cl_program'. Otherwise the one generated from parameter values is used.

kernel-program-name.description: Parameter 'Kernel program name' of type with ID 'string'. The program that should be executed. Might be overwritten by the preprocessing script.

kernel.description: Parameter 'Kernel' of type with ID 'clij2:opencl-kernel'. The OpenCL kernel script.
