override-axes.description: Parameter 'Override axes' of type with ID 'optional-string'. Allows to override the default axes for reading the values. By default, JIPipe attempts to guess the axes based on the default axis configuration XYCZT. Allowed values are X, Y, Z, C, and T (case-insensitive)

hdf5-path.description: Parameter 'HDF5 internal path' of type with ID 'expression'. Path to the HDF5 data set to import

calibration-parameters/custom-min.description: Parameter 'Custom min' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Used if 'Calibration' method is set to 'Custom'. Sets custom minimum value.

calibration-parameters/calibration-mode.description: Parameter 'Calibration method' of type with ID 'ij1-calibration-mode'. The method to apply for calibration.

calibration-parameters/custom-max.description: Parameter 'Custom max' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Used if 'Calibration' method is set to 'Custom'. Sets custom maximum value.

montage-parameters/tile-height.description: Parameter 'Tile height' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that determines the height of the tiles

montage-parameters/tile-width.description: Parameter 'Tile width' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that determines the width of the tiles

montage-parameters/force-columns.description: Parameter 'Force number of columns' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, forces the number of columns

montage-parameters/rgb-use-render.description: Parameter 'Render to RGB' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled 'Render to RGB' is used instead of a plain conversion to RGB. Only applicable if images are converted to RGB.

montage-parameters/sorting-label-express.description: Parameter 'Custom sorting label' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Optional expression that generates dedicated labels that are only used for sorting. Applied per image.

montage-parameters/label-expression.description: Parameter 'Label' of type with ID 'expression'. Expression that generates the labels. Applied per image.

montage-parameters/force-rgb.description: Parameter 'Force RGB colors' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, force rendering to RGB colors

montage-parameters/force-rows.description: Parameter 'Force number of rows' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, forces the number of rows

montage-parameters/label-parameters/avoi.description: Parameter 'Avoid drawing label on image' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, avoid that that label is drawn on top of the image

montage-parameters/label-parameters/draw.description: Parameter 'Draw label' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, draw a label for each image

montage-parameters/label-parameters/labe.description: Parameter 'Label background' of type with ID 'optional-color'. If enabled, draw a background behind the label
Parameter 'Label font size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. The font size of the drawn label
Parameter 'Label location' of type with ID 'anchor'. The location of the label
Parameter 'Label font family' of type with ID 'font-family-enum'. The font of the label
Parameter 'Label color' of type with ID 'color'. The color of the label

montage-parameters/label-parameters/limi.description: Parameter 'Limit with ellipsis' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, long labels will be limited with an ellipsis. Otherwise, the text is just cut off.

montage-parameters/canvas-parameters/can.description: Parameter 'Canvas image location' of type with ID 'anchor'. Location of the image inside the canvas

montage-parameters/canvas-parameters/bor.description: Parameter 'Border size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Size of the border drawn around the image canvas
Parameter 'Border color' of type with ID 'color'. The color of the border

montage-parameters/canvas-parameters/can.description: Parameter 'Canvas background color' of type with ID 'color'. The background color of the canvas

montage-parameters/image-parameters/scal.description: Parameter 'Scale mode' of type with ID 'ij1-transform:scale-mode'. The way how the image is scaled (if enabled)

montage-parameters/image-parameters/avoi.description: Parameter 'Avoid scaling' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, images that are small enough will not be scaled

montage-parameters/image-parameters/inte.description: Parameter 'Interpolation method' of type with ID 'ij1-interpolation-method'. The interpolation method if images are scaled

roi-properties/position-t.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Frame)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different frame/time-position. The first index is 1. If set to zero, the ROI is located on all frames.

roi-properties/fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the fill color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/line-color.description: Parameter 'Line color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the line color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/position-c.description: Parameter 'Slice position (Channel)' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Allows to relocate the ROI to a different channel-position. chunk-size.description:
Parameter 'Chunk size' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. If enabled, sets the chunk size via an expression that must return an array of five numbers, corresponding to the ImageJ1 axis order is X,Y,C,Z,T. Please note that if you come from the N5 plugin, we recommend to always return all 5 axis sizes (regardless whether the size is one), as this node will apply automated processing of the inputs. The default chunk sizes are calculated for each axis with the following principle:
  • Less than 32: no chunking
  • Between 32 and 128: chunk size 32
  • Between 128 and 512: chunk size 64
  • Other: chunk size 128

compression.description: Parameter 'Compression' of type with ID 'ij1-export-ome-zarr:compression'. The compression that should be applied

file-path.description:
Parameter 'Output path' of type with ID 'data-export-expression'. The output folder path or ZIP file This function should return a valid file path string, which can be either hardcoded or built using string or PATH_COMBINE operations. Hard-cording file paths: Just type the path into quotes as shown below 
"C:/MyData/Outputs/Output_file"
Using path combination: You can combine combine variables provided from annotations and expression variables to, for example, save create a path relative to the project directory or to a project user directory
PATH_COMBINE(project_dir, "Outputs", "Output_file")
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dirs ["outputs"], "Outputs", "Output_file")
Alternative: 
PATH_COMBINE(project_data_dir.outputs, "Outputs", "Output_file")
Using expression functions: You can also use the standard string concatenation function to generate paths
project_dir + "/Outputs/Output_file"

create-pyramid.description: Parameter 'Create multi-resolution pyramid' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Writes multiple resolutions. Increases size and processing time, but makes the ZARR more useful for cloud storage and image viewers.

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

clij-parameters/factor1.description: Parameter 'Factor 1' of type with ID 'double'. null

clij-parameters/factor2.description: Parameter 'Factor 2' of type with ID 'double'. null

keep-annotation-columns.description: Parameter 'Keep annotation columns' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, annotation columns are copied into the result tables.

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will train in 3D. Otherwise, JIPipe will prepare the data by splitting 3D data into planes.

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Cellpose operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

label-data-annotation.description:
Parameter 'Label data annotation' of type with ID 'data-annotation-query-expression'. Determines which data annotation contains the labels. Please ensure that the appropriate label data is annotated to the raw input data. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description:
Parameter 'Mean diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. The cell diameter. Depending on the model, you can choose following values:
  • Cytoplasm: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 30 pixels.
  • Nuclei: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 17 pixels.
  • Custom: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter appropriate for the model.
  • None: This will train from scratch. You can freely set the diameter. You also can set the diameter to 0 to disable scaling.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose is used.

epochs.description: Parameter 'Epochs' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of epochs that should be trained.

gpu-settings/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-settings/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

tweaks-settings/num-test-images-per-epoc.description: Parameter 'Test images per epoch' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Allows to override the number of test images per epoch. Defaults to all images.

tweaks-settings/learning-rate.description: Parameter 'Learning rate' of type with ID 'primitive.double'.

tweaks-settings/num-training-images-per-.description: Parameter 'Training images per epoch' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Allows to override the number of training images per epoch. Defaults to all images.

tweaks-settings/lambda-reconstruction.description: Parameter 'Loss weight: reconstruction' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Weighting of reconstruction loss

tweaks-settings/segmentation-model.description:
Parameter 'Segmentation model' of type with ID 'cellpose-3.x-pretrained-segmentation-model'. Pretrained segmentation model used for calculating the segmentation loss. Cannot be None.

See https://cellpose.readthedocs.io/en/latest/models.html for a description of all models

tweaks-settings/weight-decay.description: Parameter 'Weight decay' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The weight decay

tweaks-settings/lambda-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Loss weight: segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Weighting of segmentation loss

tweaks-settings/lambda-perceptual.description: Parameter 'Loss weight: perceptual' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Weighting of perceptual loss

tweaks-settings/generate-connected-compo.description: Parameter 'Generate connected components' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, JIPipe will apply a connected component labeling to the annotated masks. If disabled, Cellpose is provided with the labels as-is, which might result in issues with the training.

noise-settings/noise-type.description:
Parameter 'Noise type' of type with ID 'cellpose-3.x-denoise-noise-type'. A predefined noise type or 'Custom' to setup custom values.
  • Poisson Noise Only (poisson): poisson=0.8, blur=0.0, downsample=0.0, beta=0.7, gblur=1.0, iso
  • Poisson + Mild Blur (blur_expr): poisson=0.8, blur=0.8, downsample=0.0, beta=0.1, gblur=0.5, iso
  • Poisson + Strong Blur (blur): poisson=0.8, blur=0.8, downsample=0.0, beta=0.1, gblur=10.0, iso, uni
  • Poisson + Mild Downsample (downsample_expr): poisson=0.8, blur=0.8, downsample=0.8, beta=0.03, gblur=1.0, iso
  • Poisson + Strong Downsample (downsample): poisson=0.8, blur=0.8, downsample=0.8, beta=0.03, gblur=5.0, iso, uni
  • Poisson + Blur + Downsample (all): poisson=[0.8, 0.8, 0.8], blur=[0.0, 0.8, 0.8], downsample=[0.0, 0.0, 0.8], beta=[0.7, 0.1, 0.03], gblur=[0.0, 10.0, 5.0], iso, uni
  • Poisson + Anisotropic Blur (aniso): poisson=0.8, blur=0.8, downsample=0.8, beta=0.1, gblur=ds_max * 1.5

noise-settings/custom-gblur.description: Parameter 'Blur Gaussian StdDev' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Scale of gaussian blurring stddev

noise-settings/max-downsampling.description: Parameter 'Max downsampling factor' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. The maximum downsampling factor

noise-settings/custom-downsample.description: Parameter 'Downsample fraction of images' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Fraction of images to downsample

noise-settings/custom-blur.description: Parameter 'Blur fraction of images' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Fraction of images to blur

noise-settings/custom-beta.description: Parameter 'Poisson Beta' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Scale of poisson noise

noise-settings/custom-poisson.description: Parameter 'Poisson fraction of images' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Fraction of images to add poisson noise to

filaments-drawer/draw-edges.description: Parameter 'Draw edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, draw edges

filaments-drawer/override-vertex-radius.description: Parameter 'Override vertex radius' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Allows to override the radius of each vertex

filaments-drawer/override-edge-color.description: Parameter 'Override edge color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to override the color of edges

filaments-drawer/ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ignore the frame (T) location of each vertex

filaments-drawer/draw-vertices.description: Parameter 'Draw vertices' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, draw vertices

filaments-drawer/ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ignore the channel (C) location of each vertex

filaments-drawer/ignore-z.description: Parameter 'Ignore Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the Z location of each each vertex will be ignored

filaments-drawer/blend-mode.description:
Parameter 'Blend mode' of type with ID 'ij1-color-blend-images:blend-mode'. Determines how the rendered filaments are blended with the reference image

See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blend_modes

filaments-drawer/override-edge-thickness.description: Parameter 'Override edge thickness' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. If enabled, override the edge thickness with the specified value. Otherwise, the edge thickness interpolates between the two vertex radii.

map-line-color.description: Parameter 'Map line color' of type with ID 'optional-color-map'. Allows you to map the ROI line color to the order generated by the sorting. The color is rendered when converting into a RGB visualization.

map-fill-color.description: Parameter 'Map fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color-map'. Allows you to map the ROI fill color to the order generated by the sorting. The color is rendered when converting into a RGB visualization.

line-measurement.description: Parameter 'Line measurement' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-column'. The measurement to extract for the line color.

fill-measurement.description: Parameter 'Fill measurement' of type with ID 'ij1-measurement-column'. The measurement to extract for the filling color.

models.description: Parameter 'Models' of type with ID 'cellpose-2.x-pretrained-model-list'. The list of models to be imported

model-name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with model name' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, annotate with the name/id of the model

relative-to-project-dir.description: Parameter 'Output relative to project directory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, outputs will be preferably generated relative to the project directory. Otherwise, JIPipe will store the results in an automatically generated directory. Has no effect if an absolute path is provided.

output-directory.description: Parameter 'Output directory' of type with ID 'path'. Can be a relative or absolute directory. All collected files will be put into this directory. If relative, it is relative to the output slot's output directory that is generated based on the current run's output path.

num-trials.description: Parameter 'Number of trials' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. If larger than one, use the Multi-K-Means algorithm that repeats clustering multiple times and returns the one with the lowest sum of variances over all clusters.

input-columns.description: Parameter 'Input columns' of type with ID 'table-column-source-list'. The list of columns that will be used to create the points. Please note that string columns and NA/infinite values are automatically replaced with zeroes.

Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Cellpose will segment in 3D. Otherwise, any 3D image will be processed per-slice. Please note that 3D segmentation requires large amounts of memory.

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Cellpose operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

diameter-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with diameter' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, the diameter is attached as annotation. Useful if you want to let Cellpose estimate the object diameters.

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description: Parameter 'Average object diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. If enabled, Cellpose will use the provided average diameter to find objects. Otherwise, Cellpose will estimate the diameter by itself.

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose is used.

model.description:
Parameter 'Model' of type with ID 'cellpose-model'. The model type that should be used.

See https://cellpose.readthedocs.io/en/latest/models.html for a description of all models

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-parameters/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-parameters/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

output-parameters/output-probabilities.description: Parameter 'Output probabilities' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel

output-parameters/output-roi.description: Parameter 'Output 2D ROI' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output a ROI list containing all detected objects

output-parameters/output-labels.description: Parameter 'Output labels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Output an image that contains a unique greyscale value for each detected object.

output-parameters/output-flows-xy.description: Parameter 'Output flows XY' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel

output-parameters/output-flows-z.description: Parameter 'Output flows Z' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)

output-parameters/output-flows-d.description: Parameter 'Output flows d' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)

enhancement-parameters/normalize.description: Parameter 'Normalize' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Normalize data so 0.0=1st percentile and 1.0=99th percentile of image intensities in each channel

enhancement-parameters/anisotropy.description: Parameter 'Anisotropy (3D)' of type with ID 'optional-double'. For 3D segmentation, optional rescaling factor (e.g. set to 2.0 if Z is sampled half as dense as X or Y)

enhancement-parameters/interpolate.description: Parameter 'Interpolate (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Interpolate during 2D dynamics (not available in 3D)

enhancement-parameters/no-resample.description: Parameter 'Disable resample' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Disable dynamics on full image (makes algorithm faster for images with large diameters)

enhancement-parameters/net-average.description: Parameter 'Average all networks' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Runs the 4 built-in networks and averages them if True, runs one network if disabled

threshold-parameters/stitch-threshold.description: Parameter 'Stitch threshold' of type with ID 'optional-double'. Compute masks in 2D then stitch together masks with IoU>0.9 across planes

threshold-parameters/cell-probability-th.description: Parameter 'Cell probability threshold' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Cellprob threshold, default is 0, decrease to find more and larger masks

threshold-parameters/flow-threshold.description: Parameter 'Flow threshold (2D)' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Flow error threshold, 0 turns off this optional QC step. Default: 0.4

threshold-parameters/exclude-on-edges.description: Parameter 'Exclude masks on edges' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Discard masks which touch edges of image

ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different channels are compared

measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available

component-name-annotation.description: Parameter 'Annotate with component' of type with ID 'optional-annotation-name'. If enabled, an annotation with the numeric component index is generated.

overlap-filter-measurements.description: Parameter 'Measurements' of type with ID 'ij3d-relation-measurement-set'. The measurements that will be generated

overlap-filter.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter' of type with ID 'expression'. Determines if two objects overlap. If left empty, the objects are tested for colocalization of at least one voxel.

require-colocalization.description: Parameter 'Only measure if objects co-localize' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, only co-localizing objects are measured

ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different frames are compared

precise-colocalization.description: Parameter 'Precise colocalization' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the object co-localization for the 'Only measure if objects co-localize' setting tests for voxel colocalization (slower). Otherwise, only the bounding boxes are compared (faster).

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

overlap-filter-measurements.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter measurements' of type with ID 'ij3d-relation-measurement-set'. Measurements extracted for the overlap filter.

roi1/consume-overlap.description: Parameter 'Consume on overlap' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are consumed if an overlap is detected, meaning that no other tested ROI can overlap with it. This is useful if you want to prevent duplicate overlaps (e.g., if you compare automated vs manually segmented blobs).

roi1/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are stored into the output if they do not overlap.

roi1/overlap-filter.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter' of type with ID 'expression'. This filter is applied to any combination of ROIs that have an overlap. You will have three sets of measurements: ROI1, Overlap, and ROI2.'ROI1'and 'ROI2' correspond to a ROI from the input slots,respectively. 'Overlap' is the overlap between these ROI. Please open the expression builder to see a list of all available variables. If the filter is empty, no filtering is applied.

roi1/require-colocalization.description: Parameter 'Only measure if objects co-localize' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, only co-localizing objects are measured

roi1/output-overlaps.description: Parameter 'Output overlapping regions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the overlapping regions, instead of the ROI are extracted.

roi1/ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different frames are compared

roi1/precise-colocalization.description: Parameter 'Precise colocalization' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the object co-localization for the 'Only measure if objects co-localize' setting tests for voxel colocalization (slower). Otherwise, only the bounding boxes are compared (faster).

roi1/ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different channels are compared

roi1/enabled.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. You can use this setting to disable generating this output.

roi1/measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available. Measurements will be in the physical sizes of the reference image.

roi2/consume-overlap.description: Parameter 'Consume on overlap' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are consumed if an overlap is detected, meaning that no other tested ROI can overlap with it. This is useful if you want to prevent duplicate overlaps (e.g., if you compare automated vs manually segmented blobs).

roi2/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI are stored into the output if they do not overlap.

roi2/overlap-filter.description: Parameter 'Overlap filter' of type with ID 'expression'. This filter is applied to any combination of ROIs that have an overlap. You will have three sets of measurements: ROI1, Overlap, and ROI2.'ROI1'and 'ROI2' correspond to a ROI from the input slots,respectively. 'Overlap' is the overlap between these ROI. Please open the expression builder to see a list of all available variables. If the filter is empty, no filtering is applied.

roi2/require-colocalization.description: Parameter 'Only measure if objects co-localize' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, only co-localizing objects are measured

roi2/output-overlaps.description: Parameter 'Output overlapping regions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the overlapping regions, instead of the ROI are extracted.

roi2/ignore-t.description: Parameter 'Ignore frame' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different frames are compared

roi2/precise-colocalization.description: Parameter 'Precise colocalization' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the object co-localization for the 'Only measure if objects co-localize' setting tests for voxel colocalization (slower). Otherwise, only the bounding boxes are compared (faster).

roi2/ignore-c.description: Parameter 'Ignore channel' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, ROI located at different channels are compared

roi2/enabled.description: Parameter 'Enabled' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. You can use this setting to disable generating this output.

roi2/measure-in-physical-units.description: Parameter 'Measure in physical units' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If true, measurements will be generated in physical units if available. Measurements will be in the physical sizes of the reference image.

enable-3d-segmentation.description: Parameter 'Enable 3D segmentation' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, Omnipose will train in 3D. Otherwise, JIPipe will prepare the data by splitting 3D data into planes.

suppress-logs.description: Parameter 'Suppress logs' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will not log the status of the Python operation. Can be used to limit memory consumption of JIPipe if larger data sets are used.

label-data-annotation.description:
Parameter 'Label data annotation' of type with ID 'data-annotation-query-expression'. Determines which data annotation contains the labels. Please ensure that the appropriate label data is annotated to the raw input data. This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing annotation by its name, and (2) Matching an existing annotation by boolean operators
  1. Type in the name of the existing annotation. Put the name in double quotes (optional, but recommended). Example: 
    "#Dataset"
  2. The function iterates through all annotations. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have 'key' and 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    key == "#Dataset"

train-size-model.description: Parameter 'Train size model' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, also train a size model

cleanup-afterwards.description: Parameter 'Clean up data after processing' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, data is deleted from temporary directories after the processing was finished. Disable this to make it possible to debug your scripts. The directories are accessible via the logs (Tools > Logs).

diameter.description:
Parameter 'Mean diameter' of type with ID 'optional-double'. The cell diameter. Depending on the model, you can choose following values:
  • Cytoplasm: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 30 pixels.
  • Nuclei: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 17 pixels.
  • Custom: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter appropriate for the model.
  • None: This will train from scratch. You can freely set the diameter. You also can set the diameter to 0 to disable scaling.

pretrained-model.description:
Parameter 'Model' of type with ID 'omnipose-pretrained-model'. The pretrained model that should be used. You can either choose one of the models provided by Omnipose, a custom model, or train from scratch. The pre-trained model has influence on the diameter and how the input images should be prepared:
  • Cytoplasm: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 30 pixels.
  • Nuclei: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter of about 17 pixels.
  • Custom: You need to rescale all your images that structures have a diameter appropriate for the model. Don't forget to set the diameter value.
  • None: This will train from scratch. You can freely set the diameter. You also can set the diameter to 0 to disable scaling.
See https://github.com/kevinjohncutler/omnipose for a description of all models

override-environment.description: Parameter 'Override Python environment' of type with ID 'optional-python-environment'. If enabled, a different Python environment is used for this Node. Otherwise the one in the Project > Application settings > Extensions > Omnipose is used.

epochs.description: Parameter 'Epochs' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Number of epochs that should be trained.

channel-parameters/invert.description: Parameter 'Invert' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Invert grayscale channel

channel-parameters/nuclear-channel.description: Parameter 'Nuclear channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Nuclear channel (only used by certain models); 0: NONE, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/segmented-channel.description: Parameter 'Segmented channel' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Channel to segment; 0: GRAY, 1: RED, 2: GREEN, 3: BLUE. Default: 0

channel-parameters/all-channels.description: Parameter 'Use all channels' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Use all channels in image if using own model and images with special channels

gpu-settings/enable-gpu.description: Parameter 'With GPU' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Utilize a GPU if available. Please note that you need to setup Cellpose to allow usage of your GPU. Also ensure that enough memory is available.

gpu-settings/gpu-device.description: Parameter 'GPU device' of type with ID 'optional-integer'. Which GPU device to use

tweaks-settings/batch-size.description: Parameter 'Batch size' of type with ID 'primitive.int'.

tweaks-settings/learning-rate.description: Parameter 'Learning rate' of type with ID 'primitive.double'.

tweaks-settings/concatenate-downsampled-.description: Parameter 'Concatenate downsampled layers' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Concatenate downsampled layers with upsampled layers (off by default which means they are added)

tweaks-settings/use-style-vector.description: Parameter 'Use style vector' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

tweaks-settings/min-train-masks.description: Parameter 'Minimum number of labels per image' of type with ID 'primitive.int'. Minimum number of masks an image must have to use in training set. This value is by default 5 in the original Cellpose tool.

tweaks-settings/use-residual-connections.description: Parameter 'Use residual connections' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'.

tweaks-settings/generate-connected-compo.description: Parameter 'Generate connected components' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, JIPipe will apply a connected component labeling to the annotated masks. If disabled, Cellpose is provided with the labels as-is, which might result in issues with the training.

clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clijx.clij.name: Cli Jx Standard Deviation Of All Pixels

column-x1.description:
Parameter 'Column 'X1'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the X1 coordinate. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-x2.description:
Parameter 'Column 'X2'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the X2 coordinate. The usage of this column depends on the current 'Anchor' setting. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

mode.description:
Parameter 'Created ROI type' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-from-table-rectangular:mode'. Determines which ROI to create. 
'Rectangle' creates rectangles. 'Oval' creates ovals that fit into the space defined by the rectangle. 'MinCircle' and 'MaxCircle' creates circles that either fit into the space or encompass it. Circles are centered within the rectangle area.

column-z.description:
Parameter 'Column 'Z'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Table column that determines the Z location. For one-based positions, 0 indicates that the ROI is present in all Z-slices. For zero-based positions the value is -1 or lower. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

one-based-positions.description: Parameter 'Use one-based positions' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the first slice is 1. Otherwise, the first slice is zero.

column-metadata.description:
Parameter 'ROI metadata' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that contains ROI metadata in as JSON object (string keys, string values). If not valid JSON, the metadata will be stored into a field 'metadata_'. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-name.description:
Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the ROI name (overwritten if the ROI name in 'ROI properties' is enabled) This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-y2.description:
Parameter 'Column 'Y2'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the Y2 coordinate. The usage of this column depends on the current 'Anchor' setting. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.

column-t.description:
Parameter 'Column 'T'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Table column that determines the frame location. For one-based positions, 0 indicates that the ROI is present in all frame-slices. For zero-based positions the value is -1 or lower. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.

column-height.description:
Parameter 'Column 'Height'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the height. The usage of this column depends on the current 'Anchor' setting. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.

  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

anchor.description:
Parameter 'Anchor' of type with ID 'ij1-roi-from-table-rectangular:anchor'. Determines how the ROI are generated.
'Top left' creates the ROI at the top left X1 and Y1 coordinates with provided width and height. 'Center' creates centers the ROI at the X1 and Y1 coordinates and also requires that width and height are set. 'Two points' defines the ROI, so it fits into the rectangle defined by the points (X1, Y1) and (X2, Y2).

column-width.description:
Parameter 'Column 'Width'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the width. The usage of this column depends on the current 'Anchor' setting. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-y1.description:
Parameter 'Column 'Y1'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. The table column that is used for the Y1 coordinate. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

column-c.description:
Parameter 'Column 'C'' of type with ID 'table-column-source'. Table column that determines the channel location. For one-based positions, 0 indicates that the ROI is present in all channel-slices. For zero-based positions the value is -1 or lower. This parameter can be used to either select an existing column from a table or to generate a new column by providing a value for each row.
  • Selecting columns by exact name: Type in the name of the column into the value field. Quotation marks are optional.
  • Selecting columns by filtering: The value expression is called for each existing column and provided as variable 'value'. Return TRUE at any point to select the value. Example: value == "Mean"
  • Generating columns: The value expression is called for each row. Return a string or number. You have access to the other column values inside the row (as variables). Example: Mean + 0.5 * X. If you do not provide a valid expression, the expression itself is put in as column value (string)

roi-properties/line-width.description: Parameter 'Line width' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. Allows to change the line width when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/roi-name.description: Parameter 'ROI name' of type with ID 'optional-expression'. Allows to change the ROI name

roi-properties/fill-color.description: Parameter 'Fill color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the fill color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

roi-properties/line-color.description: Parameter 'Line color' of type with ID 'optional-color'. Allows to change the line color when rendered as RGB and within ImageJ

series-name.description:
Parameter 'Series name' of type with ID 'string-query-expression'. Expression that is used to generate the series name This parameter is an expression that has two modes: (1) Selecting an existing string, and (2) Matching an existing strings by boolean operators
  1. Type in the string in double quotes. Example: 
    "hello world"
  2. The function iterates through all strings. It should return TRUE for one of them. You will have a variable 'value' available within the expression. Example: 
    value CONTAINS "hello"

equalize-replicate-last.description: Parameter 'Equalization mode' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. Determines how multiple input images are equalized. Only applicable if the split mode is not set to 'None' and 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is enabled. The option 'Empty' will add black slices/cubes if needed, while 'Replicate' will replicate the last cube/slice.

split-mode.description: Parameter 'Split image' of type with ID 'clij-split-mode'. Allows to split the image into 2D slices or 3D cubes before passing them to CLIJ. The results are automatically merged into an image with the same number of channels/frames/slices as the input image(s). Please note that if multiple input images are provided, the node will equalize the number of channels/frames/slices prior to the algorithm execution. If you split into 2D slices, channels/frames/slices will be equalized, while for 3D cubes channels and frames will be equalized. The equalization process will add new black cubes/slices if necessary (based on the maximum over all inputs), unless you set the equalization mode to 'Replicate'. Not applicable if 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' is disabled.

avoid-gpu-memory.description: Parameter 'Avoid allocating GPU memory' of type with ID 'primitive.boolean'. If enabled, the node will be reconfigured to only allocate data into the GPU memory if absolutely necessary. Please note that the application of multiple GPU-based operations will be slower due to the repeated allocation and de-allocation of the images.

expression.description: Parameter 'Expression' of type with ID 'expression'. This expression is executed for each pixel. It provides the pixel components in the original color space, as well as in other color spaces. The expression must return an array with three components, each component ranging from 0-255 (otherwise the values are automatically clamped). Alternatively, the expression can return a number that encodes the color components as integer.

transformation-function.description:
Parameter 'Function' of type with ID 'ij1-math-math2d:transformation'. The function that is applied to each pixel.
  • Set: Set all pixel values to the provided one
  • Add: Add the provided value to all pixel values
  • Subtract: Subtract the provided value from all pixel values
  • Multiply: Multiply all pixel values with the provided value
  • Divide: Divide all pixel values by the provided value
  • Gamma: Perform gamma correction (value is gamma)
  • Minimum: Pixel values less than the provided value are set to the value
  • Maximum: Pixel values larger than the provided value are set to the value
  • Logical OR: Apply binary OR operation on pixels with value (value is converted to integer)
  • Logical AND: Apply binary AND operation on pixels with value (value is converted to integer)
  • Logical XOR: Apply binary XOR operation on pixels with value (value is converted to integer)

value.description: Parameter 'Value' of type with ID 'primitive.double'. The second operand
